#### Getting generic table data

A Generic Table is a special diagram. You can retrieve it by using the variable $Diagram. Thus, every method must accept the diagram instance or diagram name.

Use the following method to get the Generic Table instance. We use Generic Table instances to retrieve table information such as row elements and column names.

##### Getting generic table instances from diagram elements

To get a Generic Table instance from a specified diagram element, use the following code.

**getTable( diagram : Diagram) : Table**

```text

```

Where the parameter is:

- diagram – the diagram element.

Return the instance of a Generic Table.

Example code:

```text

```

##### Getting generic table instances from diagram names

To get a Generic Table instance from a specific diagram name, use the following code.

**getTable( diagramName : String) : Table**

```text

```

Where the parameter is:

- diagram – the diagram name.

Return the instance of a Generic Table.

Example code:

```text

```

#### Closing tables

Use the following method to close a table diagram.

##### Closing all diagram tables

**CloseTables() : Void**

```text

```

##### Closing a specific table diagram

**closeTable(diagram: Diagram) : Void**

```text

```

Where the parameter is:

- diagram – a table diagram you want to close.

##### Closing a table diagram with a specific diagram name

**closeTable(diagramName: String) : Void**

```text

```

Where the parameter is:

- diagramName – the name of a table diagram you want to close.

#### Getting row elements

The Generic Table consists of a series of row elements and column names. Use the following methods to retrieve a list of row elements.

##### Getting all row elements

**getRows() : List<Element>**

```text

```

The returned value is a list of Elements. The following shows an example of how to print all row element names.

```text

```

##### Getting all row elements including collapsed

**getRows() : List<Element>**

```text

```

The returned value is a list of Elements. The following shows an example of how to print all row element names including collapsed.

```text

```

##### Getting all filtered row elements

**getFilteredRows() : List<Element>**

| $table.getFilteredRows() |
| --- |

The returned value is a list of Elements that have been filtered by the Filter field.

| #foreach($row in $table.getFilteredRows()) $row.name #end |
| --- |

The figure below shows the rows returned after applying the filter.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Rows returned after filtering.

##### Getting row elements in specific row numbers

**getRow( rowNumber : int ) : Element**

```text

```

Where the parameter is:

- rowNumber – the row number starts with 0.

The returned value is an Element.

#### Getting column names

Column names are referred by column IDs. A column is a QProperty ID that is not readable or accessible by the user. We need to create other methods that provide better usability than using QProperty ID. Use the following methods to retrieve column names.

The methods in this section retrieves column names from the list returned by Show Columns menu item from generic table diagram. The methods in the preceding section, however, retrieves column names from the diagram itself and not from the list returned by Show Columns menu item.

##### Getting column names from column ID

**getColumn( columnId : String) : String**

```text

```

Where the parameter is:

- columnId – the ID of a column. Using column IDs has a benefit of consistency between different languages, for example, French and English. The column ID can be retrieved by the method getColumnIds().

The returned value is the name of a column.

##### Getting column names from column numbers

**getColumn(columnNumber : int) : String**

```text

```

Where the parameter is:

- columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.

The returned value is the name of a column.

##### Getting all column names

**getColumnNames() : List<String>**

```text

```

The returned value is a list of column names.

Example code:

Prints all column names.

```text

```

##### Getting all column IDs

**getColumnIds() : List<String>**

```text

```

The returned value is a list of column IDs.

Example code:

Print all columns IDs

```text

```

#### Getting cell values

A cell value is the value in a row element with a column name. To get a cell value, you need to provide both the row element and column. A cell value is an Element or row number. Use the following methods to retrieve a cell value.

##### Getting values from row element and column ID

**getValue( rowElement : Element, columnIdOrName : String ) : Object**

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- rowElement – a row element.
- columnId – a column ID.

The returned value is the value in a cell. If the cell contains an element, the value is the Element. 
Example code:

Prints the row, column, and value.

```text
$row.name 
#foreach($col in $table.getColumnIds()) 
$table.getColumn$col) : $table.getValue($row, $col) 
#end
#end
#end]]>
```

##### Getting values from row element and column name

**getValue( rowElement : Element, columnIdOrName : String ) : Object**

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- rowElement – a row element.
- columnName – a column name.

The returned value is the value in a cell. If the cell contains an element, the value is the Element.

##### Getting values from row element and column number

**getValue( rowElement : Element, columnNumber : int ) : Object**

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- rowElement – a row element.
- columnName – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.

The returned value is the value in a cell. If the cell contains an element, the value is the Element.

##### Getting values from row and column numbers

**getValue( rowNumber : int, columnNumber : int ) : Object**

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- rowNumber – a row number starts with 0.
- columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.

The returned value is the value in a cell. If the cell contains an element, the value is the Element.

##### Getting values from row element and column IDs as string

**getValueAsString( rowElement : Element, columnIdOrName : String ) : String**

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- rowElement – a row element.
- columnId – a column ID.

The returned value is the value in a cell and is converted into String. The String value is created by the modeling tool text representation API.

##### Getting values from row element and column name as string

**getValueAsString( rowElement : Element, columnIdOrName : String ) : String**

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- rowElement – a row element
- columnName – a column name

The returned value is the value in a cell and is converted into String. The String value is created by the modeling tool text representation API.

##### Getting values from row element and column number as string

**getValueAsString( rowElement : Element, columnNumber : int ) : String**

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- rowElement – a row element.
- columnName – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.

The returned value is the value in a cell and is converted into String. The String value is created by the modeling tool text representation API.

##### Getting values from row and column numbers as string

**getValueAsString( rowNumber : int, columnNumber : int ) : String**

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- rowNumber – a row number starts with 0.
- columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.

The returned value is the value in a cell and is converted into String. The String value is created by the modeling tool text representation API.

##### Getting values from row and column numbers as string with a full type of element in a property

**getValueAsString( rowNumber : int, columnNumber : int, showFullType : boolean ) : String**

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- rowNumber – a row number starts with 0.
- columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.
- showFullType – the default value is true for showing a full type of element in a cell in String form.

The returned value is the value in a cell, converted into String, and shown in a full type or normal form.

##### Getting cell value from returned object as string

**getCellValueAsString( object : Object ) : String**

```text

```

Where the parameter is:

- object – an object returned from the getValue function.

The returned value is the object in cell value converted into String.

For example:

```text

```

#### Getting visible column and cell values

A visible cell value is the column which is visible on a diagram. Unlike the methods described in section 23.1.5 Getting Cell Values, the following methods will retrieve only the value which is visible on a diagram.

##### Getting all visible column IDs

**getVisibleColumnIds() : List<String>**

```text

```

The returned value is a list of visible column IDs.

##### Getting visible column names from column numbers

**getVisibleColumn( columnNumber : int) : String**

```text

```

Where the parameter is:

- columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.

The returned value is the name of a visible column.

##### Getting visible values from row elements and column number

**getVisibleValue( rowElement : Element, columnNumber : int ) : Object**

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- rowElement – a row element.
- columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.

The returned value is the value in a visible cell. If the cell contains an element, the value is the Element.

##### Getting visible values from row number and column number

**getVisibleValue( rowNumber : int, columnNumber : int ) : Object**

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- rowNumber – a row number starts with 0.
- columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.

The returned value is the value in a visible cell. If the cell contains an element, the value is the Element.

##### Getting visible values from row element and column number as string

**getVisibleValueAsString( rowElement : Element, columnNumber : int ) : String**

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- rowElement – a row element.
- columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.

The returned value is the value in a visible cell and is converted into String. The String value is created by the modeling tool text representation API.

##### Getting visible values from row and column numbers as string

**getVisibleValueAsString( rowNumber : int, columnNumber : int ) : String**

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- rowNumber – a row number starts with 0.
- columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.

The returned value is the value in a visible cell and is converted into String. The String value is created by the modeling tool text representation API.

To get a cell value, you need to provide both the row element and column. A cell value is an Element or row number. Use the following methods to retrieve a cell value.</p><h3>Getting values from row element and column ID</h3><p><strong>getValue( rowElement : Element, columnIdOrName : String ) : Object</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d596b18b-096f-4636-a62e-c7caa173605f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getValue($rowElement, $columnId)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameters are: </p><ul><li>rowElement – a row element.</li><li>columnId – a column ID.</li></ul><p>The returned value is the value in a cell. If the cell contains an element, the value is the Element. <br />Example code:</p><p>Prints the row, column, and value.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="398c12e9-23d8-471a-9d49-28d353364645"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($diagram in $project.getDiagrams("Generic Table")) 
````html
#set($table = $generic.getTable($diagram)) 
#foreach($row in $table.getRows()) 
<h1>$row.name</h1> 
#foreach($col in $table.getColumnIds()) 
$table.getColumn$col) : $table.getValue($row, $col) 
#end
#end
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Getting values from row element and column name</h3><p><strong>getValue( rowElement : Element, columnIdOrName : String ) : Object</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bd5c5321-cbfc-4a9e-839c-ccbd7815640c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getValue($rowElement, $columnName)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameters are: </p><ul><li>rowElement – a row element.</li><li>columnName – a column name.</li></ul><p>The returned value is the value in a cell. If the cell contains an element, the value is the Element.</p><h3>Getting values from row element and column number</h3><p><strong>getValue( rowElement : Element, columnNumber : int ) : Object</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4e3b9762-9879-4e9e-a0bb-d3851a415da8"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getValue($rowElement, $columnNumber)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameters are: </p><ul><li>rowElement – a row element.</li><li>columnName – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number. </li></ul><p>The returned value is the value in a cell. If the cell contains an element, the value is the Element.</p><h3>Getting values from row and column numbers</h3><p><strong>getValue( rowNumber : int, columnNumber : int ) : Object</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cc4bfae1-c329-4309-966c-2648beec4e70"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getValue($rowNumber, $columnNumber)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameters are: </p><ul><li>rowNumber – a row number starts with 0.</li><li>columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number. </li></ul><p>The returned value is the value in a cell. If the cell contains an element, the value is the Element.</p><h3>Getting values from row element and column IDs as string</h3><p><strong>getValueAsString( rowElement : Element, columnIdOrName : String ) : String</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7ef43774-c8d0-44eb-9ce7-753a8b8bdea7"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getValueAsString($rowElement, $columnId)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameters are: </p><ul><li>rowElement – a row element.</li><li>columnId – a column ID.</li></ul><p>The returned value is the value in a cell and is converted into String. The String value is created by the modeling tool text representation API.</p><h3>Getting values from row element and column name as string</h3><p><strong>getValueAsString( rowElement : Element, columnIdOrName : String ) : String</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="761f3104-07b6-4da8-b947-a3db31c737c0"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getValueAsString($rowElement, $columnName)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameters are: </p><ul><li>rowElement – a row element</li><li>columnName – a column name </li></ul><p>The returned value is the value in a cell and is converted into String. The String value is created by the modeling tool text representation API.</p><h3>Getting values from row element and column number as string</h3><p><strong>getValueAsString( rowElement : Element, columnNumber : int ) : String</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8dbe3278-4be0-403c-8ab6-87df908e1b90"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getValueAsString($rowElement, $columnNumber)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameters are: </p><ul><li>rowElement – a row element.</li><li>columnName – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number. </li></ul><p>The returned value is the value in a cell and is converted into String. The String value is created by the modeling tool text representation API.</p><h3>Getting values from row and column numbers as string</h3><p><strong>getValueAsString( rowNumber : int, columnNumber : int ) : String</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a1d1e090-4dba-4e48-a1c6-0ae908c5b35f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getValueAsString($rowNumber, $columnNumber)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameters are: </p><ul><li>rowNumber – a row number starts with 0.</li><li>columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.</li></ul><p>The returned value is the value in a cell and is converted into String. The String value is created by the modeling tool text representation API.</p><h3>Getting values from row and column numbers as string with a full type of element in a property</h3><p><strong>getValueAsString( rowNumber : int, columnNumber : int, showFullType : boolean ) : String</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="96d189e3-9821-4731-81e4-caa568d08794"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getValueAsString($rowNumber, $columnNumber, showFullType)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameters are: </p><ul><li>rowNumber – a row number starts with 0.</li><li>columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.</li><li>showFullType – the default value is <strong>true</strong> for showing a full type of element in a cell in String form.</li></ul><p>The returned value is the value in a cell, converted into String, and shown in a full type or normal form.</p><h3>Getting cell value from returned object as string</h3><p><strong>getCellValueAsString( object : Object ) : String<br /></strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2361d9cf-dd0c-4bc2-a6b5-856a38528202"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getCellValueAsString($object)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameter is:</p><ul><li><p>object – an object returned from the getValue function.</p></li></ul><p>The returned value is the object in cell value converted into String.</p><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="59d0c265-e696-4d03-82ce-9cc6aca99734"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set($result = $table.getValue($row, $id))
#if($list.isArray($result))
	#foreach($r in $result)
	- $table.getCellValueAsString($r)
	#end
#else
	$table.getCellValueAsString($result)
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Getting visible column and cell values</h2><p>A visible cell value is the column which is visible on a diagram. Unlike the methods described in section 23.1.5 Getting Cell Values, the following methods will retrieve only the value which is visible on a diagram.</p><h3>Getting all visible column IDs</h3><p><strong>getVisibleColumnIds() : List&lt;String&gt;</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f10f816f-aba0-4ab2-b6b7-1544621f9deb"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getVisibleColumnIds()]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The returned value is a list of visible column IDs.</p><h3>Getting visible column names from column numbers</h3><p><strong>getVisibleColumn( columnNumber : int) : String</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="064732bf-88e3-4d8b-851d-c512ef9fc6ee"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getVisibleColumn($columnNumber)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameter is: </p><ul><li>columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.</li></ul><p>The returned value is the name of a visible column.</p><h3>Getting visible values from row elements and column number</h3><p><strong>getVisibleValue( rowElement : Element, columnNumber : int ) : Object</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0e0c1a9c-f9f8-4575-8ba1-72772f971e82"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getVisibleValue($rowElement, $columnNumber)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameters are: </p><ul><li>rowElement – a row element.</li><li>columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number. </li></ul><p>The returned value is the value in a visible cell. If the cell contains an element, the value is the Element.</p><h3>Getting visible values from row number and column number</h3><p><strong>getVisibleValue( rowNumber : int, columnNumber : int ) : Object</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8a44a419-d63e-4b05-97ce-4164d2fc9c26"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getVisibleValue($rowNumber, $columnNumber)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameters are: </p><ul><li>rowNumber – a row number starts with 0.</li><li>columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number. </li></ul><p>The returned value is the value in a visible cell. If the cell contains an element, the value is the Element.</p><h3>Getting visible values from row element and column number as string</h3><p><strong>getVisibleValueAsString( rowElement : Element, columnNumber : int ) : String</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="edda4a6c-9395-450d-90c5-b09cb8244bdd"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getVisibleValueAsString($rowElement, $columnNumber)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameters are: </p><ul><li>rowElement – a row element.</li><li>columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.</li></ul><p>The returned value is the value in a visible cell and is converted into String. The String value is created by the modeling tool text representation API.</p><h3>Getting visible values from row and column numbers as string</h3><p><strong>getVisibleValueAsString( rowNumber : int, columnNumber : int ) : String</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0e5fe694-94b0-4143-9945-7235cf9881f8"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getVisibleValueAsString($rowNumber, $columnNumber)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameters are:</p><ul><li>rowNumber – a row number starts with 0.</li><li>columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.</li></ul><p>The returned value is the value in a visible cell and is converted into String. The String value is created by the modeling tool text representation API.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

## PAGE 00545: Generic Table Wizard

- page_id: `169677099`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169677099/Generic+Table+Wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-04-10T16:43:05.443+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Model Visualizer
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Editions

639875940

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Editions

639875942

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Editions

639875941

#### NOTE: Editions

Editions

This functionality is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions only.

A [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generic table' space='MD2024xR1'] allows you to display a list of selected elements and their properties in a single place.

The Generic Table Wizard helps you create a table when model elements and their relationships are already created and specified. You can select which elements (e.g., classes, packages, use cases, and relationships) will be included in a new table. You can also select properties of the selected element types, which will be displayed as columns in the table.

The Generic Table Wizard guides you through several steps and collects information along the way. It will automatically create a table with your selected elements, freeing you from creating the table manually.

To create a Generic Table using the Generic Table Wizard

1. Open the Generic Table Wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the Diagrams menu, select Diagram Wizards > Generic Table Wizard .
  - From the Analyze menu, select Model Visualizer . In the open The Model Visualizer dialog, select to open the Generic Table Wizard and click Start .
2. Follow the steps as directed by the wizard.

639875939

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generic table' space='MD2024xR1']

You can also select properties of the selected element types, which will be displayed as columns in the table.</p><p>The Generic Table Wizard guides you through several steps and collects information along the way. It will automatically create a table with your selected elements, freeing you from creating the table manually.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a Generic Table using the Generic Table Wizard</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Generic Table Wizard</strong><strong> </strong>by doing one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the <strong>Diagrams</strong> menu, select <strong>Diagram Wizards</strong> &gt; <strong>Generic Table Wizard</strong>.</li><li>From the <strong>Analyze</strong> menu, select <strong>Model Visualizer</strong>. ## PAGE 00546: Getting a value from a MagicDraw element to a template

- page_id: `169678706`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678706/Getting+a+value+from+a+MagicDraw+element+to+a+template
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-12-26T06:28:11.236+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Template Creation Tutorial > Creating Your First Template
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1355429125

1355429128

1355429124

**On this page**

3

1355429128

#### Getting element values to a template

You can access the value of an element, and include it in a report in Report Wizard.

To get a value from a modeling tool element to a template

1. First you need to know the element type and attributes. Open the Specification dialog to see the type and attributes. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] The Specification dialog showing the element type on the title bar and the attributes in the properties specification table.
2. Open Microsoft Word and type, for example: text The example above shows that **$Class** is an array that contains all the Class elements in a **Selected Element Scope** (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report Wizard' space='MD190SP1'] for more information). **$class** is the individual Class element inside the array. To access the value of a property in **$class**, type: **$class** followed by **"."** and the attribute's name ("**.name**", "**.visibility**", and "**.isAbstract**" are the names of the **$class** attribute). The syntax for accessing the attribute value can be represented by: **$[Referenced object].[Attribute's name]**.

When you generate the output of the template for a project with a single Class element, the result will be shown as follows:

```text

```

#### Glossary

- Array An array is a container object that holds a fixed number of values of a single type. The length of an array is established when the array is created. After it is created, its length is fixed.
- Attribute Attribute defines values that can be attached to instances of the class or interface. For example, an attribute of a class represents the characteristics of the class.
- Class Class is the concrete realization of EncapsulatedClassifier and BehavioredClassifier. The purpose of a class is to specify a classification of objects, and to specify the Features that characterize the structure and behavior of those objects.
- Element Element is a constituent of a model. Every element has the inherent capability of owning other elements. When an element is removed from a model, all its ownedElements are also removed from the model. The abstract syntax for each kind of element specifies what other kind of elements it may own. Every element in a model must be owned by exactly one other element of that model, with the exception of the top-level Packages of the model.

#### More information about working with values in Report Wizard

To learn more about working with values in Report Wizard, visit the following pages:

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Retrieving Slot Values' space='MD2024xR1']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Getting property value' space='MD2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5294ab9b-0dbd-4c35-8e21-a339488bd832"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1355429125</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b1c9fce2-185b-4bb6-9dcb-d67262f12cce"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1355429128</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0309a6cd-d039-4d5b-856e-9ed3865c247f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1355429124</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c830c14c-1892-431b-a7f2-00db02d796c2"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3d5422f7-7d55-4ac3-a689-fd1b84c6cd89"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1355429128</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h2>Getting element values to a template</h2><p>You can access the value of an element, and include it in a report in Report Wizard.</p><p><br /></p><p>To get a value from a modeling tool element to a template</p><hr /><ol><li><p>First you need to know the element type and attributes. Open the Specification dialog to see the type and attributes.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="classspec.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Getting a value from a MagicDraw element to a template" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>The Specification dialog showing the element type on the title bar and the attributes in the properties specification table.</h6></li><li><p>Open Microsoft Word and type, for example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="68018f45-ecc9-4f44-898d-539c24d3e41c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach ($class in $Class)
 Name: $class.name
 Owner: $class.owner.name
 Visibility: $class.visibility
 Is Abstract: $class.isAbstract
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The example above shows that <strong>$Class</strong> is an array that contains all the Class elements in a <strong>Selected Element Scope</strong> (see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD190SP1" ri:content-title="Report Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Report Wizard user guide]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for more information). <strong>$class</strong> is the individual Class element inside the array. To access the value of a property in <strong>$class</strong>, type: <strong>$class</strong> followed by <strong>&quot;.&quot;</strong> and the attribute's name (&quot;<strong>.name</strong>&quot;, &quot;<strong>.visibility</strong>&quot;, and &quot;<strong>.isAbstract</strong>&quot; are the names of the <strong>$class</strong> attribute). The syntax for accessing the attribute value can be represented by: <strong>$[Referenced object].[Attribute's name]</strong>. </p></li></ol><p>When you generate the output of the template for a project with a single Class element, the result will be shown as follows:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8a810927-61b0-4b82-8bd9-273ff4b33faa"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Name: Customer
 Owner: com
 Visibility: public
 Is Abstract: false]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Glossary</h2><ul><li><strong>Array</strong><br />An array <span style="color: rgb(84,84,84);">is a container object that holds a fixed number of values of a single type. The length of an </span><span style="color: rgb(106,106,106);">array</span><span style="color: rgb(84,84,84);"> is established when the </span><span style="color: rgb(106,106,106);">array</span><span style="color: rgb(84,84,84);"> is created. After it is created, its length is fixed.</span></li><li><strong>Attribute</strong><br />Attribute defines values that can be attached to instances of the class or interface. For example, an attribute of a class represents the characteristics of the class.</li><li class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Class</strong><br />Class is the concrete realization of EncapsulatedClassifier and BehavioredClassifier. The purpose of a class is to specify a classification of objects, and to specify the Features that characterize the structure and behavior of those objects.</li><li class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Element</strong><br />Element is a constituent of a model. Every element has the inherent capability of owning other elements. When an element is removed from a model, all its ownedElements are also removed from the model. The abstract syntax for each kind of element specifies what other kind of elements it may own. Every element in a model must be owned by exactly one other element of that model, with the exception of the top-level Packages of the model.</li></ul><h2>More information about working with values in Report Wizard</h2><p>To learn more about working with values in Report Wizard, visit the following pages:</p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Retrieving Slot Values" /></ac:link></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Getting property value" /></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

## PAGE 00547: Getting comparison summary information and changes legend

- page_id: `169676876`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169676876/Getting+comparison+summary+information+and+changes+legend
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2019-06-05T14:29:29.725+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Comparing projects > Analyzing comparison results
- labels: ['summary-and-legend', 'comparison-summary', 'comparison-result']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can see how many differences have been detected between the base and compared project version on the **Summary and legend** panel that is located at the top right of the **Difference Viewer** window. The panel also helps to understand the decorations and highlighting of elements in both the **Differences**tree and the **Specification** panel.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can see how many differences have been detected between the base and compared project version on the <strong>Summary and legend</strong> panel that is located at the top right of the <strong>Difference Viewer</strong> window. The panel also helps to understand the decorations and highlighting of elements in both the <strong>Differences </strong>tree and the <strong> <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="fa21c6bb-741a-4318-83ff-bf732f4ae486">Specification</ac:inline-comment-marker> </strong> <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="fa21c6bb-741a-4318-83ff-bf732f4ae486"> panel.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:border="true" ac:title="Project comparison summary and legend" ac:alt="Project comparison summary and legend"><ri:attachment ri:filename="compare_legend.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Getting comparison summary information and changes legend" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169676031 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00548: Getting merge summary information and changes legend

- page_id: `169676031`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169676031/Getting+merge+summary+information+and+changes+legend
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2019-03-04T11:10:01.406+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Tools > Model Merge > Analyzing and managing merge results
- labels: ['merge-summary', 'summary-and-legend', 'merged-result', 'merge-legend']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1767089336

INLINE

1767089338

INLINE

1767089337

INLINE

The **Summary/Legend** panel is located at the bottom left of the **Merge** window. The panel helps to understand the marking and highlighting of elements in both the **Merged Result** tree/list and the **Specification** panel.

On the **Summary/Legend** panel, for 3-way merge, you can see how many differences have been detected between the ancestor and the source, as well as between the ancestor and the target.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

For 2-way merge, you can see how many differences have been detected between two versions, and what kind of changes were detected.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1767089335

INLINE

  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Viewing and analyzing changes of modified diagrams' space='MD2024xR1']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169678178 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00549: Getting property value

- page_id: `169678178`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678178/Getting+property+value
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-01T13:55:00.369+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Template variables
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can directly get the property value of the element by calling property name:

```text

```

**propertyName** should be typed in camel case style. The name and returned type of property can be found in UMLMetaModel UserGuide. It lists all the properties which can be called through each element type.

Please be aware of the type of the returned value, as some properties may return an array. You can get each value by using a **#foreach** directive or by specifying array index number. For example:

```text

```

```text

```

Moreover, the customized elements are able to get their property value immediately by calling property name:

```text

```

You can see the representation text of the element by opening the Specification dialog.

The dialog name pattern is

```text
]]>
```

While the property name is shown in the same dialog.

If the property value is not printed out to your report, you can assume the property that you call is not the property of the customized element. It may be the property of the applied stereotype. You would rather check the stereotype owner of that property by going to the Applied Stereotype property of the element, then right click on the right column, and select the stereotype in the containment tree.

If you navigate to the stereotype element on the containment tree, then you can expand it to see its owned properties. If you can see your desired property in the stereotype, you can get its value by:

```text

```

We also provide helper module in section *[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Helper tools' space='MD2024xR1']*. These modules are utility modules for getting property values. More details about openAPI of each element can be found at:

*{md_install}\plugins\com.nomagic.magicdraw.reportwizard\api\javadoc.zip*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can directly get the property value of the element by calling property name:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4704d028-1cde-4b3f-aa73-4ccaa0daed78"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$element.propertyName]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><strong>propertyName</strong> should be typed in camel case style. The name and returned type of property can be found in UMLMetaModel UserGuide. It lists all the properties which can be called through each element type.</p><p>Please be aware of the type of the returned value, as some properties may return an array. You can get each value by using a <strong>#foreach</strong> directive or by specifying array index number. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c63d14e2-10d4-42d2-9839-cfdb8d143030"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($c in $class.classifier)
$c.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="02b425b7-c111-4c29-a335-f9603f84261e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set($classifier = $class.classifier)
$classifier.get(0)
$classifier.get(1)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Moreover, the customized elements are able to get their property value immediately by calling property name:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="817fc16a-028f-4179-8361-0ddafae72d98"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$RepresentationText.propertyName]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>You can see the representation text of the element by opening the Specification dialog.</p><p>The dialog name pattern is </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b36348c4-070a-46ff-9aab-a36d797f7a4c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Specification of <RepresentationText> <ElementName>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>While the property name is shown in the same dialog.</p><p>If the property value is not printed out to your report, you can assume the property that you call is not the property of the customized element. It may be the property of the applied stereotype. You would rather check the stereotype owner of that property by going to the Applied Stereotype property of the element, then right click on the right column, and select the stereotype in the containment tree.</p><p>If you navigate to the stereotype element on the containment tree, then you can expand it to see its owned properties. If you can see your desired property in the stereotype, you can get its value by:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="af6d8949-f382-44c9-bb2d-ebd943467c0e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$report.getStereotypePropertyString($element, "stereotypeName", "propertyName")]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br />We also provide helper module in section <em><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Helper tools" /></ac:link></em>. These modules are utility modules for getting property values. More details about openAPI of each element can be found at:</p><p><em>{md_install}\plugins\com.nomagic.magicdraw.reportwizard\api\javadoc.zip</em></p>
````

## PAGE 00551: Getting started with specifying criteria

- page_id: `169676420`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169676420/Getting+started+with+specifying+criteria
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:21.357+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Specifying criteria for querying model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

2217659847

2217659849

1759777648

**On this page:**

3

2217659848

##### Opening the dialog for specifying criteria

The dialog for specifying criteria can be opened from several different places.

To open the dialog for specifying criteria, do one of the following

- In the Criteria area of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency Matrix' space='MD2024xR1'] , click the ... button next to the Dependency Criteria box. The Dependency Criteria dialog opens.
- In the Criteria area of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Relation Map' space='MD2024xR1'] , click the ... button next to the Relation Criteria box. The Relation Critera dialog opens.
- Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1'] of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart Package' space='MD2024xR1'] , under the Content category on the right, click the cell of the Query property value, and then click the ... button. The Query dialog opens.
- Open the Specification window of a selected element, click [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Traceability' space='MD2024xR1'] on the left, and then click Create on the right. The Expression dialog opens.
- On the toolbar of a generic table, click the Columns button, and then select [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Custom traceability rules' space='MD2024xR1'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with columns' space='MD2024xR1'] . The Expression dialog opens.
- Open the Specification window of an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating executable opaque behaviors' space='MD2024xR1'] , click the cell of the Body and Language property value, and then click the ... button. When the Body and Language dialog opens, select StructuredExpression from the Language drop-down list.
- Open the Specification window of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating and managing Legend Items' space='MD2024xR1'] , click the specification cell of the Elements by Condition property, and then click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] . The Elements by Condition dialog opens.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The layout of the dialog is the same in all the described cases. On the left side of the dialog, you can select an operation type that best suits for specifying the needed criterion. Selecting an operation type opens the criteria specification panel on the right side of the dialog.

##### Specified and unspecified operation display

The Simple Navigation, Metachain Navigation, Find, and Implied Relation operations are always displayed on the left side of the dialog, even if they were removed during the previous edits of the expression. For this reason, empty template operation nodes are displayed in the italic format below the specified operations. Once you specify an expression for a template operation, a template operation of that type will not appear during subsequent edits. In such a case, only the specified operation is displayed. You can create a new operation via *Create operation...* option.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']On the left: empty template operations in italic format. 
On the right: template operations with specified operations in roman format. Empty template operations of these types no longer appear during the subsequent edit.

If an operation is specified, it is displayed in roman format and at the top of the operations list. You can manually rearrange the specified operations by dragging and dropping them anywhere on the list. In such cases, the arrangement of the specified operations stays the same when you open the expression again.

Also, be advised that before starting to specify criteria it is useful to read the tips and warnings (if there are any), which appear after the pointer is hovered over the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button or the [IMAGE alt='' src='']button respectively.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
'Find' operation is specified, appearing in roman formatand at the top of the operations list. Other operations under 'Find' are not specified and are thus italicized.

2217659846

- Specifying criteria for querying model
  - Using Simple Navigation
  - Using Metachain Navigation
  - Using Find operation
  - Using Implied Relations
  - Creating new operations
    - Calling operations from the model
    - Creating script operations

For this reason, empty template operation nodes are displayed in the italic format below the specified operations. Once you specify an expression for a template operation, a template operation of that type will not appear during subsequent edits. In such a case, only the specified operation is displayed. You can create a new operation via </ac:inline-comment-marker> <em> <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="f72e8f0b-eea7-49e3-a373-0c508b60b738">Create operation...</ac:inline-comment-marker> </em> <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="f72e8f0b-eea7-49e3-a373-0c508b60b738"> option.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-bottom: 30.0px;"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="f72e8f0b-eea7-49e3-a373-0c508b60b738"> <ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="expressions_specified_for_operations.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Getting started with specifying criteria" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>On the left: empty template operations in italic format. <br />On the right: template operations with specified operations in roman format. Empty template operations of these types no longer appear during the subsequent edit.</ac:inline-comment-marker></h6><p>If an operation is specified, it is displayed in roman format and at the top of the operations list. You can manually rearrange the specified operations by dragging and dropping them anywhere on the list. In such cases, the arrangement of the specified operations stays the same when you open the expression again. </p><p>Also, be advised that before starting to specify criteria it is useful to read the tips and warnings (if there are any), which appear after the pointer is hovered over the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="info.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Getting started with specifying criteria" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button or the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="warning_small.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Getting started with specifying criteria" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>button respectively.<br /> </p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="tips_icon_italics.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Getting started with specifying criteria" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <br />'<span style="color: rgb(94,108,132);">Find' operation is specified, appearing <span style="color: rgb(94,108,132);">in roman format </span>and at the top of the operations list. Other operations under 'Find' are not specified and are thus italicized.</span></h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ec0a516b-1dbe-49e2-86dc-fd0dfb581077"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2217659846</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Specifying+criteria+for+querying+model">Specifying criteria for querying model</a><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Using+Simple+Navigation">Using Simple Navigation</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Using+Metachain+Navigation">Using Metachain Navigation</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Using+Find+operation">Using Find operation</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Using+Implied+Relations">Using Implied Relations</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Creating+new+operations">Creating new operations</a><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Calling+operations+from+the+model">Calling operations from the model</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Creating+script+operations">Creating script operations</a></li></ul></li></ul></li></ul><p> </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169677495 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00552: Global element usage search

- page_id: `169677495`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169677495/Global+element+usage+search
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:26.470+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The global element usage search allows you to check the usages of elements within the Teamwork Cloud repository. This makes it easier to examine how deleting or modifying an element can impact the projects that use it. The following demo shows how this functionality works:

600400

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

- You can only use the Global Element Usage Search feature if [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Global element usage indexing and search' space='TWCloud2024xR1'] on the server.
- You can investigate element usages only in projects that have been [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating indexing configurations' space='TWCloud2024xR1'].
- If you use a server of an earlier version than your modeling tool, you may need to specify the Web Application Platform URL option in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing environment options' space='MD2024xR1']**Environment Options** dialog .

The command is only available for the elements in an open (main) project. You cannot execute it if:

- the selected element is from a Used Project
- the selected element has not yet been committed to the server
- multiple elements are selected

To find element usages

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space='MD2024xR1'] or diagram pane, right-click an element and select Related Elements > Search in Server > Used by in Server . Choose this option if you want to search solely for the usages of the element.
  - In the Containment tab or diagram pane, right-click an element and select Related Elements > Search in Server > Used by in Server Recursively . Choose this option if you want to search for the usages of both the element and all of the elements that are owned by it.

The element usage search results panel opens with a list of server projects that use the selected element:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Only the latest indexed branch versions are found. For example, if an element is used in the historical version but is no longer in use, it is not displayed in the element usage search results panel.

If you see a warning message (see below), it means that you do not have permission to read the project(s) using the element you performed the usage search for.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Press the Refresh button [IMAGE alt='' src='']to see the latest information on element usage.

2. Select a project to see a list of project branches and versions thatuse the element.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Press **Actions [IMAGE alt='' src=''] > Open project**to open the specific project branch that uses the element.

3. To investigate element usage in more detail, select a specific branch. This shows usage details at the element level:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Global element usage indexing and search' space='TWCloud2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating indexing configurations' space='TWCloud2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The global element usage search allows you to check the usages of elements within the Teamwork Cloud repository. This makes it easier to examine how deleting or modifying an element can impact the projects that use it. The following demo shows how this functionality works:<br /><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="30b067a8-677a-493c-96e3-f83d1ee3d132"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">600</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1TUWPUtM4Pc" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">400</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><br /><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a596da95-c8bd-4ec6-932d-e8132d0bcfc0"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Prerequisites</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>You can only use the Global Element Usage Search feature if <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloud2024xR1" ri:content-title="Global element usage indexing and search" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Elasticsearch is installed]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on the server.</li><li><p>You can investigate element usages only in projects that have been <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloud2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating indexing configurations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[indexed]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></li><li>If you use a server of an earlier version than your modeling tool, you may need to specify the <strong>Web Application Platform URL</strong> option in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Customizing environment options" /><ac:link-body><strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The command is only available for the elements in an open (main) project. You cannot execute it if:</p><ul><li>the selected element is from a Used Project</li><li>the selected element has not yet been committed to the server</li><li>multiple elements are selected</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To find element usages</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Containment tab" /></ac:link><span> </span>or diagram pane, right-click an element and select <strong>Related Elements</strong> &gt;<span> </span><strong>Search in Server &gt; Used by in Server</strong>. Choose this option if you want to search solely for the usages of the element.</li><li>In the <a class="external-link" href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2022xR1/Containment+tab">Containment tab</a><span> </span>or diagram pane, right-click an element and select <strong>Related Elements</strong><span> </span>&gt;<span> </span><strong>Search in Server &gt; Used by in Server Recursively</strong>. Choose this option if you want to search for the usages of <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">both the element and </span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">all of the elements that are owned by it.</span></li></ul></li></ol><p>The element usage search results panel opens with a list of server projects that use the selected element:</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="global_element_usage_search_results.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Global element usage search" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8c567ba2-6e9a-436f-83f3-77a6c65a5ea1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Only the latest indexed branch versions are found. For example, if an element is used in the historical version but is no longer in use, it is not displayed in the element usage search results panel.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target">If you see a warning message (see below), it means that you do not have permission to read the project(s) using the element you performed the usage search for.</p><p><ac:image ac:width="674"><ri:attachment ri:filename="some_usages_cannot_be_displayed.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Global element usage search" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7b21078d-3a35-432c-b9ec-f8b2f0be9ee2"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Press the Refresh button <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Global element usage search" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>t<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">o see the latest information on element usage</span><span>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>2. Select a project to see a list of project <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">branches and versions that </span>use the element.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="element_usage_in_project_branches.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Global element usage search" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="23ec14e1-8db2-4969-a4f4-4b855a177c91"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Press <strong>Actions <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="actions_icon.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Global element usage search" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">&gt; Open project</span> </strong>to open the specific project branch that uses the element.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>3. To investigate element usage in more detail, select a specific branch. This shows usage details at the element level:</p><p><ac:image ac:width="674"><ri:attachment ri:filename="element_usage_details.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Global element usage search" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloud2024xR1" ri:content-title="Global element usage indexing and search" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloud2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating indexing configurations" /></ac:link></li></ul>
````

## PAGE 00553: Global options for Code Engineering

- page_id: `169679255`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169679255/Global+options+for+Code+Engineering
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-01T16:48:18.932+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Code Engineering
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Functionality Availability

419230510

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Functionality Availability

419230512

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Functionality Availability

419230511

##### Code Engineering Options for all Sets in your Project

Choose**Project**from the**Options**menu. TheProject Optionsdialog box appears.You can set the Code Engineering options in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space='MD2024xR1']**Project Options** dialog. The **Project Options** dialog box has two main collections of customizable options, represented by the hierarchy tree on the left side of the dialog box. The figure below illustrates **Code Engineering** related options.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Code Engineering options in the Project Options dialog.

In the option list, select **Code engineering**and specify the following items:

| Item name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Default Working Directory | Enter the name of your working directory. |
| Default Working Package | Define any package for the reverse output or code generation. Your model will be reversed or the code will be generated from this specified package. |
| Default Paths for References | Add specific profiles, used projects, or libraries to define where to search for paths for references during reverse/code generation. |
| Default Encoding | Click to open a list of available encodings. |
| Default language | When selected, the syntax checker of the selected language runs while Code Engineering is executed. |
| Use Syntax Checker | When selected, the syntax checker runs while Code Engineering is executed. |
| Directory for Temporal Files | You can specify Active Directory, System, or you can select another directory. |

The following table describes other **Code Engineering**options:

| Option | Element Name | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Code Generation |  | Set code generation options using the fields listed in the right side of the Project options dialog box. The Code generation area contains boxes with the same functionality as the Code generations options dialog box. See . |
| Reverse |  | Set reverse options for all reverse actions of the project using the options listed on the right side of the Project options dialog box. The area contains boxes that have the same functionality as in the Reverse options dialog box. |
| Java Language Options Set the generated code style for Java programming languages in the Default language field found on the right side of the Project Options dialog box. | Generate opening bracket in new line | Opens a bracket in the new line being generated. |
|  | Generate spaces | Generate spaces inside an assignment and other operators. |
|  | Generate empty documentation | Comment brackets are placed in your code, unless class in the model has no documentation. |
|  | Automatic “import” generation | Automatic generation of "import" sentences according to classes referenced in the generated class. |
|  | Class count to generate import on demand | Specify the number of classes imported from a single package until all statements importing a single class are substituted with a statement importing an entire package. |
|  | Documentation Processor | After selecting the Java Doc processor, click to open the . |
|  | Style | Two styles are available for documentation. |
|  | Use CLASSPATH | The button is activated. Search a classpath for importing sentence generation in the Set classpath dialog box.Click Get from System to get the CLASSPATH variable defined by the operating system or click Add and select the classpath directory. |
|  | Java Source | Available choices are 1.4, 5.0, 7.0, or 8.0. |
|  | Header | Add a specific header to all your code files. Click and enter the header text in the Header dialog. You can also define $DATE, $AUTHOR, and $TIME in the header. |
| C++ Language Options Set the generated code style for C++ programming languages. | Generate opening bracket in new line | Opens a bracket in the newly generated line. |
| Generate spaces | Spaces inside an assignment and other operators are generated. |  |
| Generate empty documentation | Comment brackets are placed in your code, unless the class in the model has no documentation. |  |
|  | Generate methods body into class | Select check box to generate methods body into class. |
|  | Generate field initialization into class | Generates the value initialization for the attribute. |
|  | Automatically remove unnecessary usage relationship | Tracks the use of classes and removes the unnecessary include directives. |
|  | Documentation Style | Four styles are available for documentation. |
|  | Use include path | Click and then specify the path for the #include directives in the Set Include Path dialog box. |
|  | Use explicit macros | Select the check box. The button is activated. Click it and use a set of predefined macros in the C++ Macros dialog box. |
|  | Header | Add the specific header to all your code files. Click and enter the header text in the Header screen. You can also define $DATE, $AUTHOR, and $TIME in the header. |
| CORBA IDL 3.0 language options | Generate documentation | Includes the documentation of an element in the comment. |
|  | Generate opening bracket in new line | Opens a bracket in the newly generated line. |
|  | Generate spaces | Spaces inside an assignment and other operators are generated. |
|  | Generate empty documentation | Comment brackets are placed in your code, unless the class in the model has no documentation. |
|  | Generate imports | Generation of "import" statements for classes referenced in the generated class. |
|  | Generate preprocessor directives | Generates pre-processors directives. |
|  | Generate end comments for composite elements | Generates end comments with composite element names. |
|  | Generate operation parameters on the new line | Generates operation parameters on the new line. |
|  | Documentation Style | Three styles are available for documentation. |
|  | Header “...” | Add the specific header to all your code files. Click and enter the header text in the Header dialog. You can also define $DATE, $AUTHOR, and $TIME in the header. Since modeling tool version 17.0, the variable $DOCUMENTATION can be used to export the documentation of the file component (from the File View package) as a header of the IDL file.Functionality AvailabilityThe variable $DOCUMENTATION is available with modeling tool 17.0 Service Pack 4 and later versions. |
|  | Set Include Path | Specify the path for the #include directives. Click to open the Select Folder dialog box. |
| DDL Language Options | Generate opening bracket in new line | Opens a bracket in the newly generated line. |
|  | Generate spaces | Spaces inside an assignment and other operators are generated. |
|  | Generate documentation | Comment brackets are placed in your code, unless the class in the model has no documentation. |
|  | Header | Add the specific header to all your code files. Click the button and enter header text in the Header dialog. You may also define $DATE, $AUTHOR, and $TIME in the header. |

419230507

**Related Pages**:

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Code Engineering' space='MD2024xR1']

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c63ab735-4086-48bb-a5bc-fa4ef948bd0e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">419230510</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b55434fb-167f-4633-b078-3ee8cd1dbff2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">419230512</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="23dad635-fb23-44a7-8a76-1dd694a44b6e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">419230511</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3>Code Engineering Options for all Sets in your Project</h3><p><span>Choose </span><strong>Project</strong><span> <span>from the </span><strong>Options </strong><span>menu</span>. The Code generation area contains boxes with the same functionality as the Code generations options dialog box. See <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Generating Code" /></ac:link>.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Reverse</strong></td><td colspan="1"><br /></td><td><span>Set reverse options for all reverse actions of the project using the options listed on the right side of the Project options dialog box. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Reverse Options" /></ac:link> area contains boxes that have the same functionality as in the Reverse options dialog box.</span></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span><strong>Java Language </strong></span><strong>Options </strong><span>Set the generated code style for Java programming languages in the </span><strong>Default</strong><span> language field found on the right side of the </span><strong>Project Options </strong><span>dialog box. </span></td><td colspan="1"><strong>Generate opening bracket in new line </strong></td><td colspan="1"><span>Opens a bracket in the new line being generated. </span></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><br /></td><td colspan="1"><strong>Generate spaces</strong></td><td colspan="1"><span>Generate spaces inside an assignment and other operators.</span></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><br /></td><td colspan="1"><strong>Generate empty documentation</strong></td><td colspan="1"><span>Comment brackets are placed in your code, unless class in the model has no documentation.</span></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><br /></td><td colspan="1"><strong>Automatic “import” generation</strong></td><td colspan="1"><span>Automatic generation of &quot;import&quot; sentences according to classes referenced in the generated class.</span></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><br /></td><td colspan="1"><strong>Class count to generate import on demand</strong></td><td colspan="1"><span>Specify the number of classes imported from a single package until all statements importing a single class are substituted with a statement importing an entire package.</span></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><br /></td><td colspan="1"><strong>Documentation</strong><span> </span><strong>Processor</strong></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>After selecting the Java Doc processor, click  <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="22"><ri:attachment ri:filename="three-dot-btn1.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Basic tasks in tables" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>  to open the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Java Documentation Properties dialog box" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Documentation Properties dialog box]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><br /></td><td colspan="1"><strong>Style</strong></td><td colspan="1">Two styles are available for documentation.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><br /></td><td colspan="1"><strong>Use CLASSPATH</strong></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span>The <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="22"><ri:attachment ri:filename="three-dot-btn1.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Basic tasks in tables" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button is activated. Search a classpath for importing sentence generation in the Set classpath dialog box.</span></p><p><span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="set_classpath.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Global options for Code Engineering" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p><p><span>Click </span><strong>Get from System</strong><span> to get the CLASSPATH variable defined by the operating system or click <strong>Add</strong> and select the classpath directory.</span></p></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><br /></td><td colspan="1"><strong>Java Source</strong></td><td colspan="1">Available choices are 1.4, 5.0, 7.0, or 8.0.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><br /></td><td colspan="1"><strong>Header</strong></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span>Add a specific header to all your code files. and then specify the path for the #include directives in the </span><strong>Set Include Path</strong> dialog box.</p></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><br /></td><td colspan="1"><strong>Use explicit macros</strong></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span>Select the check box. The <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="22"><ri:attachment ri:filename="three-dot-btn1.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Basic tasks in tables" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button is activated. Click it and</span> use a set of predefined macros <span>in the </span><strong>C++ Macros</strong> dialog box.</p></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><br /></td><td colspan="1"><strong>Header</strong></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span>Add the specific header to all your code files. Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="22"><ri:attachment ri:filename="three-dot-btn1.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Basic tasks in tables" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </span>and enter the header text in the <strong>Header</strong> screen. You can also define $DATE, $AUTHOR, and $TIME in the header. </p></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>CORBA IDL 3.0 language options</strong></td><td colspan="1"><p><strong>Generate documentation</strong></p></td><td colspan="1"><p><span>Includes the documentation of an element in the comment.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><br /></td><td colspan="1"><strong>Generate opening bracket in new line </strong></td><td colspan="1"><p><span>Opens a bracket in the newly <span>generated</span> line.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><br /></td><td colspan="1"><strong>Generate spaces </strong></td><td colspan="1"><p><span>Spaces inside an assignment and other operators are generated.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><br /></td><td colspan="1"><strong>Generate empty documentation </strong></td><td colspan="1"><p><span>Comment brackets are placed in your code, unless the class in the model has no documentation.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><br /></td><td colspan="1"><strong>Generate imports </strong></td><td colspan="1"><p><span>Generation of &quot;import&quot; statements for classes referenced in the generated class.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><br /></td><td colspan="1"><strong>Generate preprocessor directives </strong></td><td colspan="1">Generates pre-processors directives.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><br /></td><td colspan="1"><strong>Generate end comments for composite elements</strong></td><td colspan="1">Generates end comments with composite element names.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><br /></td><td colspan="1"><strong>Generate operation parameters on the new line</strong></td><td colspan="1"><p>Generates operation parameters on the new line.</p><p><br /></p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><br /></td><td colspan="1"><strong>Documentation Style </strong></td><td colspan="1"><p><span>Three styles are available for documentation.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><br /></td><td colspan="1"><strong>Header “...” </strong></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span>Add the specific header to all your code files. You can also define $DATE, $AUTHOR, and $TIME in the header. </span></p><p>Since modeling tool version 17.0, the variable $DOCUMENTATION can be used to export the documentation of the file component (from the File View package) as a header of the IDL file.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4a0c4e0e-b9f7-4dcf-92ee-500dec1899d0"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Functionality Availability</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body>The variable $DOCUMENTATION is available with modeling tool 17.0 Service Pack 4 and later versions.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><br /></td><td colspan="1"><strong>Set Include Path </strong></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span>Specify the path for the <span>#include directives</span>. You may also define $DATE, $AUTHOR, and $TIME in the header.</span></p></div></td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="510836c5-77a3-4a16-8544-8b91435348ab"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">419230507</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related Pages</strong>:</p><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="490faf13-3e88-47fe-b7f7-5fb0aa644afe" /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="02d35c92-f5cd-436c-8780-8041a4ea0520"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Code Engineering" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````
