## PAGE 00565: Hiding packages

- page_id: `169677488`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169677488/Hiding+packages
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-03-22T08:15:11.792+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud
- labels: ['hidden-packages', 'non-hidden-part', 'non-hidden-model', 'non-hidden-project', 'synchronization', 'manual-synchronization', 'automatic-synchronization', 'stripping-project', 'reusing-model', 'reusing-project']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Example

1191527483

INLINE

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Example

1191527485

INLINE

1333798886

INLINE

**On this page**

33

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Example

1191527484

INLINE

The Hiding packages utility lets you specify which model[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package' space='MD2024xR1']elements within the model packages, and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing project usages' space='MD2024xR1'] should be hidden. Once specified, the public version of a model will be created by cutting/deleting the hidden parts. You can strip your project to reuse only a particular part of the project in another project. To strip your project, you must hide packages you do not want to share.

700500

##### Hiding packages

To hide packages

1. Open a server project.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Right-click the package you want to hide. On the shortcut menu, click Project Usages > Hide Packages .
  - On the main menu, click Collaborate > Hidden packages .
3. The **Hidden Packages** dialog opens. In the **Tree/List**select the packages you want to hide and****add them to the **Hidden Packages**side. Move the selected package back to the **Tree/List**side to unhide it. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Click **OK** when you are done.
5. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Analyzing usages and dependencies' space='MD2024xR1'] is suggested. Usage checking may be time-consuming; however, it helps to identify elements that are related to the soon-to-be hidden parts . If any issues are found, they are displayed and described in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Usages and dependencies search results window' space='MD2024xR1']**Package Usage window** .
6. After you complete or skip Usage checking, you will see a notification that hiding the selected elements will be completed only after committing your project.
7. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Committing changes to Teamwork Cloud' space='MD2024xR1'] the project.
8. The stripped model version is created to be available to use in other projects. Hidden packages are marked with a lock sign [ATTACHMENT filename='hidden_package_notation.png'] in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='MD2024xR1'] .

##### Hiding and unhiding used projects

You can hide/unhide a used project the same way as other packages. When hiding a used project, you cannot hide separate used project parts: you need to hide the whole used project.After hiding is done, a dedicated branch is created with the stripped model version, whereall the elements of the used project are hidden from the Containment tree due to being unused, and all references to these elements are cut from other model places.

To hide an indirectly used project, you need to hide it in the directly used project first.

#### TIP: Example

Example

Let's say you have a project A that uses a project B, and this project uses a project C. You want to hide the project C inside the project A. In this case, you need to hide it in the project B directly first and then use the public branch of the project B.

Indirectly used projects can also be hidden/unhidden automatically.Typically, this happens when the status of the used project changes:

- when the used project is hidden/unhidden
- when the used project is updated
- when the used project is removed
- when the new used project is used
- when used or using projects are merged
- when the used project is exported
- when the used project is imported

To hide an indirectly used project automatically, you need to hide all directly used projects that use the specific indirectly used project.

#### TIP: Example

Example

Let's say you have a project A that uses a project B, and this project uses a project C. You hide the project B and then the project C is hidden as well; however, if you add a new usage D that also uses C, the hidden usage of the project C is automatically unhidden because not all directly used projects using the project C are hidden in this case.

##### Dedicated branch for stripped model

A stripped model is stored in a dedicated branch created when element hiding is performed for the first time. By default, this branch is named *Public Project*. You can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Branching projects' space='MD2024xR1'] if you need to.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Project with it`s Public Project branch. The public project now can be reused in another project.

If all packages from the model are unhidden, the dedicated branch will be deactivated.

If the main project is using a public branch of another project and that branch gets deactivated, you will be informed about this change with a warning icon in the main project with a warning icon [IMAGE alt='' src=''] in:

- Containment tree (under Project Usages root),
- Project Usages dialog,
- Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog.

The state of the used project is checked at the same time interval as checking for a new project version in the background, or by clicking the Project Usages Update button.

##### Hidden package changes

After a package or a used project is successfully hidden or unhidden, an icon with a lock on a package (or used project) is shown in a Difference viewer.

The icon with a lock on a package (or on a used project package) is also shown in:

- Upcoming Changes browser panel,
- Local changes browser panel,
- Commit dialog,
- Content History panel,
- Element History.

##### Model synchronization

You must have the read-write permissionfor the stripped model branchto synchronize it.

A stripped model is read-only. It can only be used for reuse in other projects, not for editing (it can only be modified by synchronizing it – read-write permission is required for the stripped model branch). It can only be used for reuse in other projects, not for editing. However, you can edit the main project. On committing, checking is performed to see if the changes affect hidden and public parts of the model, and model synchronization is initialized. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space='MD2024xR1']**Project Options**dialog, you can define the model synchronization mode. The default mode is **Manual**; that is, after committing project changes, you will be notified about local changes affecting a public model stored in a dedicated branch. You can then decide either to synchronize changes or not.

The server project with hidden packages (and public branches) might get out of sync with the current version of the model in the public branch after set as the latest functionality in the **History** dialog is used. Therefore, you will be asked to perform hidden package synchronization.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To select a synchronization mode

1. On the main menu, click Options > Projects . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. In the list of option groups, select General and then select Collaboration in the list of options.
3. For the option Synchronization Mode , specify one of the following values:

- 
  - Automatic - to synchronize changes automatically on committing the project changes.
  - Manual (default) - to synchronize changes upon your decision.
  - None - do not perform any synchronization.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Selecting a synchronization mode to specify how changes in the main project are synchronized to a branch where a stripped model version is stored

1191527482

INLINE

You can <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Branching projects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[rename the branch]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> if you need to.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Publick_branch.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Hiding packages" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Project with it`s Public Project branch. dialog,</li><li>Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="62d3a7e1-7e28-4a87-813a-52539891a348"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The state of the used project is checked at the same time interval as checking for a new project version in the background, or by clicking the Project Usages Update button.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Hidden package changes  </h3><p>After a package or a used project is successfully hidden or unhidden, an icon with a lock on a package (or used project) is shown in a Difference viewer. </p><p>The icon with a lock on a package (or on a used project package) is also shown in:</p><ul><li>Upcoming Changes browser panel,</li><li>Local changes browser panel,</li><li>Commit dialog,</li><li>Content History panel,</li><li>Element History.</li></ul><h3>Model synchronization</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6fb36360-1e55-4302-8b93-cde414cf437b"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="ee458cf5-7fd9-42d7-ba66-5bccef748f14">You</ac:inline-comment-marker> must have the read-write permission</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> for the stripped model branch </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">to synchronize it. </span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>A stripped model is read-only. It can only be used for reuse in other projects, not for editing (it can only be modified by synchronizing it – read-write permission is required for the stripped model branch). It can only be used for reuse in other projects, not for editing. However, you can edit the main project. On committing, checking is performed to see if the changes affect <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="bff5d5da-d58a-46fc-a1c0-bfe139277cd6">hidden and public</ac:inline-comment-marker> parts of the model, and <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6d73fb86-dba4-4bc2-b359-51b0b40eda60">model</ac:inline-comment-marker> synchronization is initialized. In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Setting project options" /><ac:link-body><strong>Project Options </strong>dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>, you can define the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="5cdbe521-8cae-47be-a959-afdbfa78efcd">model</ac:inline-comment-marker> synchronization mode. The default mode is <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Manual</strong>; that is, <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="eeb6f786-be7e-472d-a58a-1bf81c497ccb">after</ac:inline-comment-marker> committing project changes, <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="ec088f7a-de5d-4145-b477-9562b3af9a0f">you will be notified</ac:inline-comment-marker> about local changes affecting a <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="4e001a51-fa61-4e5a-a17a-b1caa8b3ef17">public model</ac:inline-comment-marker> stored in a dedicated branch. You can then decide either to synchronize changes or not.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0f1f7b51-8f8d-4527-b1e7-67b4c991d290"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The server project with hidden packages (and public branches) might get out of sync with the current version of the model in the public branch after set as the latest functionality in the <strong>History</strong> dialog is used. Therefore, you will be asked to perform hidden package synchronization.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Hidden_packages_out_of_sync.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Hiding packages" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To select a synchronization mode</p><hr /><ol><li>On the main menu, click <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Projects</strong>. The <strong>Project Options </strong>dialog opens.</li><li>In the list of option groups, select <strong> General</strong> and then select <strong>Collaboration </strong>in the list of options.</li><li>For the option <strong>Synchronization Mode</strong>, specify one of the following values:</li></ol><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li><strong>Automatic</strong> - to synchronize changes automatically on committing the project changes.</li><li><strong>Manual </strong>(default) - to synchronize changes upon your decision.</li><li><strong>None</strong> - do not perform any synchronization.</li></ul></li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="synchronization_mode.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Hiding packages" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(112,112,112);">Selecting a synchronization mode to specify how changes in the main project are synchronized to a branch where a stripped model version is stored</span></h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="53f7957a-7599-4786-9733-1999a9bbb8d0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1191527482</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Branching projects" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Identifying Package Dependencies" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Committing changes to Teamwork Cloud" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169677095 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00566: Hierarchy Diagram Wizard

- page_id: `169677095`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169677095/Hierarchy+Diagram+Wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-01-08T10:19:09.233+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Model Visualizer
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This functionality is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions only.

The **Hierarchy Diagram Wizard** collects the largest hierarchies and allows each of them to be displayed either as separate diagrams or all in one diagram.

To create a diagram using the **Hierarchy Diagram Wizard**

1. Open the Hierarchy Diagram Wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the Diagrams menu, select Diagram Wizards > Hierarchy Diagram Wizard .
  - From the Analyze menu, select Model Visualizer . In the open Model Visualizer dialog, select to open the Hierarchy Diagram Wizard and click **Start** .
2. Follow the steps as directed by the wizard.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="33df9e1d-7457-4043-ac81-6afd9c3c69da"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This functionality is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions only.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The <strong>Hierarchy Diagram Wizard</strong> collects the largest hierarchies and allows each of them to be displayed either as separate diagrams or all in one diagram.</p><p> </p><p>To create a diagram using the <strong>Hierarchy Diagram Wizard</strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Hierarchy Diagram Wizard</strong><strong> </strong>by doing one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the <strong>Diagrams</strong> menu, select <strong>Diagram Wizards</strong> &gt; <strong>Hierarchy Diagram Wizard</strong>.</li><li>From the <strong>Analyze</strong> menu, select <strong>Model Visualizer</strong>. In the open Model Visualizer dialog, select to open the <strong>Hierarchy Diagram Wizard</strong> and click <strong><strong>Start</strong></strong>.</li></ul></li><li>Follow the steps as directed by the wizard.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169674879 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00568: How aspects match with parts and ports

- page_id: `169674879`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674879/How+aspects+match+with+parts+and+ports
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T11:59:56.250+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Diagram aspects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The diagram aspects mechanism identifies whether or not the aspect name and name of the stereotypes applied for the parts and ports (or their types) match. The stereotype name must contain a matching aspect name. For example, a diagram aspect name is *Electrical,*which matches a port stereotype name W*Electrical.* When these conditions are met, you can display only the *Electrical* part of your model in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Composite Structure diagram' space='MD2024xR1']. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports according to aspects' space='MD2024xR1']>]]>

If the diagram aspect name doesn't match the name of the part/port stereotype or its type, you can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MD2024xR1'] with the same name as the aspect's and [apply it for parts and ports](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Stereotype#Stereotype-Applyingastereotype). Or you can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating custom aspects' space='MD2024xR1'].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Matching the diagram aspect *Electrical* with the port stereotype name *Electrical*.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The diagram aspects mechanism identifies whether or not the aspect name and name of the stereotypes applied for the parts and ports (or their types) match. The stereotype name must contain a matching aspect name. For example, a diagram aspect name is <em>Electrical, </em>which matches a port stereotype name W<em>Electrical.</em> When these conditions are met, you can display only the <em>Electrical</em> part of your model in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Composite Structure Diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports according to aspects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to display parts and port according to aspect >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4704f5b7-46ac-40ca-a191-e433fd556328"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the diagram aspect name doesn't match the name of the part/port stereotype or its type, you can <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Stereotype" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create a custom stereotype]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> with the same name as the aspect's and <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Stereotype#Stereotype-Applyingastereotype">apply it for parts and ports</a>. Or you can <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating custom aspects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create a custom diagram aspect]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="aspects_and_stereotype_match.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="How aspects match with parts and ports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Matching the diagram aspect <em>Electrical</em> with the port stereotype name <em>Electrical</em>.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169678739 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00569: How modeling tool adds its elements to velocity context

- page_id: `169678739`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678739/How+modeling+tool+adds+its+elements+to+velocity+context
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-01T14:29:48.021+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Template Creation Tutorial > Linking report templates to the modeling tool
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1358598954

1358598955

1358598955

In Velocity, whenever you want to reference to a Java object you need to manually add the object to the Velocity template and name it. In Report Wizard, however, this process is done automatically. What you need to understand is how it is done as it will help you to call the modeling tool elements.

All elements in the modeling tool such as Class, Use Case, and Activity are the sub-classes of elements. During report generation, the report engine will get all the elements from a project within a selected scope that you have defined in Report Wizard, and then classify and add them to a list of the same type. The list will then be added to the Velocity Context with its type as its name. For instance, in a project where there are two types of Element, Class, and Use Case, all the elements that are identified as Classes will be kept on the same list and the list will be added to the Velocity Context and labeled as "Class" during report generation. This process also applies to the Use Case elements. To reference to a specific Class element, first you will have to make a reference to **$Class** (list of Class elements), and then iterate through the list to find that particular Class element by using the **#foreach**, **#forrow**, **#forcol**, or **#forpage** directive, followed by the **#if** directive to search for the name of the class.

This section will use part of the "*Inventory Control System.mdzip*" file in *<install.root>/samples/casestudies* as an example to show you how to reference elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Use Case View.

You need to know the elements inside the "Use Case View" package before using it, see figure above. To do this, follow the instructions in the previous section, *[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Your First Template' space='MD2024xR1']*.

The "Use Case View" package contains three element types: (i) Actor, (ii) UseCase, and (iii) Diagram that you can call in a template by typing **"$Actor**," **"$UseCase**," and **"$Diagram**" respectively.

In this example you will create a report that will print the names of the Actors, UseCases, and diagram image.

1358598953

**Related pages**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fd5026eb-b266-4e77-84e4-80d96f9f56b1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1358598954</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8e4490c1-171d-45cf-9578-7d18d05418f5"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1358598955</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="247ac079-b3f9-47c7-b124-207b4c96b153"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1358598955</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>In Velocity, whenever you want to reference to a Java object you need to manually add the object to the Velocity template and name it. In Report Wizard, however, this process is done automatically. What you need to understand is how it is done as it will help you to call the modeling tool elements.</p><p>All elements in the modeling tool such as Class, Use Case, and Activity are the sub-classes of elements. During report generation, the report engine will get all the elements from a project within a selected scope that you have defined in Report Wizard, and then classify and add them to a list of the same type. The list will then be added to the Velocity Context with its type as its name. For instance, in a project where there are two types of Element, Class, and Use Case, all the elements that are identified as Classes will be kept on the same list and the list will be added to the Velocity Context and labeled as &quot;Class&quot; during report generation. This process also applies to the Use Case elements. To reference to a specific Class element, first you will have to make a reference to <strong>$Class</strong> (list of Class elements), and then iterate through the list to find that particular Class element by using the <strong>#foreach</strong>, <strong>#forrow</strong>, <strong>#forcol</strong>, or <strong>#forpage</strong> directive, followed by the <strong>#if</strong> directive to search for the name of the class.</p><p>This section will use part of the &quot;<em>Inventory Control System.mdzip</em>&quot; file in <em>&lt;install.root&gt;/samples/casestudies</em> as an example to show you how to reference elements.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Use Case and Actor" ac:alt="Use Case and Actor"><ri:attachment ri:filename="worddavb053991ffb02adecec8c94bef76403bd.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="How modeling tool adds its elements to velocity context" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;">Use Case View.</h6><p>You need to know the elements inside the &quot;Use Case View&quot; package before using it, see figure above. To do this, follow the instructions in the previous section, <em><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating Your First Template" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating your first template]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></em>.</p><p>The &quot;Use Case View&quot; package contains three element types: (i) Actor, (ii) UseCase, and (iii) Diagram that you can call in a template by typing <strong>&quot;$Actor</strong>,&quot; <strong>&quot;$UseCase</strong>,&quot; and <strong>&quot;$Diagram</strong>&quot; respectively.</p><p>In this example you will create a report that will print the names of the Actors, UseCases, and diagram image.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5863349b-5f1c-4533-8b27-111ca2bfbf72"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1358598953</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="8804a6f4-8982-4de8-b9e7-353a297adadb" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673713 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00570: How to avoid triggering element recovery

- page_id: `169673713`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673713/How+to+avoid+triggering+element+recovery
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-04-05T14:23:57.628+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Project partitioning > Maintaining decomposed model integrity > Managing unresolved references
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following recommendations will help you avoid triggering element recovery in your project:

1. Avoid unexpected modifications of shared elements, such as removing a shared element or moving the element to a non-shared location, etc.
2. Configure the modeling tool to prompt you for confirmation before proceeding with a potentially dangerous action, such as attempting to stop sharing a package, removing the content of a shared package, or moving elements from one module to another, when both modules are used in the read-write mode (shown in the following figure).
3. Select the relevant level of model integrity and project composition inspection.
4. Manage unresolved references as soon as possible, as they might produce other unresolved references. Remember that the fewer recovered elements your model has, the more valid it is.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Examples of messages prompting to confirm potentially unsafe actions

To define a set of actions for modeling supervision to prevent recovered elements creation

1. Select Options > Projects to open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space='MD2024xR1']**Project Options** dialog .
2. In the option group list, select the General > Validation .
3. In the Validation options list under the Composition Inspection , set the Detect Illegal Model References in Shared Projects property value to true .
4. Click OK when you are done.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

inspection

To select the relevant level of project composition inspection

1. Select Options > Projects to open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space='MD2024xR1']**Project Options** dialog .
2. In the option group list, select the General > Validation .
3. In the Validation options list under the Composition Inspection , specify the Composition inspection option value:
  - Select Standard if you need the active validation to search for issues only in the project, but not in the projects it uses.
  - Select Advanced if you need the active validation to search for issues in the project as well as in the projects it uses.
4. Click OK when you are done.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The following recommendations will help you avoid triggering element recovery in your project:</p><ol><li>Avoid unexpected modifications of shared elements, such as removing a shared element or moving the element to a non-shared location, etc.</li><li>Configure the modeling tool to prompt you for confirmation before proceeding with a potentially dangerous action, such as attempting to stop sharing a package, removing the content of a shared package, or moving elements from one module to another, when both modules are used in the read-write mode (shown in the following figure).</li><li>Select the relevant level of model integrity and project composition inspection.</li><li>Manage unresolved references as soon as possible, as they might produce other unresolved references. Remember that the fewer recovered elements your model has, the more valid it is.</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="avoid_recovery.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="How to avoid triggering element recovery" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Examples of messages prompting to confirm potentially unsafe actions</h6><p>To define a set of actions for modeling supervision to prevent recovered elements creation</p><hr /><ol><li>Select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Projects</strong> to open the<strong> </strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Setting project options" /><ac:link-body><strong>Project Options</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>In the option group list, select the <strong>General</strong> &gt; <strong>Validation</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Validation </strong>options list under the <strong> Composition Inspection</strong>, set the <strong>Detect Illegal Model References in Shared Projects </strong>property value to <em>true</em>.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="detect_illegal_model_references_in_shared_projects.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="How to avoid triggering element recovery" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2eb30e2f-6a0b-40da-97d1-2126e8b29b05"><ac:parameter ac:name="">inspection</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p>To select the relevant level of project composition inspection</p><hr /><ol><li>Select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Projects</strong> to open the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Setting project options" /><ac:link-body><strong>Project Options</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li style="text-align: left;">In the option group list, select the <strong>General</strong> &gt; <strong>Validation</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Validation </strong>options list under the <strong>Composition Inspection</strong>, specify the <strong>Composition inspection</strong> option value:<ul><li>Select <strong>Standard</strong> if you need the active validation to search for issues <em>only</em> in the project, but not in the projects it uses.</li><li>Select <strong>Advanced</strong> if you need the active validation to search for issues in the project as well as in the projects it uses.</li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675916 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00571: HTML editor

- page_id: `169675916`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675916/HTML+editor
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-07-28T17:20:20.123+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

630043998

630044000

630043999

You can easily add or edit HTML text using the HTML editor. It also allows you to preserve the text format when copying formatted text.

To set the note, text box, or separator text as HTML

Do one of the following:

- Select a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Note' space='MD2024xR1'] / [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Text Box' space='MD2024xR1'] shape and click the HTML button when Switch To HTML Text appears on the lower-left corner of the shape. [ATTACHMENT filename='switch_text_to_html.png']

- Draw the note/text box, using the Note(HTML text) or Text Box(HTML text) buttons on the diagram palette.

Click the text area on the selected shape or start typing to open the HTML editor toolbar. The buttons on the HTML editor toolbar have the same functions as a normal editing toolbar.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']HTML editor toolbar

You can write HTML text in various dialogs, but you must turn on the HTML mode first.

To turn on the HTML mode in a dialog

- Simply click to select the HTML check box. The following scenarios show the various ways of reaching the check box:
  - Element [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1'] :
    1. Open an element's specification window.
    2. Click the Documentation/Comments tab.
    3. Check HTML. Click Close . See the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### HTML editor toolbar in Documentation/Hyperlinks tab of element Specification window

- In the Model Browser:
  1. Click the element in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment Tree' space='MD2024xR1'] .
  2. Click the **Documentation** tab.
  3. Click **HTML**, then click anywhere on the diagram pane. See the figure below. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] HTML editor toolbar in Documentation panel on Model Browser
- Element Specification window:
  1. Open an element specification window.
  2. Click the Tags property group, when editing a tagged value.
  3. Click HTML . Then click Close . See the figure below. [ATTACHMENT filename='customer_user_is_added.png'] HTML editor toolbar in Tags tab of element Specification window when editing tag value
- In the **To Do** field:
  1. Open an element specification window.
  2. In the dialog, click the**To Do** field to edit it.
  3. Click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button to open the **To Do** text dialog.
  4. In the **To Do** text dialog, click **HTML**. Edit the text as you wish, then click **OK** and click **Close**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']HTML editor toolbar in To Do dialog

You can also use the HTML editor toolbar when editing a tagged value directly on the element shape.

To use the HTML editor toolbar for editing a tagged value

1. Click the HTML button on the element shape to switch to the HTML mode.
2. On the element shape, click the tagged value. The HTML editor toolbar opens for editing the tagged value.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

630043997

**Related Pages:**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="62ffa767-a455-488f-9218-cba5d604408d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">630043998</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="51b4e83a-e544-475b-9fc1-6c25f288282d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">630044000</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cc9463d7-5438-44cd-8790-b781d84d9d03"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">630043999</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can easily add or edit HTML text using the HTML editor. It also allows you to preserve the text format when copying formatted text.</p><p><br /></p><p>To set the note, text box, or separator text as HTML</p><hr /><p>Do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Select a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Note" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[note]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>/<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Text Box" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[text box]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> shape and click the <strong>HTML</strong> button when <strong>Switch To HTML Text </strong>appears on the lower-left corner of the shape.<br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="switch_text_to_html.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="HTML editor" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><ul><li>Draw the note/text box, using the <strong>Note(HTML text)</strong> or <strong>Text Box(HTML text)</strong> buttons on the diagram palette.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>Click the text area on the selected shape or start typing to open the HTML editor toolbar. The buttons on the HTML editor toolbar have the same functions as a normal editing toolbar. </p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="HTML editor toolbar.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="HTML editor" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><span style="color: rgb(112,112,112);font-size: 12.0px;">HTML editor toolbar</span></h6><p>You can write HTML text in various dialogs, but you must turn on the HTML mode first.</p><p><br /></p><p>To turn on the HTML mode in a dialog</p><hr /><ul><li>Simply click to select the <strong>HTML</strong> check box. The following scenarios show the various ways of reaching the check box: <br /><ul><li>Element <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>:<ol><li>Open an element's specification window.</li><li>Click the <strong>Documentation/Comments</strong> tab.</li><li>Check HTML. Click <strong>Close</strong>. See the figure below. </li></ol></li></ul></li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="html_docummentation_comments.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="HTML editor" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">HTML editor toolbar in Documentation/Hyperlinks tab of element Specification window </h6><p><br /></p><ul><li>In the Model Browser: <ol><li>Click the element in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Containment Tree" /></ac:link>.</li><li><p style="text-align: left;">Click the <strong>Documentation</strong> tab.</p></li><li><p style="text-align: left;">Click <strong>HTML</strong>, then click anywhere on the diagram pane. See the figure below.</p><h6><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="HTML editor toolbar in Documentation panel.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="HTML editor" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h6><h6 style="text-align: center;">HTML editor toolbar in Documentation panel on Model Browser </h6></li></ol></li><li>Element Specification window: <ol><li>Open an element specification window.</li><li>Click the <strong>Tags</strong> property group, when editing a tagged value.</li><li>Click <strong>HTML</strong>. Then click <strong>Close</strong>. See the figure below.<br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="customer_user_is_added.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="HTML editor" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><h6 style="text-align: center;">HTML editor toolbar in Tags tab of element Specification window when editing tag value</h6></li></ol></li><li><span>In the <strong>To Do</strong> field:</span><ol><li><span>Open an element specification window.</span></li><li><span>In the dialog, click the<strong> To Do</strong> field to edit it. </span></li><li><span>Click the <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="16"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Screen Shot 2017-08-26 at 11.56.26 AM.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="HTML editor" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button to open the <strong>To Do</strong> text dialog.</span></li><li><p>In the <strong>To Do</strong> text dialog, click <strong>HTML</strong>. Edit the text as you wish, then click <strong>OK</strong> and click <strong>Close</strong>.</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="specification.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="HTML editor" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><span style="color: rgb(94,108,132);">HTML editor toolbar in To Do dialog </span></h6></li></ol></li></ul><p>You can also use the HTML editor toolbar when editing a tagged value directly on the element shape.</p><p><br />To use the HTML editor toolbar for editing a tagged value</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the <strong>HTML</strong> button on the element shape to switch to the HTML mode.</li><li>On the element shape, click the tagged value. The HTML editor toolbar opens for editing the tagged value.</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="requirement_shape.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="HTML editor" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dea1aaa0-32bf-4270-be2d-8a2f472031f9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">630043997</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related Pages:</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="ca856143-1191-4029-83e7-6566092d07e2" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675835 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00572: Hyperlinks in texts

- page_id: `169675835`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675835/Hyperlinks+in+texts
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:17.705+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Manage Navigation > Defining hyperlinks
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Descriptions of boxes and tabs

760290566

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Descriptions of boxes and tabs

760290568

1348070756

**On this page**

32

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Descriptions of boxes and tabs

760290567

#### Introduction

You can link text directly to internal or external resources by adding hyperlinks. This enables you to open a document, wizard, diagram, or other [hyperlink type](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Edit+Hyperlink+dialog#EditHyperlinkdialog-Hyperlinktypes) by clicking the hyperlink.

#### Places to add hyperlink in text

You can add a hyperlink in the following places:

- In all elements of the text format (e.g. Note, Anchor, Comment).
- In all element property values of the text format (e.g. **To Do**, **Documentation**, **Body**, **Body** **and Language**, **Text**,**Min**,**Max**). You can insert a hyperlink only for LiteralString and OpaqueExpression [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Value Specification' space='MD2024xR1'] (e.g **Default Value** property, **Value** property, and **Argument** property values).
- In all element [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1'] > Documentation/Hyperlinks property groups.

#### Adding hyperlinks in text

Before adding a hyperlink, select the text format, either HTML or Plain text. If you select:

- HTML text - you can add any type of hyperlink . >]]>[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Hyperlinks in texts' space='MD2024xR1']
- Plain text - you can add a hyperlink only to an element/symbol. >]]>[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Hyperlinks in texts' space='MD2024xR1']

##### all typesAdding a hyperlink in text to any hyperlink type

To add a hyperlink in text to any [hyperlink type](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Edit+Hyperlink+dialog#EditHyperlinkdialog-Hyperlinktypes)

1. Select a place to add a hyperlink. [All places to add hyperlinks in text >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Hyperlinks+in+texts#Hyperlinksintexts-Placestoinserthyperlinkintext)
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] or press Ctrl+K.
3. In the **Edit Hyperlink** dialog , select the tab according to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Edit Hyperlink dialog' space='MD2024xR1'] .
4. Specify the boxes and options in the selected tab. Descriptions of boxes and tabsPlease see the following for more in-depth information about the boxes and options on each tab: 
 - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Address tab' space='MD2024xR1']**Address** tab. 
 - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='File tab' space='MD2024xR1']**File** tab. 
 - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Element Symbol tab' space='MD2024xR1']**Element/Symbol** tab. 
 - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Linking model elements to OSLC resources' space='CDH2024xR1']**OSLC Link** tab.
5. Click **OK**.The hyperlink added in text becomes underlined and blue. For example, the figure below shows where the hyperlink on the text "Car" will open the Specification window of the *Car* Class. The hyperlinked text will be represented with icon and will be automatically updated after changes. [IMAGE alt='' src='']The Element/Symbol tab in the Edit Hyperlink dialog where the text "Car" refers to the Car Class from the model. It is represented with an icon and will be automatically updated after changes.

##### elementAdding hyperlink in text to element/symbol

Two additional ways to add a hyperlink in text to an element/symbol include:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Hyperlinks in texts' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Hyperlinks in texts' space='MD2024xR1']

****

###### **drag**Adding hyperlink to element by dragging element on Note or Text box shape

This method of adding hyperlinks in text is useful only when inserting a hyperlink to element on Note or Text box shapes.

To add a hyperlink to an element by dragging an element on a Note or Text box shape

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='MD2024xR1'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1'] , select one or more elements.
2. Drag the selected elements onto the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Note' space='MD2024xR1'] or the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Text Box' space='MD2024xR1'] shapes on the diagram pane. 
The hyperlink to the element Specification window is created on the Note or Text Box. The text become underlined and blue. The **Update Mode** and **Text to display** values are selected according default settings. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Default settings of hyperlinks to model elements' space='MD2024xR1']How to change the default settings of hyperlinks to model elements >>

###### **guards**Adding hyperlinks to elements on Guard expressions, Change Event expressions, and State Invariant

This method of adding hyperlinks in text is useful when inserting hyperlinks to properties on:

- A Transition Guard in a State Machine diagram.
- A Flow Guard in an Activity diagram.
- A State Invariant in a Sequence diagram.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Assigning Event Type' space='MD2024xR1']

To add a hyperlink on a guard expression, State Invariant, and Change Event

1. Select one of the following:
  - guard expression on Transition in State Machine diagram.
  - guard expression on Flow in Activity diagram.
  - State Invariant in Sequence diagram.
  - Change Event expression in State Machine diagram.
2. Press Ctrl+Space or Ctrl+****Backspace. If you press Ctrl+K, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Edit Hyperlink dialog_old' space='MD2024xR1']**Edit Hyperlink** dialog opens and you can insert a hyperlink to an appropriate property. Makesure the selected property is correct.
3. Select the property from the list. The list provides all parameters, structural members of behavior, and structural members of the contextual classifier of behavior. The hyperlinked property is added according to the default setting of the **Update Mode** and **Text to display** list boxes. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Default settings of hyperlinks to model elements' space='MD2024xR1']How to change the default settings of hyperlinks to model elements >> For example, in the figure below, the hyperlink to the *currentSpeed* Property is added on the guard expression. It is updated after renaming the *currentSpeed* Property to *speed*.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The hyperlink to the currentSpeed Property is added on the guard expression. It is updated after renaming the currentSpeed Property to speed in the Model Browser.

760290565

**Related pages**

3[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Defining hyperlinks' space='MD2024xR1']

- Linking model elements to OSLC resources
- Managing hyperlinks
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='HTML editor' space='MD2024xR1']

expression on Flow in Activity diagram.</li><li>State Invariant in Sequence diagram.</li><li>Change Event expression in State Machine diagram. </li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Press Ctrl+Space or Ctrl+<strong> </strong>Backspace.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="57e731a0-9fbf-4ba6-94c2-467137bc7b40"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you press Ctrl+K, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Edit Hyperlink dialog_old" /><ac:link-body><strong>Edit Hyperlink</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens and you can insert a hyperlink to an appropriate property. Make<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> sure the selected property is correct.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Select the property from the list.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e6f19df2-7086-4d4b-bd80-70b5a3f59e16"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The list provides all parameters, structural members of behavior, and structural members of the contextual classifier of behavior.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">The hyperlinked property is added according to the default setting of the <span class="current"><strong>Update Mode</strong> and <strong>Text to display</strong> list boxes</span>. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Default settings of hyperlinks to model elements" /><ac:link-body>How to change the default settings of <span class="current">hyperlinks to model elements</span> &gt;&gt;</ac:link-body></ac:link></p><p class="auto-cursor-target">For example, in the figure below, the hyperlink to the <em>currentSpeed</em> Property is added on the guard expression. It is updated after renaming the <em>currentSpeed</em> Property to <em>speed</em>.</p></li></ol><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="updated_hyperlink_on_guard_expression.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Hyperlinks in texts" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The hyperlink to the currentSpeed Property is added on the guard expression. It is updated after renaming the currentSpeed Property to speed in the Model Browser.</h6><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="28233b6d-3d9a-4514-b139-ee3cb7619bda"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">760290565</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="36cf85bd-3f90-4b7a-b409-d391719f1434"><ac:parameter ac:name="depth">3</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Defining hyperlinks" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Linking+model+elements+to+OSLC+resources" class="current">Linking model elements to OSLC resources</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Managing+hyperlinks">Managing hyperlinks</a></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="HTML editor" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675840 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00573: Hyperlinks on elements

- page_id: `169675840`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675840/Hyperlinks+on+elements
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:18.462+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Manage Navigation > Defining hyperlinks
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

756999583

756999585

756999584

#### Introduction

Hyperlinked elements or diagrams allow you to quickly navigate through the model, to other resources, or between them. If an element or diagram refers to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Hyperlinks on elements' space='MD2024xR1'], you can access that content by double clicking an element or an entire diagram. You can add all [types of hyperlinks](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Edit+Hyperlink+dialog#EditHyperlinkdialog-Hyperlinktypes) on elements and diagrams.

#### Adding a hyperlink on an element or entire diagram

To add a hyperlink on an element or entire diagram

1. Select the element or diagram.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Click the smart manipulator [IMAGE alt='' src='']**Go To** > **Manage Navigation**.
  - From the shortcut menu, choose **Go To**> **Manage Navigation**.
3. The **Specification** window opens. Choose the **Navigation/Hyperlinks** property group, and click the **Add** button.
4. In the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Edit Hyperlink dialog' space='MD2024xR1']**Edit Hyperlink** dialog, select a tab according to the [hyperlink type](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Edit+Hyperlink+dialog#EditHyperlinkdialog-Hyperlinktypes) you want to insert.
5. Specify the boxes and options according to the selected tab. The meaning of boxes and options of each tab is described in the following pages: 
 - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Address tab' space='MD2024xR1']**Address** tab 
 - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='File tab' space='MD2024xR1']**File** tab 
 - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Element Symbol tab' space='MD2024xR1']**Element/Symbol** tab 
 - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Linking model elements to OSLC resources' space='CDH2024xR1']**OSLC Link** tabThe **Text to display** box does not exist in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Edit Hyperlink dialog' space='MD2024xR1']**Edit Hyperlink** dialog.
6. Select the **Active** check box to activate the hyperlink. Only one hyperlink can be active. By default the last added hyperlink is active. You can also:
  - Click the **Open** button, to open the content of the hyperlink, e.g., a webpage or another external resource, a file on your filesystem, or an element/symbol.
  - Click the **Edit** button, to edit an existing hyperlink in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Edit Hyperlink dialog' space='MD2024xR1']**Edit Hyperlink** dialog.
  - Click the **Add** button, to add a new hyperlink in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Edit Hyperlink dialog' space='MD2024xR1']**Edit Hyperlink** dialog.
  - Click the **Remove** button, to delete the hyperlink. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
7. Click **OK**. 
The hyperlink is added on the selected element or diagram. In the Model Browser, you will see the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Hyperlinks/Go To icon on the element. On the diagram pane, on the symbol, you will see the icon representing the subject the hyperlink is added to. You can instantly open a hyperlink by double clicking an element or diagram.

756999582

**Related pages**

**3[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Defining hyperlinks' space='MD2024xR1']**

- Linking model elements to OSLC resources
- Managing hyperlinks

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169676846 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00574: Identifying Package Dependencies

- page_id: `169676846`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169676846/Identifying+Package+Dependencies
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2016-11-11T20:18:45.269+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In a model, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package' space='MD2024xR1'] can be associated with other packages or model elements in a project scope or outside the project scope. In other words, packages can have relations with external elements in used projects, shared libraries, profiles, etc. An element depends on the used project when at least one of its metaproperties reference elements from at least one share of that used project. In this case, the element depends on that used project.

To avoid any violations on editing a package, especially on removing or exporting a package to another project, it is always good to know all of the package dependencies. Dependency analysis allows users to detect cyclic dependencies that impede reusing the package in the current or other projects because it can cause an infinite recursion. This analysis manages the impact of changes to other related elements.

In the modeling tool, the Dependency Checker allows you to analyze package dependencies in the whole project scope or analyze dependencies between the selected package/model and shared packages. The dependency severity level status shows how a package depends on a particular element. There are three severity levels:

- Info . The lowest severity.
- Warning . Conflicts that require your attention are displayed.
- Error . The highest severity.

#### NOTE: Severity levels

Severity levels

While checking dependencies, conflicts of the selectedseverity (and higher) are displayed in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package Dependencies panel' space='MD2024xR1']**Package Dependencies** panel.

In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space='MD2024xR1']**Project Options** dialog, you can define the default values for checking dependencies. You can specify the following:

- The Check for Cyclic Dependencies among Used Projects option. Click to select true if you want the cyclic dependencies to be checked while exporting or sharing the packages.
- The Dependency Checker Severity Level option. Specify the severity level. The dependencies of the specified severity (and higher) will be displayed.
- The Ignore Standard/System Profiles option. Click to select true if you want to exclude the standard/system profiles from checking for cyclic dependencies in used projects.

Each time you start the dependency checking, you can change the default settings.

In the following procedures, learn how to work with the Dependency Checker:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Identifying Package Dependencies' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Identifying Package Dependencies' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Identifying Package Dependencies' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Identifying Package Dependencies' space='MD2024xR1']

setDefaultTo set default values for the Dependency Checker

1. On the main menu, click Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. Expand the General option in the option group list.
3. Click the **Dependency Checker** option. The list of options opens.
4. In the Dependency Checker options list, set the desired values and click **OK** when you are done.

#### NOTE: Description of an option

Description of an option

Each option has a short description. Click the option to see the description in the description area of the dialog.

analyzeWholeTo analyze package dependencies of the whole project or between the selected package/model and shared packages

1. Choose one of the following:
  - To analyze package dependencies of the whole project, on the main menu, click **Analyze** > **Dependency Checker**.
  - To analyze dependencies between the selected package/model and shared packages, right-click the package or model in the Model Browser or on the diagram pane, point to **Tools**and then click**Dependency Checker**.
2. The Dependency Checker dialog opens. Select the Minimal Severity from the list, check ignore or not standard and system profiles while checking and click OK to execute the dependency checker. If some dependencies are found, they are displayed in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package Dependencies panel' space='MD2024xR1']**Package Dependencies** panel .

analyzeExportTo identify package dependencies while exporting packages

1. From the package/model shortcut menu, choose Project Usages > Export Packages to New Project . The Export Packages to New Project dialog appears.
2. Select the packages you want to export. For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Exporting packages to new projects' space='MD2024xR1'] .
3. Click OK .
4. In the question dialog, click **Yes**. NotesDependencies between the selected package/model and shared packages will be analyzed and shown in the opened[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package Dependencies panel' space='MD2024xR1']**Package Dependency** panel.If you want to discover *cyclic dependencies*, select the **Check for cyclic dependencies among used projects** check box. This enables for the dependencies which have **Error** and **Warning** severity levels to be displayed.

analyzeSharingTo identify package dependencies while sharing packages

1. From the package/model shortcut menu, choose Project Usages > Share Packages . The Shared Packages dialog appears.
2. Select the packages you want to share. For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sharing project data' space='MD2024xR1'].
3. Click OK .
4. In the question dialog, click **Yes**. NotesDependencies between the selected package/model and shared packages will be analyzed and shown in the opened [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package Dependencies panel' space='MD2024xR1']**Package Dependency** panel.If you want to discover *cyclic dependencies*, select the **Check for cyclic dependencies among used projects** check box. This enables for the dependencies which have**Error**and**Warning**severity levels to be displayed.

#### INFO: Cyclic dependency

Cyclic dependency

If there is a chain of dependencies such that A → M(1), M(1) → M(2), M(2) → M(3), ..., M(X) → M(A), where:

- A is an element from the used project M(A)
- M(1..X) are other used projects
- A → M(x) is element A dependency on used project M(x)
- M(y) → M(x) is a dependency of at least one element in used project M(y) on used project M(x),

then this chain is called a cyclic dependency and every atomic dependency in this chain is considered as part of cyclic dependency.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The dependencies of the specified severity (and higher) will be displayed.</li><li>The <strong>Ignore Standard/System Profiles</strong> option. Click to select <em>true</em> if you want to exclude the standard/system profiles from checking for cyclic dependencies in used projects.</li></ul><p>Each time you start the dependency checking, you can change the default settings.</p><p>In the following procedures, learn how to work with the Dependency Checker:</p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="setDefault"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to set default values for the Dependency Checker]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Identifying Package Dependencies" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="analyzeWhole"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to analyze package dependencies of the whole project or between the selected package/model and shared packages]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Identifying Package Dependencies" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="analyzeExport"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to analyze package dependencies while exporting packages]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Identifying Package Dependencies" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="analyzeSharing"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to analyze package dependencies while sharing packages]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Identifying Package Dependencies" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="60e04eb5-bf0e-481b-80d4-d0f607991a55"><ac:parameter ac:name="">setDefault</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To set default values for the Dependency Checker</p><hr /><ol><li>On the main menu, click <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>. The <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog opens.</li><li><span>Expand the </span><strong>General</strong><span> option in the option group list.</span></li><li><span><span>Click the <strong>Dependency Checker</strong> option. The list of options opens.</span></span></li><li><span><span>In the Dependency Checker options list, set the desired values and click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done. </span></span><br /><span><span><br /></span></span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6331d8e7-b12b-4819-be61-16929d96f31a"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Description of an option</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Each option has a short description. Click the option to see the description in the description area of the dialog.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><br /></span></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f0c22bfb-bfa0-4e26-8fff-ea26299092c6"><ac:parameter ac:name="">analyzeWhole</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To analyze package dependencies of the whole project or between the selected package/model and shared packages</p><hr /><ol><li>Choose one of the following:<ul><li><p>To analyze package dependencies of the whole project, on the main menu, click <strong>Analyze</strong> &gt; <strong>Dependency Checker</strong>.</p></li><li><p>To analyze dependencies between the selected package/model and shared packages, right-click the package or model in the Model Browser or on the diagram pane, point to <strong>Tools </strong>and then click<strong> Dependency Checker</strong>.</p></li></ul></li><li>The <strong>Dependency Checker</strong> dialog opens. Select the <strong>Minimal Severity</strong> from the list, check ignore or not standard and system profiles while checking and click <strong>OK </strong>to execute the dependency checker. If some dependencies are found, they are displayed in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Package Dependencies panel" /><ac:link-body><strong>Package Dependencies</strong> panel</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li></ol><h4><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></h4><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a6fbddf9-c38f-4e51-b775-d1375adca93e"><ac:parameter ac:name="">analyzeExport</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To identify package dependencies while exporting packages</span></p><hr /><ol><li>From the package/model shortcut menu, choose <strong>Project Usages</strong> &gt; <strong>Export Packages to New Project</strong>. The <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Export Packages to New Project</strong> dialog appears.</li><li>Select the packages you want to export. For more information, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Exporting packages to new projects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Exporting packages to new projects]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li><p>In the question dialog, click <strong>Yes</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4ed83e0a-d035-4270-bf19-7144a508c3e6"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Notes</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Dependencies between the selected package/model and shared packages will be analyzed and shown in the opened<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Package Dependencies panel" /><ac:link-body> <strong>Package Dependency</strong> panel</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>If you want to discover <em>cyclic dependencies</em>, select the <strong>Check for cyclic dependencies among used projects</strong> check box. This enables for the dependencies which have <strong>Error</strong> and <strong>Warning</strong> severity levels to be displayed.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5f07b3ff-006b-4bd1-9311-449cc930d658"><ac:parameter ac:name="">analyzeSharing</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To identify package dependencies while sharing packages</span></p><hr /><ol><li>From the package/model shortcut menu, choose <strong>Project Usages</strong> &gt; <strong>Share Packages</strong>. The <strong>Shared Packages</strong> dialog appears. </li><li><p>Select the packages you want to share. For more information, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Sharing project data" /></ac:link>.</p></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li><p>In the question dialog, click <strong>Yes</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3dbff1aa-82b9-49bf-8f0f-67fd53c9331d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Notes</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Dependencies between the selected package/model and shared packages will be analyzed and shown in the opened <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Package Dependencies panel" /><ac:link-body><strong>Package Dependency</strong> panel</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>If you want to discover <em>cyclic dependencies</em>, select the <strong>Check for cyclic dependencies among used projects</strong> check box. <span>This enables for the dependencies which have </span><strong>Error</strong><span> and </span><strong>Warning</strong><span> severity levels to be displayed.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="36b8cd71-2b36-479a-aa43-86de22bc15ed"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Cyclic dependency</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If there is a chain of dependencies such that A → M(1), M(1) → M(2), M(2) → M(3), ..., M(X) → M(A), where:</p><ul><li>A is an element from the used project M(A)</li><li>M(1..X) are other used projects</li><li>A → M(x) is element A dependency on used project M(x)</li><li>M(y) → M(x) is a dependency of at least one element in used project M(y) on used project M(x),</li></ul><p>then this chain is called a cyclic dependency and every atomic dependency in this chain is considered as part of cyclic dependency.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f8f1e285-e27f-4a7e-a0fa-517801cea20e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p> <ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="adb780d4-8338-4982-a517-dcadef4fb11e" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673343 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00575: Image Library tab

- page_id: `169673343`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673343/Image+Library+tab
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-22T15:28:45.298+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Getting started > Understanding the user interface
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### EXCERPT: Image formats

INLINE

The**Image Library** tab displays images grouped in logical sets. You can use a bundled image collection or add your own set of images.

To open the **Image Library** tab

- From the Window menu, select Image Library. The Image Library tab opens on the right side of the window.

To add your own set of images into **Image Library**, do either

- Compress a folder (.zip) with a set of desired images and place it in the modeling tool's installationdirectory: *\data\imagelibraries\commonsymbols*. Image formatsYou can add images of the following formats: .gif, .jpg, .jpeg, .svg, .png, .wmf.
- Create an Image Library plugin. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Writing plugins' space='DEVG2024xR1'] page.

Using the **Image Library** tab, you can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying image' space=''].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The **Image Library** tab consist of three components:

##### Quick filter

You can search for images in the **Whole hierarchy** or **Recently used** tabs using one of the following way:

- Simple Search: type what you want to find in the hierarchy.
- Pattern Search: type * to find any image. For example, type "*Bank" if you want to find "Bank" somewhere in the hierarchy.

##### Image Library tree

You can drag an image from the **Image Library** tree directly:

- On the symbol (image is applied for symbol).
- To the diagram pane (image shape is created).

##### Image Library Preview area

You can preview the image before applying it on the symbol in the **Image Library** Preview area.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675551 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00576: Image Shape

- page_id: `169675551`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675551/Image+Shape
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-27T17:07:13.543+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Auxiliary diagram symbols
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

490233696

490233698

490233697

The image shape provides a simple and quick way to insert a image into a diagram. Images may include logos, graphs, tables, or others. The default shape size after the insertion is the actual image size.

You can format image shape representation properties in the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MD2024xR1']**Symbol Properties**dialog.

To insert an image on the diagram pane

1. On the diagram palette, in the Common category, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='Screen Shot 2017-06-28 at 12.58.27 PM.png'] Image Shape button or press the I key.
2. Click on the diagram pane. The Open dialog opens.
3. Browse for an image in the *.gif, *.jpg, *.jpeg, *.svg, or *.png format and click Open .

490233695

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Auxiliary diagram symbols' space='MD2024xR1']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169678212 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00577: Image tool

- page_id: `169678212`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678212/Image+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-09-10T09:34:03.031+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Helper tools
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1267909359

1267909360

1267909360

$image provides a rich set of image manipulation methods that enable you to transform images during report generation. With **$image**, you can scale, rotate, split, and resize images, include external images, and export them for TXT and HTML templates.

**Syntax**

These manipulation methods can be broadly divided into seven categories:

1. Scaling images
2. Rotating images
3. Splitting images
4. Resizing fixed-pixel images
5. Resizing fixed-measurement images
6. Including external images
7. Exporting images for TXT and HTML templates
8. Limiting image size

Multiple image transformations will have cumulative effects. Desired sizes can be specified either as pixel counts or as measurements in inches, centimeters, or millimeters. While the parameters for resizing an image with a specific measurement (in, cm, mm, pt, or px) must be specified in a pair of quote marks, all other size parameters do not need to be specified in a pair of quote marks.

The **keepRatio** parameter takes either a true or false value; a true value will resize the image and keep the original image height/width ratio; a false value will only resize one axis, as set by the method name, resulting in a stretched image.

1267909358

**Related pages**

With <strong>$image</strong>, you can scale, rotate, split, and resize images, include external images, and export them for TXT and HTML templates.</p><p><strong>Syntax</strong></p><p>These manipulation methods can be broadly divided into seven categories:</p><ol><li>Scaling images</li><li>Rotating images</li><li>Splitting images</li><li>Resizing fixed-pixel images</li><li>Resizing fixed-measurement images</li><li>Including external images</li><li>Exporting images for TXT and HTML templates</li><li>Limiting image size</li></ol><p>Multiple image transformations will have cumulative effects. Desired sizes can be specified either as pixel counts or as measurements in inches, centimeters, or millimeters. While the parameters for resizing an image with a specific measurement (in, cm, mm, pt, or px) must be specified in a pair of quote marks, all other size parameters do not need to be specified in a pair of quote marks.</p><p>The <strong>keepRatio</strong> parameter takes either a true or false value; a true value will resize the image and keep the original image height/width ratio; a false value will only resize one axis, as set by the method name, resulting in a stretched image.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f0e833e7-926f-4a24-bee8-36421e59fb28"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1267909358</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="b0bb516e-26b0-4363-a404-fddac23bdb9f" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
