<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169676937 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00578: Implemented Operations

- page_id: `169676937`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169676937/Implemented+Operations
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-12-27T13:12:28.421+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Validation > UML validation rules > UML completeness constraints rules
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Abbreviation**

ImplOp

**Description**

This validation rule checks if all operations of realized interfaces are implemented.

**Severity**

warning

**Constrained Element**

Class

**Solvers**

- To fix this, make sure all operations of realized interfaces are implemented.

**Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The realized interface *Air Control*contains the *CoolAir*operation that is not implemented. The error is resolved by implementing the operation.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Abbreviation</strong></p><p>ImplOp</p><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>This validation rule checks if all operations of realized interfaces are implemented.</p><p><strong>Severity</strong></p><p>warning</p><p><strong>Constrained Element</strong></p><p>Class</p><p><strong>Solvers</strong></p><ul><li>To fix this, make sure all operations of realized interfaces are implemented.</li></ul><p><strong>Example</strong></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Implemented_Operations.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Implemented Operations" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The realized interface <em>Air Control </em>contains the <em>CoolAir </em>operation that is not implemented. The error is resolved by implementing the operation. </h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169676186 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00579: Implementing or Overriding Operations

- page_id: `169676186`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169676186/Implementing+or+Overriding+Operations
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:20.461+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Tools
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Shortcut Key

356006158

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Shortcut Key

356006160

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Shortcut Key

356006159

#### NOTE: Modeling Tools

Modeling Tools

This functionality is available in Standard, Professional, Architect and Enterprise editions only.

When you inherit Classes from the base Class that has abstract functions, you have to redefine them in the inherited Classes. The implement/override operations tool will help you generate operations that are defined as abstract in the base Class.

The**Implement/Override Operations**command can be invoked in two cases:

- When one classifier inherits operations from the base classifier ( [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Case study #1: Adding a rule to create a Generalization relationship on drag-and-drop' space='MD2024xR1'] ).
- When some classifiers implement an Interface ( [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Forward traceability - realization' space='MD2024xR1'] ).

To start the Implement/Override Operations****tool

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='DH Links panel shortcut menu and Sync Status icons' space='CDH2024xR1'] of the selected Class, select Tools > Code Engineering .
2. Select Implement/Override Operations.
3. The Select Operations to Implement/Override dialog box opens.

| Box | Function |
| --- | --- |
| All | Contains names of all operations defined within the selected class. |
| Selected | Contains the selected operations. |
| > | Moves the selected operation from the All list to the Selected list. |
| < | Moves selected operation from the Selected list to the All list. |
| >> | Moves all operations from the All list to the Selected list. |
| << | Moves all operations from the Selected to the All list. |
| OK | Generates operations that are in the Selected list. |
| Cancel | Exits the dialog box without any changes. |
| Help | Displays help. |

#### TIP: Shortcut Key

Shortcut Key

Double-click the item name and it will be moved to the opposite list.

356006156

**Related Pages**

- Tools
- Class
- Class diagram
- Generalization relationship
- Realization relationship

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675610 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00581: Import Maps migration from CSV Import Plugin

- page_id: `169675610`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675610/Import+Maps+migration+from+CSV+Import+Plugin
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:13.778+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Importing data from Excel or CSV files
- labels: ['import-map-migration', 'show-excel-csv-import-migration-message', 'csv-import-map-migration']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This page is for those who used the CSV Import Plugin to import data into the model. As of the 2021x version, this plugin is deprecated and import functionality is covered by [Excel/CSV Sync](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Sync+with+Excel+or+CSV+files) (import to table) and [Excel/CSV Import](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Importing+data+from+Excel+or+CSV+files) (import to model) features.

All Import Maps or Map Groups created with the CSV Import Plugin can be reused by migrating them. The procedure outlined below shows how to import those maps:

To import data from the Import Maps or Map Groups created with the CSV Import Plugin

1. Open the project containing the Import Maps/Map Groups created with the CSV Import Plugin.
2. In the top left corner of the modeling tool, click the File > Import From > Excel/CSV File > **Import Using Existing Map** or ******Import Using Existing Map Group****** .
3. In the **CSV Import Map Migration** message, select **Yes**. To perform the migration of Import Maps or Map Groups later, make sure the **Do not ask again** check box is clear.
4. In the **Select Import Map** or **Select Map Group**dialog, select Import Map or Map Group.
5. Click OK . The **Excel/CSV Import** dialog opens.
6. Select the file, check mapping options. How to >>
7. Click the **Import** button. 
The data is imported. A Map Group is migrated only if all Import Maps belonging to that Map Group are migrated at the same time. If at least one is not migrated, the whole Map Group will not be migrated.

If the **CSV Import Map Migration** message does not show again when trying to migrate the Import Map or Map Group, you can enable this option in the **Project Options** dialog. For this, follow the procedure below.

To enable the **CSV Import Map Migration** message

1. From the main menu, select Options > Project .
2. In the search box of the Project Options dialog, type " mig ".
3. On the right side of the Project Options dialog, set the Show Excel/CSV Import Migration Message option value to true to enable the functionality.
4. Click OK .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The procedure outlined below shows how to import those maps:</p><p><br /></p><p>To import data from the Import Maps or Map Groups created with the CSV Import Plugin</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the project containing the Import Maps/Map Groups created with the CSV Import Plugin.</li><li>In the top left corner of the modeling tool, click the <strong>File</strong> &gt; <strong>Import From</strong> &gt; <strong>Excel/CSV File &gt; <strong>Import Using Existing Map </strong></strong>or <strong><strong><strong><strong>Import Using Existing Map Group</strong></strong></strong></strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <strong>CSV Import Map Migration</strong> message, select <strong>Yes</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d34b7df2-dfb8-43dd-aa7f-895a7782a5b7"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To perform the migration of Import Maps or Map Groups later, make sure the <strong>Do not ask again</strong> check box is clear.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <strong>Select Import Map</strong> or <strong>Select Map Group </strong>dialog, select Import Map or Map Group. </p></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>. The <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog"><strong>Excel/CSV Import</strong> dialog</a> opens. </li><li>Select the file, check mapping options. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog#ExcelandCSVImportdialog-SelectExcel/CSVfilearea" rel="nofollow">How to &gt;&gt;</a></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click the <strong>Import</strong> button.<br />The data is imported.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="903c2d06-1978-4902-a2a1-54dc3404e22c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>A Map Group is migrated only if all Import Maps belonging to that Map Group are migrated at the same time. If at least one is not migrated, the whole Map Group will not be migrated.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /><br /></p></li></ol><p>If the <strong>CSV Import Map Migration</strong> message does not show again when trying to migrate the Import Map or Map Group, you can enable this option in the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog. For this, follow the procedure below.</p><p><br /></p><p>To enable the <strong>CSV Import Map Migration</strong> message</p><hr /><ol><li>From the main menu, select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>.</li><li>In the search box of the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog, type &quot;<em>mig</em>&quot;.</li><li>On the right side of the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog, set the <strong>Show Excel/CSV Import Migration Message </strong>option value to <em>true</em> to enable the functionality.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169678599 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00582: Import syntax

- page_id: `169678599`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678599/Import+syntax
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:28.000+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Import tool
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Information

715763235

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Information

715763237

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Information

715763236

To use the Import tool syntax

1. To declare Import tool in the template, type #import ('import', 'com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.ImportTool') in the document that you want to import its content to the report. The #import directive will load Import tool so that it can be accessed inside the template.
  - The first parameter 'import' is for identifying the alias of Import tool (in this case, 'import' , but it can be named anything).
  - The second parameter 'com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.ImportTool' has to be copied as it is (do not rename it). Otherwise, Import tool will not be loaded.
2. To import any content from a child template, type the following in the document that you want to import its content to the report:

- $import.include('child.docx') to import a complete document at the current position of the template, and/or
- $import.includeSection('child.docx', 'Section A') to include only the text contained in the named section of the document.

****

**$import**

This part names the alias assigned to Import tool when declaring it to the template.

****

**$import.include(FileName)**

**FileName** in [$import.include(FileName)](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Import+usage#Importusage-UsageinExample1) specifies the location of a file in the file system. **FileName** can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system. The **FileName** parameter can be set either statically or dynamically. If the file is not found in the file system, **FileName** would be searched as an Attached File element in a project in the **File** property.

****

**$import.includeSection(FileName, SectionName)**

**FileName** in [$import.includeSection(FileName, SectionName)](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Import+usage#Importusage-UsageinExample2) functions the same as in the sub-section above. **FileName** can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system. **SectionName** denotes a paragraph in the document named **FileName**, which is delimited by the **#sectionBegin(SectionName)** and **#sectionEnd** identifiers. The **SectionName** variable can also be set either statically or dynamically. If the file is not found in the file system, **FileName** would be searched as an Attached File element in a project in the **File** property.

#### INFO: Information

Information

You can also use **$attachedFile**in the parameter of **$import.include(FileName)** and **$import.includeSection(FileName, SectionName)**.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The <strong>#import</strong> directive will load Import tool so that it can be accessed inside the template.<br /><ul><li>The first parameter<strong> 'import'</strong> is for identifying the alias of Import tool (in this case, <strong>'import'</strong>, but it can be named anything).</li><li>The second parameter <strong>'com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.ImportTool'</strong> has to be copied as it is (do not rename it). Otherwise, Import tool will not be loaded.</li></ul></li><li>To import any content from a child template, type the following in the document that you want to import its content to the report:    </li></ol><ul><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><strong>$import.include('child.docx')</strong> to import a complete document at the current position of the template, and/or</li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><strong>$import.includeSection('child.docx', 'Section A')</strong> to include only the text contained in the named section of the document.</li></ul><p><strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>$import</strong></p><p>This part names the alias assigned to Import tool when declaring it to the template.</p><p><strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>$import.include(FileName)</strong></p><p><strong>FileName</strong> in <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Import+usage#Importusage-UsageinExample1">$import.include(FileName)</a> specifies the location of a file in the file system. <strong>FileName</strong> can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system. The <strong>FileName</strong> parameter can be set either statically or dynamically. If the file is not found in the file system, <strong>FileName</strong> would be searched as an Attached File element in a project in the <strong>File</strong> property.</p><p><strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>$import.includeSection(FileName, SectionName)</strong></p><p><strong>FileName</strong> in <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Import+usage#Importusage-UsageinExample2">$import.includeSection(FileName, SectionName)</a> functions the same as in the sub-section above. <strong>FileName</strong> can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system. <strong>SectionName</strong> denotes a paragraph in the document named <strong>FileName</strong>, which is delimited by the <strong>#sectionBegin(SectionName)</strong> and <strong>#sectionEnd</strong> identifiers. The <strong>SectionName</strong> variable can also be set either statically or dynamically. If the file is not found in the file system, <strong>FileName</strong> would be searched as an Attached File element in a project in the <strong>File</strong> property.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dd51469a-e548-4f4f-83ef-ac7e3af3ab2e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Information</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can also use <strong>$attachedFile </strong>in the parameter of <strong>$import.include(FileName)</strong> and <strong>$import.includeSection(FileName, SectionName)</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169678598 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00583: Import tool

- page_id: `169678598`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678598/Import+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:40:38.764+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1175109811

1175109813

1175109812

Import tool is a module of functions that allows importing documents to be in generated reports. Import tool enables you to dynamically import whole or parts of documents into reports, giving you greater flexibility when generating reports that require dynamic resources. You can now include documents whose location is only known at the actual translation time.

The Import tool allows you to:

- Parse and import RTF , DOCX, HTML , and text templates from any location in the file system
- Reuse the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report Wizard custom language' space='MD2024xR1'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report Wizard custom language' space='MD2024xR1'] syntax to import any part of a document. The Import tool uses the predefined syntaxes #sectionBegin and #sectionEnd , introduced with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report Wizard custom language' space='MD2024xR1'] .

1175109810

**Related pages**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Import tool enables you to dynamically import whole or parts of documents into reports, giving you greater flexibility when generating reports that require dynamic resources. You can now include documents whose location is only known at the actual translation time.</p><p>The Import tool allows you to:</p><ul><li>Parse and import <em>RTF</em>, <em>DOCX, HTML</em>, and text templates from any location in the file system</li><li>Reuse the <strong><ac:link ac:anchor="#includeSectionDirective"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Wizard custom language" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[#sectionBegin]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> and <strong><ac:link ac:anchor="#includeSectionDirective"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Wizard custom language" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[#sectionEnd]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> syntax to import any part of a document. The Import tool uses the predefined syntaxes <strong>#sectionBegin</strong> and <strong>#sectionEnd</strong>, introduced with the <strong><ac:link ac:anchor="#includeSectionDirective"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Wizard custom language" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[#includeSection Directive]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>.</li></ul><p> </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5c24317f-6306-485b-83a6-79ca6155bab8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1175109810</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="02c30e2f-93e2-4da8-9cd7-9b065513f43f" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169678600 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00584: Import usage

- page_id: `169678600`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678600/Import+usage
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-09-21T09:42:00.147+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Import tool
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This section explains the examples of usage of the main and child templates. These examples allow you to create documents as the main template and reuse the content of child templates into it.

#### Preparatory Step

Define a child template that you will use as in Examples 1, 2, and 3. This child template contains the header line and named sections (**Section A** and **Section B**). Type the following lines in the document that you want to define the child template:

```text

```

#### Usage in Example 1

This example shows how to statically include the complete child template as shown in the Preparatory Step. To include it, call the **$import.include('child.docx')** method. Note that there is no specified path. This path to the child template shows you that the main and child templates reside in the same directory. Type the following lines in the document that you want to include all of its content to the report:

```text

```

The output right below will include the entire text from the main template (right above) and from the child template minus the template directives. The included content will be then parsed and stripped of all velocity directives. The output is as follows:

```text

```

#### Usage in Example 2

This example shows how to statically include a section of the child template as shown in the Preparatory Step. To include the section named **Section A** from the child template, call the **$import.includeSection('templates/child.rtf', 'Section A')** method. In this example, the main and child templates reside in different directories, which means that the child template will reside in a subdirectory called *templates*. This *templates* directory will thus reside in the same directory as the main template. Type the following lines in the document that you want to include its content in **Section A** to the report:

```text

```

The output will be:

```text

```

#### Usage in Example 3

This example shows how to dynamically include the section of a child template as shown in the Preparatory Step. Before dynamically including the section of the child template named **Section B**, set a variable for the file location of the child template **[#set ($child = "***C:/ImportTool/child.rtf***")]** and another one for the section name to be included**[#set ($section = "Section B")]**. To include the **Section B** section from the child template, call the **$import.includeSection($child, $section)** method. In this example, the child's template location is provided by an absolute path. Note that dynamic values must not be specified in a pair of quote marks, otherwise the section will not be included. Type the following lines in the document that you want to include its content in **Section B** to the report:

```text

```

The output will be:

```text

```

The Import tool supports *DOCX, RTF, HTML, XML*, and*text* files only.

#### Usage in Example 4

This example shows how to include a document as an Attached File element in a project. First, you need to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Attaching, reattaching, and detaching files' space='MD2024xR1'] element in a project. The **File** property will then be used as a parameter to make a reference to the Attached File element. Type the following lines in the document that you want to include the content of the Attached File element to the report:

```text

```

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Including a document as an Attached File element in a project.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>This section explains the examples of usage of the main and child templates. These examples allow you to create documents as the main template and reuse the content of child templates into it.</p><h2>Preparatory Step</h2><p>Define a child template that you will use as in Examples 1, 2, and 3. This child template contains the header line and named sections (<strong>Section A</strong> and <strong>Section B</strong>). Type the following lines in the document that you want to define the child template:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c5fc47c3-5cca-4eaa-b170-f0b958c676e7"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[This is the child template.
#sectionBegin(Section A)
This is Section A.
#sectionEnd
#sectionBegin(Section B)
This is Section B.
#sectionEnd]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Usage in Example 1</h2><p>This example shows how to statically include the complete child template as shown in the Preparatory Step. To include it, call the <strong>$import.include('child.docx')</strong> method. Note that there is no specified path. This path to the child template shows you that the main and child templates reside in the same directory. Type the following lines in the document that you want to include all of its content to the report:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a66eea4e-eb0c-446b-ab4d-73b4d0b8e223"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#import ('import', 'com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.ImportTool')
This is the 1st main template
Include child template
$import.include('child.docx')]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The output right below will include the entire text from the main template (right above) and from the child template minus the template directives. The included content will be then parsed and stripped of all velocity directives. The output is as follows:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9edd6ed8-2585-454f-9e9a-5029cff67dea"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[This is the 1st main template
Include child template
This is the child template.
This is Section A.
This is Section B.]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Usage in Example 2</h2><p>This example shows how to statically include a section of the child template as shown in the Preparatory Step. To include the section named <strong>Section A</strong> from the child template, call the <strong>$import.includeSection('templates/child.rtf', 'Section A')</strong> method. In this example, the main and child templates reside in different directories, which means that the child template will reside in a subdirectory called <em>templates</em>. This <em>templates</em> directory will thus reside in the same directory as the main template. Type the following lines in the document that you want to include its content in <strong>Section A</strong> to the report:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3ce50327-1838-4616-b129-4f4d56003298"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#import ('import', 'com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.ImportTool')
This is the 2nd main template
Include Section A
$import.includeSection('templates/child.rtf', 'Section A')]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The output will be:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="32e6166f-cb4b-4901-a003-982722bd78e1"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[This is the 2nd  main template
Include Section A
This is Section A.]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Usage in Example 3</h2><p>This example shows how to dynamically include the section of a child template as shown in the Preparatory Step. Before dynamically including the section of the child template named <strong>Section B</strong>, set a variable for the file location of the child template <strong>[#set ($child = &quot;</strong><em>C:/ImportTool/child.rtf</em><strong>&quot;)]</strong> and another one for the section name to be included<strong> [#set ($section = &quot;Section B&quot;)]</strong>. To include the <strong>Section B</strong> section from the child template, call the <strong>$import.includeSection($child, $section)</strong> method. In this example, the child's template location is provided by an absolute path. Note that dynamic values must not be specified in a pair of quote marks, otherwise the section will not be included. Type the following lines in the document that you want to include its content in <strong>Section B</strong> to the report:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d10683bc-cf02-44d9-ab7a-35406d92e52a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#import ('import', 'com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.ImportTool')
This is the 3rd main template
Include Section B
#set ($child = "C:/ImportTool/child.rtf")
#set ($section = "Section B")
$import.includeSection($child, $section)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The output will be:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="be15115f-a695-4e24-9cb6-10022f943c67"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[This is the 3rd main template
Include Section B
This is Section B.]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3e9e5e3b-600a-4d8a-b607-5ba5ccd20fdc"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Import tool supports <em>DOCX, RTF, HTML, XML</em>, and<em> text</em> files only.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Usage in Example 4</h2><p>This example shows how to include a document as an Attached File element in a project. First, you need to <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Attaching, reattaching, and detaching files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create an Attached File]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> element in a project. The <strong>File</strong> property will then be used as a parameter to make a reference to the Attached File element. Type the following lines in the document that you want to include the content of the Attached File element to the report:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eade2b34-65c8-4540-8454-0bc694a8a8ed"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#import ('import', 'com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.ImportTool')
#foreach($included in $AttachedFile)
	#if($included.file == "included.docx")
		$import.include($included)
	#end
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Attach File Property.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Import usage" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Including a document as an Attached File element in a project.</h6><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169678708 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00585: Importing a template to modeling tool using Report Wizard

- page_id: `169678708`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678708/Importing+a+template+to+modeling+tool+using+Report+Wizard
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:28.270+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Template Creation Tutorial > Creating Your First Template
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1356305390

1356305392

1356305389

**On this page**

3

1356305392

#### Importing a template to the modeling tool

Once you have created a template, you will need to import it into the modeling tool by using Report Wizard.

To import a template into a modeling tool using Report Wizard

1. Click Tools > Report Wizard on the main menu.
2. Click the **New** button in the **Select a report template** wizard. The **New Template** dialog will open. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Opening the Report Wizard dialog and creating a new custom template by clicking the New button.
3. In the **New Template** dialog, type the name and description. Select a category, and then click**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Entering the new template name and description, and then selecting a template file in the New Template dialog.
4. Find the template file that you have created, and click [ATTACHMENT filename='Select_button.png'] .
5. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='Create_button.png'] to import the template.

To generate a report based on a template, select the template (in this example, **My template)**from the template list, and follow the steps as instructed by the wizard. See [Report Wizard User Guide](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Report+Wizard) for more information.

#### Glossary

- Report template Ready-made report configurations that you can use to quickly create a specific type of report in Report Wizard.

#### More information about working with templates and reports

For more information about working with templates and reports, visit the following pages:

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Template Management Pane' space='MD2024xR1']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Default templates' space='MD2024xR1']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report Data Management Pane' space='MD2024xR1']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating Reports from Report Wizard' space='MD2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The <strong>New Template</strong> dialog will open.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="newTemplate.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Importing a template to modeling tool using Report Wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>Opening the Report Wizard dialog and creating a new custom template by clicking the New button.</h6></li><li><p>In the <strong>New Template</strong> dialog, type the name and description. See <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Report+Wizard">Report Wizard User Guide</a> for more information.</p><h2>Glossary</h2><ul><li><strong>Report template</strong><br />Ready-made report configurations that you can use to quickly create a specific type of report in Report Wizard.</li></ul><h2>More information about working with templates and reports</h2><p>For more information about working with templates and reports, visit the following pages:</p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Template Management Pane" /></ac:link></p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Default templates" /></ac:link></p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ac:link></p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Generating Reports from Report Wizard" /></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675615 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00586: Importing Blocks and their properties

- page_id: `169675615`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675615/Importing+Blocks+and+their+properties
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:14.141+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Importing data from Excel or CSV files > Import examples
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Lets say, we need to import the engine structure including Blocks, their Part and Value Properties. Firstly, open an existing or create new project. In this case, we create new project. Next, do the following:

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing Blocks and their properties' space='MD2024xR1']
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing Blocks and their properties' space='MD2024xR1']
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing Blocks and their properties' space='MD2024xR1']
4. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing Blocks and their properties' space='MD2024xR1']

###### Importing Blocks

To import Block properties, the an *Engine* and *Engine Type* Blocks must be created in the model. In this case, we import those blocks. For this, in the top-left corner of the modeling tool, click the**File**>**Import From**>**Excel/CSV File > Import Using New Map**. In the [**Excel/CSV Import** dialog](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog) specify the following options:

1. Select the Excel file which contains the spreadsheet with the structure. In this case, the Engine Excel file is selected.
2. Select Block as Element Type option value.
3. Specify the location of imported elements in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment Tree' space='MD2024xR1'] by selecting the Target Scope option value. In this case, the Structure Package is selected.
4. Specify the Excel file sheet in which the table is prepared to import. In this case, the table is in the Sheet 1.
5. Create mapping between element property Name and excel table cell Name.
6. Click Import . The Engine and Engine Type Blocks are created in the model. [ATTACHMENT filename='importing_engine_blocks.png']

###### Saving properties Import Maps

After the Blocks are imported, their all properties can be imported at once by creating a Map Group which consists of 3 Import Maps: *Engine Parts*, *Engine Value Properties*, *Engine Type Value Properties*. Follow the procedures below to save Import Maps.

The *Engine* Block is decomposed to *Engine Type* Part Property. Let's create an Import Map for the Part Property. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, click the**File**>**Import From**>**Excel/CSV File > Import Using New Map**. In the [**Excel/CSV Import** dialog](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog) specify the following options:

1. Select the Excel file which contains the spreadsheet with the structure. In this case, the Engine Excel file is selected.
2. Select Part Property as Element Type option value.
3. Select the Engine Block as the Target Scope option value.
4. Specify the Excel file sheet in which the table is prepared to import. In this case, the table is in the Sheet 2.
5. Create mapping between element property Name and excel table cell Name.
6. Click Save Map .
7. In the Save Import Map dialog, select a location wherein to save the Import Map. We highly recommend select the Excel/CSV Imports Package.
8. Click the Create button. In the opened Specification window, type the Import Map name - in this case - Engine Part . Click OK > Close . The Engine Part Import Map is saved. [ATTACHMENT filename='saving_engine_parts_import_map.png']

The *Engine* Block contains *serialNum* and *year*Value Properties. To save this Import Map, in the already opened [**Excel/CSV Import** dialog](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog) specify options:

1. Select the Excel file which contains the spreadsheet with value properties. In this case, the Engine Excel file is selected.
2. Select Value Property as Element Type option value.
3. Select Name and Type properties as Properties to Map option value.
4. Select the Engine Block as the Target Scope option value.
5. Specify the Excel file sheet in which the table is prepared to import. In this case, the table is in the Sheet 3.
6. Create mapping between element property Name, Type and excel table cell Name, Type.
7. Select to search for references in entire model, to set types for Value Properties.
8. Click Save Map .
9. In the Save Import Map dialog, select a location wherein to save the Import Map. We highly recommend select the Excel/CSV Imports Package.
10. Click the Create button. In the opened Specification window, type the Import Map name - in this case - Engine Value Properties . Click OK > Close . The Engine Value Properties Import Map is saved.

Also, the E*ngine Type* Block contains *make*, *model*, *hp*, and *fuel***Value Properties. To save this Import Map, in the already opened [**Excel/CSV Import** dialog](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog) specify options:

1. Select the Excel file which contains the spreadsheet with value properties. In this case, the Engine Excel file is selected.
2. Select Value Property as Element Type option value.
3. Select Name and Type properties as Properties to Map option value.
4. Select the Engine Type Block as the Target Scope option value.
5. Specify the Excel file sheet in which the table is prepared to import. In this case, the table is in the Sheet 4.
6. Create mapping between element property Name and excel table cell Name.
7. Select to search for references in entire model, to set types for Value Properties.
8. Click Save Map .
9. In the Save Import Map dialog, select a location wherein to save the Import Map. We highly recommend select the Excel/CSV Imports Package.
10. Click the Create button. In the opened Specification window, type the Import Map name - in this case - Engine Type Value Properties . Click OK > Close . The Engine Type Value Properties Import Map is saved.

###### Creating Map Group

After, the properties Import Maps are prepared, let's create the *Engine Properties Import* Map Group and add the following Import Maps: *Engine Parts*, *Engine Value Properties*, *Engine Type Value Properties.* For this, in the top-left corner of the modeling tool, click the**File**>**Import From**>**Excel/CSV File > Manage Map Groups.**In the **Manage Map Groups** dialog, do the following:

1. In the **Tree** tab, select the *Excel/CSV imports* Package as the owner for the new Map Group.
2. Click the **Create** button.
3. In the Specification window, type thename *Engine Properties Import* for the new Map Group and click **Close**. The newly created Map Group is selected as the Select Map Group for Editing option value.
4. Press Ctrl and in the Tree tab, select the *Engine Parts*, *Engine Value Properties*, *Engine Type Value Properties* Import Maps.
5. C lick [ATTACHMENT filename='add_import_map.png'] to add them to the *Engine Properties Import* Import Map.
6. Click **OK**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

****

###### Importing Map Group

Finally, import an *Engine Properties Import* Map Group. For this, in the top-left corner of the modeling tool, click the**File**>**Import From**>**Excel/CSV File > Import Using Existing Map Group.**In the **Select Map Group** dialog, select the *Engine Properties Import* Map Group and click **OK**. All properties are created for the *Engine* and *Engine Type* Blocks at once.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Lets say, we need to import the engine structure including Blocks, their Part and Value Properties. Firstly, open an existing or create new project. In this case, we create new project. Next, do the following:</p><ol><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Importing Blocks"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Import Blocks]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Importing Blocks and their properties" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Saving properties Import Maps"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Save properties Import Maps]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Importing Blocks and their properties" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Creating Map Group"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create Map Group]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Importing Blocks and their properties" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Creating Map Group"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Import Map Group]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Importing Blocks and their properties" /></ac:link></li></ol><h4>Importing Blocks</h4><p>To import Block properties, the an <em>Engine</em> and <em>Engine Type</em> Blocks must be created in the model. In this case, we import those blocks. For this, i<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">n the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">lick the </span><strong>File</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> &gt; </span><strong>Import From</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> &gt; </span><strong>Excel/CSV File &gt; Import Using New Map</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. In t</span>he <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog"><strong>Excel/CSV Import</strong> dialog</a> specify the following options:</p><ol><li>Select the Excel file which contains the spreadsheet with the structure. In this case, the <em>Engine</em> Excel file is selected.</li><li>Select Block as <strong>Element Type</strong> option value.</li><li>Specify the location of imported elements in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Containment Tree" /></ac:link> by selecting the<strong> Target Scope</strong> option value. In this case, the <em>Structure</em> Package is selected.</li><li>Specify the Excel file sheet in which the table is prepared to import. In this case, the table is in the Sheet 1.</li><li>Create mapping between element property Name and excel table cell Name.</li><li>Click <strong>Import</strong>.<br />The <em>Engine</em> and <em>Engine Type</em> Blocks are created in the model.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="importing_engine_blocks.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Importing Blocks and their properties" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li></ol><h4>Saving properties Import Maps</h4><p>After the Blocks are imported, their all properties can be imported at once by creating a Map Group which consists of 3 Import Maps: <em>Engine Parts</em>, <em>Engine Value Properties</em>, <em>Engine Type Value Properties</em>. Follow the procedures below to save Import Maps.</p><p>The <em>Engine</em> Block is decomposed to <em>Engine Type</em> Part Property. Let's create an Import Map for the Part Property. <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">I</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">n the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">lick the </span><strong>File</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> &gt; </span><strong>Import From</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> &gt; </span><strong>Excel/CSV File &gt; Import Using New Map</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. In t</span>he <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog"><strong>Excel/CSV Import</strong> dialog</a> specify the following options:</p><ol><li>Select the Excel file which contains the spreadsheet with the structure. In this case, the <em>Engine</em> Excel file is selected.</li><li>Select Part Property as <strong>Element Type</strong> option value.</li><li>Select the <em>Engine </em>Block as the<strong> Target Scope</strong> option value.</li><li>Specify the Excel file sheet in which the table is prepared to import. In this case, the table is in the Sheet 2.</li><li>Create mapping between element property Name and excel table cell Name.</li><li>Click <strong>Save Map</strong>. </li><li>In the <strong>Save Import Map </strong>dialog, select a location wherein to save the Import Map. We highly recommend select the <em>Excel/CSV Imports</em> Package.</li><li>Click the <strong>Create</strong> button. In the opened Specification window, type the Import Map name - in this case - <em>Engine Part</em>. Click <strong>OK </strong>&gt; <strong>Close</strong>.<br />The <em>Engine Part</em> Import Map is saved.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="saving_engine_parts_import_map.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Importing Blocks and their properties" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p>The <em>Engine</em> Block contains <em>serialNum</em> and <em>year </em>Value Properties. To save this Import Map, i<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">n t</span>he already opened <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog"><strong>Excel/CSV Import</strong> dialog</a> specify options:</p><ol><li>Select the Excel file which contains the spreadsheet with value properties. In this case, the <em>Engine</em> Excel file is selected.</li><li>Select Value Property as <strong>Element Type</strong> option value.</li><li>Select Name and Type properties as <strong>Properties to Map</strong> option value.</li><li>Select the <em>Engine </em>Block as the<strong> Target Scope</strong> option value.</li><li>Specify the Excel file sheet in which the table is prepared to import. In this case, the table is in the Sheet 3.</li><li>Create mapping between element property Name, Type and excel table cell Name, Type.</li><li>Select to search for references in entire model, to set types for Value Properties.</li><li>Click <strong>Save Map</strong>. </li><li>In the <strong>Save Import Map </strong>dialog, select a location wherein to save the Import Map. We highly recommend select the <em>Excel/CSV Imports</em> Package.</li><li>Click the <strong>Create</strong> button. In the opened Specification window, type the Import Map name - in this case - <em>Engine Value Properties</em>. Click <strong>OK </strong>&gt; <strong>Close</strong>. <br />The <em>Engine Value Properties </em>Import Map is saved.<br /><br /></li></ol><p>Also, the E<em>ngine Type</em> Block contains <em>make</em>, <em>model</em>, <em>hp</em>, and <em>fuel</em><em> </em>Value Properties. To save this Import Map, i<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">n t</span>he already opened <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog"><strong>Excel/CSV Import</strong> dialog</a> specify options:</p><ol><li>Select the Excel file which contains the spreadsheet with value properties. In this case, the <em>Engine</em> Excel file is selected.</li><li>Select Value Property as <strong>Element Type</strong> option value.</li><li>Select Name and Type properties as <strong>Properties to Map</strong> option value.</li><li>Select the <em>Engine Type </em>Block as the<strong> Target Scope</strong> option value.</li><li>Specify the Excel file sheet in which the table is prepared to import. In this case, the table is in the Sheet 4.</li><li>Create mapping between element property Name and excel table cell Name.</li><li>Select to search for references in entire model, to set types for Value Properties.</li><li>Click <strong>Save Map</strong>. </li><li>In the <strong>Save Import Map </strong>dialog, select a location wherein to save the Import Map. We highly recommend select the <em>Excel/CSV Imports</em> Package.</li><li>Click the <strong>Create</strong> button. In the opened Specification window, type the Import Map name - in this case - <em>Engine Type Value Properties</em>. Click <strong>OK </strong>&gt; <strong>Close</strong>.<br />The <em>Engine Type Value Properties </em>Import Map is saved.</li></ol><h4>Creating Map Group</h4><p>After, the properties Import Maps are prepared, let's create the <em>Engine Properties Import</em> Map Group and add the following Import Maps: <em>Engine Parts</em>, <em>Engine Value Properties</em>, <em>Engine Type Value Properties.</em> For this, i<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">n the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">lick the </span><strong>File</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> &gt; </span><strong>Import From</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> &gt; </span><strong>Excel/CSV File &gt; Manage Map Groups. </strong>In the <strong>Manage Map Groups</strong> dialog, do the following:</p><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">In the <strong>Tree</strong> tab, select the <em>Excel/CSV imports</em> Package as the owner for the new Map Group.</span></p></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Click the <strong>Create</strong> button.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">In the Specification window, <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">type the</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> name <em>Engine Properties Import</em> for the new Map Group and click <strong>Close</strong>. </span></span>The newly created Map Group is selected as the <strong>Select Map Group for Editing</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> option value.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Press Ctrl and in the Tree tab, select the <em>Engine Parts</em>, <em>Engine Value Properties</em>, <em>Engine Type Value Properties</em> Import Maps.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">C</span>lick <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="23"><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_import_map.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Importing Blocks and their properties" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">to add them to the <em>Engine Properties Import</em> Import Map.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click <strong>OK</strong>.</span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="creating_map_group.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Importing Blocks and their properties" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h4>Importing Map Group</h4><p>Finally, import an <em>Engine Properties Import</em> Map Group. For this, i<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">n the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">lick the </span><strong>File</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> &gt; </span><strong>Import From</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> &gt; </span><strong>Excel/CSV File &gt; Import Using Existing Map Group. </strong>In the <strong>Select Map Group</strong> dialog, select the <em>Engine Properties Import</em> Map Group and click <strong>OK</strong>. All properties are created for the <em>Engine</em> and <em>Engine Type</em> Blocks at once.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="importing_map_group.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Importing Blocks and their properties" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p>
````

## PAGE 00587: Importing custom Jython libraries

- page_id: `169679638`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169679638/Importing+custom+Jython+libraries
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-05-23T06:04:42.252+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Macro Engine Documentation > Supported scripting languages
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

It is possible to import custom Python libraries.

To import and use a custom *.py* file

1. Create a Lib directory in the <tool_installation_directory>\plugins\com.nomagic.magicdraw.automaton\lib\engines\jython directory.
2. Add the custom .py file to the Lib directory.
3. To use the custom *.py* script, execute the following (e.g. *myscript.py* is the name of the *.py* file):

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>It is possible to import custom Python libraries.</p><p>To import and use a custom <em>.py</em> file</p><hr /><ol><li>Create a <em>Lib</em> directory in the <em>&lt;tool_installation_directory&gt;\plugins\com.nomagic.magicdraw.automaton\lib\engines\jython</em> directory.</li><li>Add the custom <em>.py</em> file to the <em>Lib</em> directory.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">To use the custom <em>.py</em> script, execute the following (e.g. <em>myscript.py</em> is the name of the <em>.py</em> file):</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="114a7d2c-f334-462d-a92c-d86e3afd2bb9"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import myscript
myscript.calculate(1, 5)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol>
````

## PAGE 00588: Importing custom tool to template

- page_id: `169678796`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678796/Importing+custom+tool+to+template
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-12-26T10:27:04.133+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Appendix A: report extensions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Report Wizard uses the import directive for importing a Custom Tool to a template.

An example of a directive:

```text

```

The import directive declares that the template uses the custom tool, names the tool that defines it, and specifies its tag prefix before the custom tool is used in a template page. You can use more than one import directive in a template, but the prefix defined in each template page must be unique.

#### Attributes

**name** (type:String) 
 The uniform or full qualified class name that locates the Tool class, for example, mytool.HelloTool

**prefix** (type:String) 
 The prefix that precedes the custom tool name such as a string in $hello.getHello(). Empty prefixes are not allowed. When developing or using custom tools, do not use tag prefixes such as bookmark, sorter, template, file, array, group, map, iterator, list, date, report, exporter, profiling, and project, as they are reserved by Report Wizard.

The code fragment indicated below declares a custom tool.

```text

```

The output from the above template is:

```text

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Report Wizard uses the import directive for importing a Custom Tool to a template.</p><p>An example of a directive:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dd50b953-d474-470e-a26b-45c8eefbac29"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#import('prefix', 'name')]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The import directive declares that the template uses the custom tool, names the tool that defines it, and specifies its tag prefix before the custom tool is used in a template page. You can use more than one import directive in a template, but the prefix defined in each template page must be unique.</p><h2>Attributes</h2><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><strong>name</strong> (type:String)<br />            The uniform or full qualified class name that locates the Tool class, for example, mytool.HelloTool</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><strong>prefix</strong> (type:String)<br />            The prefix that precedes the custom tool name such as a string in $hello.getHello(). Empty prefixes are not allowed. When developing or using custom tools, do              not use tag prefixes such as bookmark, sorter, template, file, array, group, map, iterator, list, date, report, exporter, profiling, and project, as they are reserved                by Report Wizard.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">The code fragment indicated below declares a custom tool.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8a6f2762-f7a5-40ea-9382-07cadf641971"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#import('hello', 'mytool.HelloTool')
Get Hello
$hello.getHello()
Get Hello Foo
$hello.getHello('foo')]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The output from the above template is:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cfe379ac-2d2c-4a4a-98ff-bd3a4fbdaf5b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Get Hello
Hello World
Get Hello Foo
Hello foo]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675576 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00589: Importing data from Excel or CSV files

- page_id: `169675576`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675576/Importing+data+from+Excel+or+CSV+files
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:12.078+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

2052901168

100.00002%

2052901183

2052901173

**On this page**

43

2052901166

INLINE

##### Introduction

You can import data from Excel (*.xls*and*.xlsx*) or*CSV*(*.csv*and*.txt*) format files to your model. You are able to specify the import mapping options between file columns and element properties of the model. Additionally, you can save your maps, collect them in groups, manage those groups, and use an entire group instead of a single map for data import. Before importing data from Excel or CSV files to model, create new or open an existing project.

700500

You can choose the following import commands:

- 
  - Import Using New Map - to import data from Excel or CSV file by specifying new mapping options and/or save an Import Map. >]]>[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing data from Excel or CSV files' space='MD2024xR1'] [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Saving an Import Map' space='MD2024xR1']>]]>
  - Import Using Existing Map - to import data from an Excel or CSV file by selecting a previously saved map. >]]>[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing data from Excel or CSV files' space='MD2024xR1'] [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Saving an Import Map' space='MD2024xR1']>]]>
  - **Import Using Existing Map Group** - to import data from Excel or CSV file by selecting the map group that collects as many Import Maps as you added. 
>]]>[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing data from Excel or CSV files' space='MD2024xR1']
  - Manage Map Groups - to create, delete or change the order of the m ap groups. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing Map Groups' space='MD2024xR1']>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### All Excel/CSV file import commands from the File menu.

##### Preparing Excel or CSV file to import

To import data from Excel or CSV files, the files must met the following prerequisites:

- Heading is located in the first row.
- When creating relationships, the spreadsheet must contain the source and target columns. The source and target columns contains the names/qualified names of elements respectively that are the client and supplier of the relationship.

Recommended prerequisites:

- Eliminate merged cells.
- Every column has a unique column name.
- Every row has the same number and name of columns.
- Every cell has the element name or qualified element name.

##### Importing data by using a new map

To import data from an Excel or CSV file to model by using a new map

1. Create new or open an existing project.
2. Make sure: 
 - the auto usages of Excel Import Plugin are removed. How to >>[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing data from Excel or CSV files' space='MD2024xR1']- the Excel or CSV file met the basic prerequisites. >]]>[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing data from Excel or CSV files' space='MD2024xR1']
3. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c lick the File > Import From > Excel/CSV File > Import Using New Map . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Excel and CSV Import dialog' space='MD2024xR1']**Excel/CSV Import** dialog opens.
4. Select the file and specify mapping options. [How to >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog#ExcelandCSVImportdialog-SelectExcel/CSVfilearea) You must select the file in the **Select Excel/CSV file** areaand specify the**Element Type**,**Properties to Map,**and **Target Scope** mapping option values to import data.
5. In the Mapping area, create mapping between the Excel/CSV file data and modeling tool properties. [How to >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog#ExcelandCSVImportdialog-Mappingarea)
6. (Optional) Click the Save Map button to save your map options as the Import Map . [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Saving an Import Map' space='MD2024xR1']>]]>
7. Click the Import button. The data from the Excel or CSV file is imported to the model according to the specified options.

##### Importing data by using an existing map

To import data from Excel or CSV file to model by using an existing map

1. Create new or open an existing project.
2. Make sure: 
 - the auto usages of Excel Import Plugin are removed. How to >>[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing data from Excel or CSV files' space='MD2024xR1']- the Excel or CSV file met the basic prerequisites. >]]>[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing data from Excel or CSV files' space='MD2024xR1']
3. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c lick the File > Import From > Excel/CSV File > Import Using Existing Map .
4. In the **Select Import Map** dialog, select existing Import Map. If you cannot see any Import Maps, you need to save it first. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Saving an Import Map' space='MD2024xR1']>]]>
5. Click OK . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Excel and CSV Import dialog' space='MD2024xR1']**Excel/CSV Import** dialog opens.
6. (Optional) Select the file, change mapping options. [How to >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog#ExcelandCSVImportdialog-SelectExcel/CSVfilearea)
7. (Optional) Click the Save Map button to override the selected Import Map or create a new one. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Saving an Import Map' space='MD2024xR1']>]]>
8. Click the Import button. The data from the Excel or CSV file is imported to the model according to the specified options.

##### Importing data by using an existing map group

To import data from an Excel or CSV file to model by using an existing map group

1. Create new or open an existing project.
2. Make sure: 
 - the auto usages of Excel Import Plugin are removed. How to >>[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing data from Excel or CSV files' space='MD2024xR1']- the Excel or CSV file met the basic prerequisites. >]]>[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing data from Excel or CSV files' space='MD2024xR1']
3. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c lick the File > Import From > Excel/CSV File > Import Using Existing Map Group .
4. In the **Select Map Group** dialog, choose the map group. If you cannot see any map group, you need to create it first. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing Map Groups' space='MD2024xR1']>]]>
5. Click **OK**. 
The data from Excel or CSV files are imported to the model according to Import Maps and their order in the Map Group. The import of a map group can fail because of the following reasons:the Excel or CSV file is missing;the element type does not exist in the model;properties do not exist in the selected element;the target scope does not exist in the model.

2052905962

**Related pages**

****

- [Sync with Excel or CSV files](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Sync+with+Excel+or+CSV+files)

****

You are able to specify the import mapping options between file columns and element properties of the model.  Additionally, you can save your maps, collect them in groups, manage those groups, and use an entire group instead of a single map for data import. Before importing data from Excel or CSV files to model, create new or open an existing project.</span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8a32c6d2-dd3b-4b6e-b54d-0811f8700266"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">700</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=x18wXfSfeTM&amp;feature=youtu.be" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">500</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><br /></span></p><p><br />You can choose the following import commands:</p><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li><strong>Import Using New Map</strong> - to import data from Excel or CSV file by specifying new mapping options and/or save an Import Map.<br /><ac:link ac:anchor="Importingdatabyusinganewmap"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to import data by using a new map >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Importing data from Excel or CSV files" /></ac:link><br /><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Saving an Import Map" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to save a map >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><strong>Import Using Existing Map</strong> - to import data from an Excel or CSV file by selecting a previously saved map. <br /><ac:link ac:anchor="Importingdatabyusingexistingmap"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to import data by using existing map >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Importing data from Excel or CSV files" /></ac:link><br /><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Saving an Import Map" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to override an existing map >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Import Using Existing Map Group</strong> - to import data from Excel or CSV file by selecting the map group that collects as many Import Maps as you added. <br /><ac:link ac:anchor="Importingdatabyusingexistingmapgroup"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to import data by using existing map group >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Importing data from Excel or CSV files" /></ac:link></p></li><li><strong>Manage Map Groups</strong> - to <span>create, delete or change the order of the m</span>ap groups. <br /><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Managing Map Groups" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to manage Map Groups >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Import_commands.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Importing data from Excel or CSV files" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">All Excel/CSV file import commands from the File menu.</h6><h3 class="with-breadcrumbs">Preparing Excel or CSV file to import</h3><p>To import data from Excel or CSV files, the files must met the following p<span style="color: rgb(57,57,60);">rerequisites</span>:</p><ul><li>Heading is located in the first row.</li><li>When creating relationships, the spreadsheet must contain the source and target columns. The source and target columns contains the names/qualified names of elements respectively that are the client and supplier of the relationship.<br /><br /></li></ul><p>Recommended prerequisites:</p><ul><li>Eliminate merged cells.</li><li>Every column has a unique column name.</li><li>Every row has the same number and name of columns.</li><li>Every cell has the element name or qualified element name. <br /><br /></li></ul><h3>Importing data by using a new map</h3><p>To import data from an Excel or CSV file to model by using a new map</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Create new or open an existing project.<br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Make sure:<br /> - the auto usages of Excel Import Plugin are removed. <ac:link><ac:link-body>How to &gt;&gt;<br /> </ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Importing data from Excel or CSV files" /></ac:link>- the Excel or CSV file met the basic p<span style="color: rgb(57,57,60);">rerequisites. <ac:link><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Basic Prerequisites >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Importing data from Excel or CSV files" /></ac:link></span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c</span>lick the <strong>File</strong> &gt; <strong>Import From</strong> &gt; <strong>Excel/CSV File &gt; Import Using New Map</strong>. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Excel and CSV Import dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Excel/CSV Import</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Select the file and specify mapping options. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog#ExcelandCSVImportdialog-SelectExcel/CSVfilearea">How to &gt;&gt;</a></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fed5b67b-4625-43a1-a3ff-52dc368f2a9b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You must select the file in the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Select Excel/CSV file</strong> area </span>and specify the<strong> Element Type</strong>,<strong> Properties to Map,</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> and <strong>Target Scope</strong> mapping option values to import data.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>In the Mapping area, create mapping between<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> the Excel/CSV file data and <span>modeling tool</span> properties. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog#ExcelandCSVImportdialog-Mappingarea">How to &gt;&gt;</a></span></li><li>(Optional) Click the<strong> Save Map</strong> button <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">to save your map options as the Import Map</span>. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Saving an Import Map" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li>Click the <strong>Import</strong> button.<br />The data from the Excel or CSV file is imported to the model according to the specified options.</li></ol><h3>Importing data by using an existing map</h3><p>To import data from Excel or CSV file to model by using an existing map</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span>Create new or open an existing project.</span><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Make sure:<br /> - the auto usages of Excel Import Plugin are removed. <ac:link><ac:link-body>How to &gt;&gt;<br /> </ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Importing data from Excel or CSV files" /></ac:link>- the Excel or CSV file met the basic p<span style="color: rgb(57,57,60);">rerequisites. <ac:link><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Basic Prerequisites >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Importing data from Excel or CSV files" /></ac:link></span></span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c</span>lick the <strong>File</strong> &gt; <strong>Import From</strong> &gt; <strong>Excel/CSV File &gt; Import Using Existing Map</strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <strong>Select Import Map</strong> dialog, select existing Import Map. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="69c538e8-90bb-4db3-8e65-c060114faded"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you cannot see any Import Maps, you need to save it first. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" Import</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens. </li><li>(Optional) Select the file, change <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">mapping options. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog#ExcelandCSVImportdialog-SelectExcel/CSVfilearea">How to &gt;&gt;</a></span></li><li>(Optional) Click the <strong>Save Map</strong> button to override the selected Import Map or create a new one. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Saving an Import Map" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li>Click the <strong>Import</strong> button.<br />The data from the Excel or CSV file is imported to the model according to the specified options.</li></ol><h3>Importing data by using an existing map group</h3><p><br /></p><p>To import data from an Excel or CSV file to model by using an existing map group</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span>Create new or open an existing project.</span><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Make sure:<br /> - the auto usages of Excel Import Plugin are removed. <ac:link><ac:link-body>How to &gt;&gt;<br /> </ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Importing data from Excel or CSV files" /></ac:link>- the Excel or CSV file met the basic p<span style="color: rgb(57,57,60);">rerequisites. <ac:link><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Basic Prerequisites >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Importing data from Excel or CSV files" /></ac:link></span></span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the top-left corner ac:macro-id="ce5ad524-c962-4cc7-b721-c82462ca4f50"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>If you cannot see any map group, you need to create it first. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e8b55090-d00b-494c-b107-4c36ef33b9f0"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The import of a map group can fail because of the following reasons:</p><ul><li>the model.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br
