<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673426 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00590: Importing data from other UML tools and formats

- page_id: `169673426`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673426/Importing+data+from+other+UML+tools+and+formats
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T11:59:49.266+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects
- labels: ['importing-uml', 'importing-data']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If you need to open a model of a different format or created with another UML tool, you have to import it to the modeling tool instead of simply opening it. The modeling tool allows importing projects from the following file formats:

- Another .mdzip project
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Excel and CSV Import dialog' space='MD2024xR1']
- UML 2.1/2.5 XMI File
- MagicDraw Native XML File
- MOF XMI File
- CA ERwin Data Modeler v7.x
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing ReqIF files' space='CRMP2024xR1']
- OWL Ontology File
- Eclipse UML2 (v2.x, v3.x, v4.x, v5.x) XMI file
- XPDL File
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='FMU import' space='SYSMLP2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Simulink import' space='SYSMLP2024xR1']
- Modelica Library

- Enterprise Architect UML 2.1 XMI 2.1 File. Data can be imported from an Enterprise Architect 7.1, 7.5, or 8.0 file. Please note that Enterprise Architect does not export 100% standard UML 2.1 XMI, and this causes some data loss during the import.

- Use[Sodius](https://www.sodiuswillert.com/en/home)to import data from the following products:
  - Publisher for Rational Software Architect
  - Publisher for System Architect
  - Publisher for Rhapsody

- UML diagrams from Microsoft Visio using XMI files. Microsoft Visio 2007 and earlier requires extra add-ons for exporting UML model to XMI files.

To import an external model

1. Select File > **Import** From .
2. Select a file format you want to import a model from.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>If you need to open a model of a different format or created with another UML tool, you have to import it to the modeling tool instead of simply opening it. <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The modeling tool allows importing projects from the following file formats:</span></p><p><br /></p><ul><li>Another .mdzip project</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Excel and CSV Import dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Excel/CSV File]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li>UML 2.1/2.5 XMI File</li><li>MagicDraw Native XML File</li><li>MOF XMI File</li><li>CA ERwin Data Modeler v7.x</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CRMP2024xR1" ri:content-title="Importing ReqIF files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Requirements Interchange Format (ReqIF) File]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li>OWL Ontology File</li><li>Eclipse UML2 (v2.x, v3.x, v4.x, v5.x) XMI file</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">XPDL File</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR1" ri:content-title="FMU import" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[FMU File]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR1" ri:content-title="Simulink import" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Simulink File]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Modelica Library</span></li></ul><ul><li><p>Enterprise Architect UML 2.1 XMI 2.1 File. Data can be imported from an Enterprise Architect 7.1, 7.5, or 8.0 file.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5e76de8f-6f1d-46d0-a283-b9757cf03d8c"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Please note that Enterprise Architect does not export 100% standard UML 2.1 XMI, and this causes some data loss during the import.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Use </span><a href="https://www.sodiuswillert.com/en/home">Sodius</a><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> to import data from the following products:</span><br /></span><ul><li>Publisher for Rational Software Architect</li><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Publisher for System Architect</span></p></li><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Publisher for Rhapsody</span></p></li></ul></li></ul><ul><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">UML diagrams from Microsoft Visio using XMI files.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="55b087f9-d2c1-461d-b087-fd7b5f19607d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Microsoft Visio 2007 and earlier requires extra add-ons for exporting UML model to XMI files.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To import an external model</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Select </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">File</strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> &gt; </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><strong>Import</strong></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">From</strong>.</li><li>Select a file format you want to import a model from. </li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673441 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00591: Importing Diagrams

- page_id: `169673441`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673441/Importing+Diagrams
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-01T17:25:47.178+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Importing data from other UML tools and formats > Enterprise Architect Import
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Enterprise Architect Import allows you to import diagrams. The imported diagram information includes:

33

##### Geometry Properties

The geometry properties imported to a UML model are:

- Positions on a diagram (for shape elements)
- Width and height (for shape elements)
- Path break points (for link elements)

#### NOTE: Note

Note

Other display properties can override geometry information. For example, if an imported element width is shorter than the required width to display text on the element, the width will be adjusted automatically.

##### Color Properties

Color properties will be imported along with the diagrams to a modeling tool developed by NoMagic. Each color property has a different name in a modeling tool. The following table shows color properties mapping.

| EA | Modeling tool by NoMagic |
| --- | --- |
| Background | Fill color |
| Border color (for shape element) | Pen color |
| Font color | Text color |
| Line color (for link element) | Pen color |

##### Display Properties

Display properties in EA can be categorized into three groups: shape, link, and diagram. Only those properties corresponding to the modeling tool will be imported. For example, the **Show Diagram Details**property in EA will be imported as the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Table with diagram information' space='MD2024xR1']**Show Diagram Info**property in a modeling tool developed by NoMagic.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Enterprise Architect Import allows you to import diagrams. The imported diagram information includes:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6eeee738-6a1a-47fa-bb30-70cfafa46597"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">3</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h3>Geometry Properties</h3><p>The geometry properties imported to a UML model are:</p><ul><li>Positions on a diagram (for shape elements)</li><li>Width and height (for shape elements)</li><li>Path break points (for link elements)</li></ul><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9067b152-2de0-447c-a428-0923be62ccce"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Other display properties can override geometry information. For example, if an imported element width is shorter than the required width to display text on the element, the width will be adjusted automatically.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Color Properties</h3><p>Color properties will be imported along with the diagrams to a modeling tool developed by NoMagic. Each color property has a different name in a modeling tool. The following table shows color properties mapping.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 32.1145%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 64.0199%;" /><col style="width: 35.9801%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>EA</th><th><span>Modeling tool by NoMagic</span></th></tr><tr><td>Background</td><td><p>Fill color</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Border color (for shape element)</p></td><td><p>Pen color</p></td></tr><tr><td><span>Font<span> </span>color</span></td><td><p>Text color</p></td></tr><tr><td><span>Line color (for link element)</span></td><td><p>Pen color</p></td></tr></tbody></table><h3>Display Properties</h3><p>Display properties in EA can be categorized into three groups: shape, link, and diagram. Only those properties corresponding to the modeling tool will be imported. For example, the <strong>Show Diagram Details </strong>property in EA will be imported as the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Table with diagram information" /><ac:link-body><strong>Show Diagram Info </strong>property</ac:link-body></ac:link> in a modeling tool developed by NoMagic.</p><p><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675625 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00592: Importing instances

- page_id: `169675625`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675625/Importing+instances
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:14.708+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Importing data from Excel or CSV files > Import examples
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Lets say, we need to import the nested instances with slots in the already prepared Engine model structure where the *Engine* is decomposed to *Engine Type*, as shown in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The *Engine* Block will be a classifier for three engine instances. Each engine instance will contain *serialNum*, *year*slots with defined values and nested *engine Type* instances*.* Each nested *engine Type* instances will contain four *fuel*, *hp*, *make* and *model* slots with defined values. In the following figure, the Excel spreadsheet is prepared for the instances import. [Learn more how to prepare the Excel or CSV file for import >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Importing+data+from+Excel+or+CSV+files#ImportingdatafromExcelorCSVfiles-PreparingExcelorCSVfiletoimport)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Next, begin the import: in the top-left corner of the modeling tool, click the**File**>**Import From**>**Excel/CSV File > Import Using New Map**. In the [**Excel/CSV Import** dialog](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog) specify options:

1. Select the Excel file which contains the spreadsheet with instances. In this case, the Vehicle model import Excel file is selected.
2. Select Instances as Import Type option value.
3. Select Engine Block as Instance Classifier option value.
4. Select Value Properties of the Engine Block and engine Type Part Property as the Nested Properties To Map option values. [ATTACHMENT filename='select_nested_properties.png']
5. Specify the location of imported elements in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment Tree' space='MD2024xR1'] by selecting the Target Scope option value. In this case, the Instances Package is selected.
6. Specify the Excel file sheet in which the table is prepared to import. In this case, the table is in the Sheet 4.
7. Create mapping between model properties and excel table cells.
8. Click Import . [ATTACHMENT filename='instances_import_dialog_options.png']

As a result, three nested engine instances and their slots are created with defined values.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Lets say, we need to import the nested instances with slots in the already prepared Engine model structure where the <em>Engine</em> is decomposed to <em>Engine Type</em>, as shown in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="engine_decomposition.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Importing instances" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The <em>Engine</em> Block will be a classifier for three engine instances. Each engine instance will contain <em>serialNum</em>, <em>year </em>slots with defined values and nested <em>engine Type</em> instances<em>.</em> Each nested <em>engine Type</em> instances will contain four <em>fuel</em>, <em>hp</em>, <em>make</em> and <em>model</em> slots with defined values. In the following figure, the Excel spreadsheet is prepared for the instances import. <a style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;" href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Importing+data+from+Excel+or+CSV+files#ImportingdatafromExcelorCSVfiles-PreparingExcelorCSVfiletoimport">Learn more how to prepare the Excel or CSV file for import &gt;&gt;</a></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="engines_excel_spreadsheet.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Importing instances" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>Next, begin the import: i<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">n the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">lick the </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">File</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> &gt; </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Import From</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> &gt; </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Excel/CSV File &gt; Import Using New Map</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. In t</span>he <a style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;" href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog"><strong>Excel/CSV Import</strong> dialog</a> specify options:</p><ol><li>Select the Excel file which contains the spreadsheet with instances. In this case, the <em>Vehicle model import</em> Excel file is selected.</li><li>Select Instances as <strong>Import Type</strong> option value.</li><li>Select <em>Engine</em> Block as <strong>Instance Classifier</strong> option value.</li><li>Select Value Properties of the <em>Engine</em> Block and <em>engine Type</em> Part Property as the <strong>Nested Properties To Map</strong> option values.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_nested_properties.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Importing instances" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Specify the location of imported elements in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Containment Tree" /></ac:link> by selecting the<strong> Target Scope</strong> option value. In this case, the <em>Instances </em>Package is selected.</li><li>Specify the Excel file sheet in which the table is prepared to import. In this case, the table is in the Sheet 4.</li><li>Create mapping between model properties and excel table cells.</li><li>Click <strong>Import</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="instances_import_dialog_options.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Importing instances" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p>As a result, three nested engine instances and their slots are created with defined values. </p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="imported_instances.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Importing instances" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673679 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00593: Importing models

- page_id: `169673679`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673679/Importing+models
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-03-20T14:29:12.376+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Project partitioning > Composing model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Importing models from used projects

If you no longer need to have the set of elements in a separate project, you can import these elements into the main project.

To import a used project into the main project

1. In the Model Browser, select the package of this used project and open the shortcut menu .
2. Select Project Usages > Import .

All the elements of this used project are copied into the main project, and links to this used project are removed from the main project. The unlinked project and its elements continue to exist in the file system or server after the import.

- If the used project you want to import uses other projects, after the import these projects become directly used in the main project.
- The Standard/System Profiles cannot be imported according to our tool policy.

##### Importing models from other projects

Another way of composing your model, is to import a model from another project.

To import a model from another project

1. In the main menu, select File > Import From > Another Project .
2. Select a project you want to import.
3. Click **Import**. All elements from another project will be imported. If the model that you are importing also uses projects, these projects will be reused directly in your project after the model import.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3>Importing models from used projects</h3><p>If you no longer need to have the set of elements in a separate project, you can import these elements into the main project.</p><p> </p><p>To import a used project into the main project</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Model Browser, select the package of this used project and <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="cd108d97-84da-4865-8ccf-44b486b6e0c6">open the shortcut menu</ac:inline-comment-marker>.</li><li>Select <strong>Project Usages</strong> &gt; <strong>Import</strong>.</li></ol><p>All the elements of this used project are copied into the main project, and links to this used project are removed from the main project. The unlinked project and its elements continue to exist in the file system or server after the import.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4790239a-deb3-4f59-ba25-1ce2e1cdf086"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>If the used project you want to import uses other projects, after the import these projects become directly used in the main project.</li><li>The Standard/System Profiles cannot be imported according to our tool policy.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3 class="with-breadcrumbs">Importing models from other projects</h3><p>Another way of composing your model, is to import a model from another project.</p><p> </p><p>To import a model from another project</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu, select <strong>File</strong> &gt; <strong>Import From</strong> &gt; <strong>Another Project</strong>.</li><li>Select a project you want to import.</li><li><p>Click <strong>Import</strong>. All elements from another project will be imported.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b8f81a64-5f1e-4400-a66f-28ac9ff8134f"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the model that you are importing also uses projects, these projects will be reused directly in your project after the model import.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675612 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00594: Importing nested Requirements

- page_id: `169675612`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675612/Importing+nested+Requirements
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:13.906+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Importing data from Excel or CSV files > Import examples
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Lets' say we need to import nested structure of requirements from the Excel file. For this, you must define the Owner property in the Excel spreadsheet with the name or id of each requirement. As example, see the following figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Requirements in the Excel spreadsheet with defined Owner property.

To import nested Requirements

1. Open the project in the modeling tool.
2. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, click the **File** > **Import From** > **Excel/CSV File > Import Using New Map**. For element import via tables, use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sync with Excel or CSV files' space='MD2024xR1'] functionality.
3. In the [**Excel/CSV Import** dialog](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog), select the Excel file. Make sure the Excel file contains Owner column. See the example above.
4. Specify the mapping options:
  - Import Type - select the Element value.
  - Element Type - select the Requirement type you want to create.
  - Properties to Map - select Name, Text, id and Owner properties you want to fill in with data from Excel file.
  - **Target Scope**- select the Package you want to contain imported Requirements. If the Owner property is selected as the **Properties to Map** option value, the data is imported according the owner. You can specify more mapping options according to your needs. [Learn more about mapping****options >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog#ExcelandCSVImportdialog-MappingOptionsarea)
5. Create the mapping between Excel file columns and element properties by dragging the column on the right to the property on the left. [Learn more about mapping area >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog#ExcelandCSVImportdialog-Mappingarea)
6. (Optional) Click the Save Map button to save your map options as the Import Map. Learn more how to save Import Map >>
7. Click the Import button. The Requirements are imported. If any of the imported Requirement already exist in the model t hey are updated. To avoid duplication of elements specify **Identification Property** value or **I****dentify Relationship (End) Source by** and **Identify Relationship (End) Target by** values. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Excel and CSV Import dialog' space='MD2024xR1']>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673756 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00595: Importing projects from Ecore files

- page_id: `169673756`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673756/Importing+projects+from+Ecore+files
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T11:59:50.981+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Ecore support
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There is no possibility to import Ecore files directly. Ecore files should be imported using the EMOF import feature. 
 
To import an Ecore file

1. In the Eclipse environment, open the *.ecore file and save it as *.emof.
2. Use the Importing projects from MOF files procedure.

This way of importing Ecore files may cause some Ecore-specific model details that don't exist in EMOF to be lost.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>There is no possibility to import Ecore files directly. Ecore files should be imported using the EMOF import feature. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /> <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> To import an Ecore file</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the Eclipse environment, open the *.ecore file and save it as *.emof.</li><li>Use the <a href="http://documentation.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Importing+projects+from+MOF+files">Importing projects from MOF files</a> procedure.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="32276341-4f32-43be-be16-e7105068a796"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This way of importing Ecore files may cause some Ecore-specific model details that don't exist in EMOF to be lost.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673734 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00596: Importing projects from MOF files

- page_id: `169673734`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673734/Importing+projects+from+MOF+files
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-03-20T14:35:30.142+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > MOF support
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To import a project from a MOF file

1. From the main menu, select File > Import From > MOF XMI File .
2. In the dialog, select the EMOF or CMOF file you want to import, and click Open . The file is imported as a separate project.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To import a project from a MOF file</p><hr /><ol><li>From the main menu, select <strong>File</strong> &gt; <strong>Import From</strong> &gt; <strong>MOF XMI File</strong>.</li><li>In the dialog, select the EMOF or CMOF file you want to import, and click <strong>Open</strong>. The file is imported as a separate project.</li></ol><p> </p>
````

## PAGE 00597: Importing relationships

- page_id: `169675621`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675621/Importing+relationships
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:14.389+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Importing data from Excel or CSV files > Import examples
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

73869989

100.00002%

73870000

73869990

**On this page**

5

73869987

##### Main conditions

You can create relationships between existing model elements by importing them from Excel/CSV files. For thisthe following conditions must be met:

- 
  - The source/client and target/supplierelements you want to create relationships between must exist in the project.
  - The Excel/CSV source and target columns must be mapped with the Client/Source and Supplier/Target properties in the mapping area of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Excel and CSV Import dialog' space='MD2024xR1'].
  - The source and target elements must be legal in terms of Metamodel, DSL and code rules, e.g., the satisfy relationship client must be a requirement.

##### Preparing the Excel file to import relationships

When creating relationships in the model by importing them, you need to make sure the Excel file contains the source and target columns. The source column contains the names of elements that are the client of the relationship. The target column contains the names of the elements that are the suppliers of the relationship. In the example below, the Source column contains the names of the Blocks and the Target column contains the names of Requirements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Excel file content prepared with element names for the relationships import.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing Blocks and their properties' space='MD2024xR1']>]]>[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing nested Requirements' space='MD2024xR1']>]]>

If you have to deal with a large a scope of information, the elements are not under the same target scope, or you want to increase the import speed, use the following:

- Select to search elements and references in the Target Scope Only in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Excel and CSV Import dialog' space='MD2024xR1']**Excel/CSV Import** dialog wh en importing data.
- Define your source and target columns in the Excel file with qualified names of elements. In the example below, the Source column contains the qualified names of the Blocks and the Target column contains the qualified names of Requirements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The The Excel file content prepared with qualified element names for the relationships import.

##### Relationships import procedure

To import relationships

1. Prepare the Excel file for relationships import. >]]>[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing relationships' space='MD2024xR1']
2. Open the project in the modeling tool. Make sure the elements you want to create relationships between exist in the project. 
[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing Blocks and their properties' space='MD2024xR1']>]]>[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing nested Requirements' space='MD2024xR1']>]]>
3. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, click the **File** > **Import From** > **Excel/CSV File > Import Using New Map**. For element import via tables, use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sync with Excel or CSV files' space='MD2024xR1'] functionality.
4. In the **Excel/CSV Import** dialog , select the Excel file. How to >>
5. Specify the mapping options:
  - Import Type - select the Element value.
  - Element Type - select the relationship type you want to create.
  - Properties to Map - the Client and Supplier properties are selected by default.
  - **Target Scope**- select the Package you want to contain imported relationships. If the Owner property is selected as the **Properties to Map** option value, the data is imported according the owner. You can specify more mapping options according to your needs. [Learn more about mapping****options >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog#ExcelandCSVImportdialog-MappingOptionsarea)
6. Create the mapping between Excel file columns and element properties by dragging the Source column on the right to the Client property on the left. Do the same with the Target and Supplier or other columns/properties.[Learn more about mapping area >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog#ExcelandCSVImportdialog-Mappingarea)
7. (Optional) Cli ck the Save Map button to save your map options as the Import Map. Learn more how to save Import Map >>
8. Click the Import button. The relationships are imported. If any of the imported relationships already exist in the model t hey are updated. To avoid duplication of elements specify **Identification Property** value or **I****dentify Relationship (End) Source by** and **Identify Relationship (End) Target by** values. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Excel and CSV Import dialog' space='MD2024xR1']>]]>

The relationship import can fail due to the following reasons:

- The relationship end is empty in the data file.
- The source or the target elements do not exist in the model or the mechanism didn't find any.

In the following example, you can see specified import options in the **Excel/CSV Import** dialog before the import. After the import the Satisfy relationships are created between the defined Source and Target elements: Blocks and Requirements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The import of Satisfy relationship.

73874634

**Related pages**

- Importing data from Excel or CSV files
- Saving an Import Map
- Managing Map Groups
- Excel and CSV Import dialog
- [Sync with Excel or CSV files](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Sync+with+Excel+or+CSV+files)

For this </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">the following conditions must b<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="170073a5-01d5-48de-8278-606d3004334e">e met:</ac:inline-comment-marker></span></p><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The source<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">/client</span> and target/supplier </span>elements you want to create relationships between must exist in the project.</span><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The Excel/CSV source and target columns must be mapped with the Client<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">/S</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">ource</span> and Supplier/<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Target</span> properties in the mapping area of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Excel and CSV Import dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Excel/CSV Import dialog]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The source and target elements must be legal in terms of Metamodel, DSL and code rules, e.g., the satisfy relationship client must be a requirement.</span></li></ul></li></ul><h3><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Preparing the Excel file to import relationships </span></h3><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When creating relationships in the model by importing them, you need to make sure the Excel file contains the source and target columns. The source column contains the names of elements that are the client of the relationship. The t</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">arget column contains the names of the elements that are the suppliers of the relationship.  In the example below, the Source column contains the names of the Blocks and the Target column contains the names of <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="9c0b0b59-fcb1-4385-9b40-f83d60b8d996">Requireme</ac:inline-comment-marker>nts.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="excel_file_content_element_names.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Importing relationships" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The Excel file content prepared with element names for the relationships import.</span></h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a8537137-5dd5-4660-9a7c-210ae5426b85"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Importing Blocks and their properties" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to import Blocks and their properties >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><br /></span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Importing nested Requirements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to import Requirements >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br />If you have to deal with a large a scope of information, the elements are not under the same target scope, or you want to increase the import speed, use the following:</span></p><ul><li>Select to search elements and references in the <strong>Target Scope Only</strong> in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Excel and CSV Import dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Excel/CSV Import</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> wh<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c1e0d34a-fa2c-4683-9fe7-351af29daba7">en importing data.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li>Define your source and target columns in the Excel file with qualified names of elements. In the example below, the Source column contains the qualified names of the Blocks and the Target column contains the qualified names of Requirements.</li></ul><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="excel_file_content_qualified_names.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Importing relationships" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The Excel file content prepared with qualified element names for the relationships import.</span></span></h6><h3>Relationships import procedure</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To import relationships</span></p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Prepare the Excel file for relationships import. <ac:link><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to prepare the Excel for the ac:macro-id="c49ee855-2c1f-4fc8-b6b9-d286c8518d83"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Make sure the elements you want to create relationships between exist in the project.<br >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><br /></span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Importing nested Requirements" the top-left corner of the modeling tool, <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="71e8ffc2-6548-4aba-9c64-c52e50cfb4b4">click</ac:inline-comment-marker> the <strong>File</strong> &gt; <strong>Import ac:macro-id="550f0d57-9ef5-4e47-be80-3ea69ef5a3be"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For element import via tables, use the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Sync with Excel or CSV href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog"><strong>Excel/CSV Import</strong> dialog</a>, select the Excel file. <a /></span><ul><li><strong>Import Type </strong>- select the Element value.</li><li><strong>Element Type </strong>- select the relationship type you want to create.</li><li><strong>Properties to to <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="03469e68-1443-462c-b12c-dc3869e0edd5">contain imported relat</ac:inline-comment-marker>ionships. <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">If the Owner property is selected as the <strong>Properties to Map</strong> option value, the data is imported according the owner.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="980f374f-bd9c-4ec1-aa28-1eccd62e0348"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> You can specify more mapping options according to your needs. <a &gt;&gt;</a></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></li><li><p>Create the mapping between Excel file columns and element properties by dragging the Source column on the right to the Client property on the left. Do the same with the Target and Supplier or other co<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a8279882-b14f-4017-9fdb-333f23229d0d">lumns/properties.<a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog#ExcelandCSVImportdialog-Mappingarea"> Learn more about mapping area &gt;&gt;</a></ac:inline-comment-marker></p></li><li>(Optional) Cli<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="ccfbd794-fb77-4e40-abf7-462fe69d4a95">ck the</ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="ccfbd794-fb77-4e40-abf7-462fe69d4a95"> Save Map</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong> button to save your map options as the Import Map. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Saving+an+Import+Map">Learn more how to save Import Map &gt;&gt;</a></li><li>Click the <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Import</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> button.<br /></span>The relationships are imported. If any of the imported relationships already exist in the model t<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">hey are <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="9ad23a6a-1e5d-480f-a37d-9ea1fee47058">updated. To avoid</ac:inline-comment-marker> duplication of elements specify <strong>Identification Property</strong> value</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> or <strong>I</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><strong>dentify Relationship (End) Source by</strong> and <strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">I</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">dentify Relationship (End) Target by</span></strong> values. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Excel and CSV Import dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about mapping options in the Excel/CSV Import dialog>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><br /></span></span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><br /></span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="11b36b6e-52a8-475f-92cc-71a4f243e323"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The relationship import can fail due to the following reasons:</span></p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The relationship end is empty in the data file.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The source or the target elements <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7f13de85-ee44-446f-82a6-1e7e8d41f49b">do not exist</ac:inline-comment-marker> in the model or the mechanism didn't find any.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the following example, you can see specified import options in the <strong>Excel/CSV Import</strong> dialog before the import. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169677214 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00598: Importing server project file

- page_id: `169677214`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169677214/Importing+server+project+file
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-29T15:00:50.454+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud > Collaboration between disconnected teams > Using server project file format
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To add a project as a new server project, import the project*.szip*file.

To import a server project file

1. Select File > Import From > Server Project File.
2. Select the .szip file you want to use and click **Open**. This opens the **Import Project to the Server**dialog: 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. In the Action column, you see the action that is performed with the project once you click Import . Possible actions include:
  - Add to Server – the project is added to the server.
    - If the action is Add to Server , you can modify the **Name on Server**column to choose a new name for the imported project.
    - If you want to skip certain projects, you can change the Add to Server action to Skip .
  - Skip – the server project is skipped.
  - Skip (Project Removed) – the project is removed. You must use Data Manager to either delete it completely from the server or restore the deleted version.
  - Skip (Project Exists) – the project already exists on the server and cannot be imported . If you still need to upload the .szip version of the project to Teamwork Cloud, use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Updating a server project with local changes' space='MD2024xR1']**Update from Local Project**functionality.
4. To choose the category for the newly added project, select [IMAGE alt='' src=''] at the bottom of the dialog. This opens the **Select Category**dialog : 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. Choose the category and click **OK**to return to the **Import Project to the Server**dialog.
6. Click **Import**.****The project will be added as a new server project.

**Please note:**

- If the project you are importing was saved together with used projects, all of the used projects will be visible in the import dialog. You can then decide if you want to include them in the import.
- If a main or used project you are importing already exists in the server, it will be skipped and not imported. You can see the results of the import action in the Notification Window: [ATTACHMENT filename='server_projects_notification.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

This opens the <strong>Import Project to the Server </strong>dialog:<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="import_project_to_server.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Importing server project file" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></span></li><li>In the <strong>Action </strong>column, you see the action that is performed with the project once you click <strong>Import</strong>. Possible actions include:<ul><li><strong>Add to Server</strong> – the project is added to the server.<ul><li>If the action is <strong>Add to Server</strong>, you can <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">modify the <strong>Name on Server </strong>column to choose a new name for the imported project.</span> </li><li>If you want to skip certain projects, you can change the <strong>Add to Server </strong>action to <strong>Skip</strong>.</li></ul></li><li><strong>Skip </strong>– the server project is skipped.</li><li><strong>Skip (Project Removed)</strong> – the project is removed. You must use Data Manager to either delete it completely from the server or restore the deleted version.</li><li><strong>Skip (Project Exists)</strong> – the project already exists on the server and cannot be <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e5075efb-8b1f-459b-8bfd-e58eb1442197">imported</ac:inline-comment-marker>. If you still need to upload the <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">.szip version of the project to Teamwork Cloud, use the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Updating a server project with local changes" /><ac:link-body><strong>Update from Local Project </strong></ac:link-body></ac:link>functionality.</span></li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">To choose the category for the newly added project, select <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="three_dots_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2022xR1" ri:content-title="Adding projects to Teamwork Cloud" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> at the bottom of the dialog. This opens the <strong>Select Category </strong>dialog</span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">:<br /><br /><ac:image ac:width="461"><ri:attachment ri:filename="server_project_category_selection.jpg"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Importing server project file" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Choose the category and click <strong>OK </strong>to return to the <strong>Import Project to the Server </strong>dialog.<br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Click <strong>Import</strong>.<strong> </strong>The project will be added as a new server project.<br /><br /></span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4fdaf3a5-e1fc-4b1c-aac0-0eb146562935"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Please note:</strong></p><ul><li>If the project you are importing was saved together with used projects, all of the used projects will be visible in the import dialog. You can then decide if you want to include them in the import.</li><li>If a main or used project you are importing already exists in the server, it will be skipped and not imported.<br />You can see the results of the import action in the Notification Window:<br /><br /><ac:image ac:height="235"><ri:attachment ri:filename="server_projects_notification.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Importing server project file" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=173573740 space=MD2024xR1 version=7 -->
## PAGE 00599: Importing Teamwork Cloud projects to the 3DEXPERIENCE platform

- page_id: `173573740`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/173573740/Importing+Teamwork+Cloud+projects+to+the+3DEXPERIENCE+platform
- version_number: 7
- version_date: `2024-06-18T19:08:46.566+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

After exporting Teamwork Cloud projects to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using server project file format' space=''], you can import themto the**3D**EXPERIENCE platform. You can alsouse .szip files to update the projects already stored on the platform.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Moving projects to the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform by using the server project file format.

##### Prerequisites

To be able to import projects to the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform using the server project file format, the exported Teamwork Cloud projects must meet the following criteria:

- Exported projects cannot have cyclic project usages. A cyclic project usage is the kind of usage where a project uses itself through other used projects and forms a usage loop. You can use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Usage Map' space='TWCloud2024xR1'] application to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Identifying suspicious usages' space='TWCloud2024xR1'] .
- Exported projects cannot have version conflicts in project usages. A version conflict occurs when a project uses different versions of the same project directly and through other used projects. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Troubleshooting used project version conflicts' space=''].
- Exported projects cannot have recovered elements. A recovered element is an indication of a dangling reference. It means that a project has a reference to the element in a used project which can no longer be found. In this case, a modeling tool recovers the missing element to maintain model integrity. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing unresolved references' space=''].
- Standard/system profiles used by exported projects (including used projects) must be imported to the 3D EXPERIENCE platform. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding standard profiles to the 3DEXPERIENCE platform' space='']Learn to add standard/system profiles to the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform . In addition, used projects must be committed with the same modeling tool version that you use to work with your main project
- If a server exchange file is exported without used projects, all of these used projects must be already imported to the 3D EXPERIENCE platform (the versions of all used projects must match the used project versions on the platform).

##### Importing Teamwork Cloud projects

To import/update projects from a server project file

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Logging in to 3DEXPERIENCE platform' space='']Log in to the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform .
2. In the main menu, select File > Import From > Server Project File .
3. In the open dialog, select the .szip file you want to import and click the **Open** button. The **Import Project to the Server** dialog opens. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] If the imported project is new, the action displayed in the **Action** column is **Add to Sever**. If the project already exists on the platform, the action is **Update**, meaning the existing project will be updated.If you import a new project, you can manually change its name in the **Name on Server** column
4. If the project already exists on the 3D EXPERIENCE platform, optionally change the revision that you want to update:
  1. In the Revision column, click [ATTACHMENT filename='select_revision.png'] .
  2. In the Select Revision dialog, select the revision you want to update and click OK .
5. Click the Import button.

When the import is complete, the **Notification Window** opens with the message that projects were imported successfully.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span>After exporting Teamwork Cloud projects to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using server project file format" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[server project file (.szip) format]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, you can import them</span><span> to the </span><strong>3D</strong><span>EXPERIENCE platform. You can also<span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"> use .szip files to update the projects already stored on the platform.</span></span></p><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="moving_projects_to_platform.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 40.0px;"><span>Moving projects to the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform by using the server project file format.</span></h6><h3><span>Prerequisites</span></h3><p style="text-align: left;">To be able to import projects to the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform using the server project file format, the exported Teamwork Cloud projects must meet the following criteria: </p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li>Exported projects cannot have cyclic project usages.<br />A cyclic project usage is the kind of usage where a project <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">uses itself through other used projects and forms a usage loop.</span> You can use the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloud2024xR1" ri:content-title="Resource Usage Map" /></ac:link> application to <ac:link ac:anchor="Cyclic usages"><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloud2024xR1" ri:content-title="Identifying suspicious usages" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[identify cyclic project usages]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.<br /><br /></li><li>Exported projects<strong> </strong>cannot have version conflicts in project usages.<br />A version conflict occurs when a project <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">uses different versions of the same project directly and through other used projects. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Troubleshooting used project version conflicts" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn to troubleshoot used project version conflicts]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.<br /><br /></span></li><li>Exported projects cannot have recovered elements.<br />A recovered element is an indication of a dangling reference. It means that a project has a reference to the element <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">in a used project which can no longer be found. In this case, a modeling tool recovers the missing element to maintain model integrity. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing unresolved references" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn to resolve dangling references]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.<br /><br /></span></li><li>Standard/system profiles used by exported projects (including used projects) must be imported to the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Adding standard profiles to the 3DEXPERIENCE platform" /><ac:link-body>Learn to add standard/system profiles to the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform</ac:link-body></ac:link>.<br />In addition, used projects must be committed with the same modeling tool version that you use to work with your main project<br /><br /></li><li>If a server exchange file is exported without used projects, all of these used projects must be already imported to the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform (the versions of all used projects must match the used project versions on the platform).</li></ul><p><br /></p><h3>Importing Teamwork Cloud projects</h3><p><br /></p><p>To import/update projects from a server project file</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Logging in to 3DEXPERIENCE platform" /><ac:link-body>Log in to the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>In the main menu, select <strong style="text-align: left;"><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">File</span></strong><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"> &gt; </span><strong style="text-align: left;"><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Import From</span></strong><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"> &gt; </span><strong style="text-align: left;"><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Server Project File</span></strong><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">In the open dialog, select the .szip file you want to import and click the <strong>Open</strong> button. The <strong>Import Project to the Server</strong> dialog opens.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="importing_project_to_server.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8cc84f47-ecc2-457a-b418-452ed36aeee3"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>If the imported project is new, the action displayed in the <strong>Action</strong> column is <strong>Add to Sever</strong>. If the project already exists on the platform, the action is <strong>Update</strong>, meaning the existing project will be updated.</li><li>If you import a new project, you can manually change its name in the <strong>Name on Server</strong> column</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>If the project already exists on the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform, optionally change the revision that you want to update:<ol><li>In the <strong>Revision</strong> column, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="13"><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_revision.png" /></ac:image>.</li><li>In the <strong>Select Revision</strong> dialog, select the revision you want to update and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol></li><li>Click the <strong>Import</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br />When the import is complete, the <strong>Notification Window</strong> opens with the message that projects were imported successfully.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169677460 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00600: Importing the content of a used project into your project

- page_id: `169677460`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169677460/Importing+the+content+of+a+used+project+into+your+project
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-10-01T10:43:50.963+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud > Managing project usages
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To import the content of a used project into your project, you must have an **Administer Resources** [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Permissions' space='TWCloud185'].

If you no longer need to keep a part of the model in the used project, you can import the content of that used project into your project. After the import, all the elements of the used project are copied to the main project. The inner structure of the used project is included in the project as a package.

Standard/system profiles, such as *UML_Standard_Profile.mdzip*, cannot be imported.

To import the contents of a used project into your project

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Locking model for edit' space='MD2024xR1'] .
2. On the main menu, click Collaborate > Project Usages > Project Usages .
3. Select the used project and click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. Shortcut menuThis step can be done via shortcut menu. Right-click project and then select **Import**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. In the Import dialog, choose how to import the elements of the used project: with new IDs or with old.
5. If you receive a message asking to commit changes made in your project before the import, click **Yes** and wait while the import completes. This may take some time. NoteThe imported project no longer appears in the **Project Usages** dialog.
6. Click OK .

The imported project also disappears from the package *Project Usages* and a new package named after the imported project appears under the root package in the Model Browser. If the imported project uses other projects, these projects will be directly used in your project.

After it is imported to the contents of your project, a used project will still exist in the Teamwork Cloud (TWCloud) repository.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
