After the import, all the elements of the used project are copied to the main project. The inner structure of the used project is included in the project as a package.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5a3d4aa3-1caa-4487-8226-47573b3c0e12"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Standard/system profiles, such as <em>UML_Standard_Profile.mdzip</em>, cannot be imported.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p>To import the contents of a used project into your project</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Locking model for edit" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Lock the used project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>On the main menu, click <strong> Collaborate</strong> &gt; Project Usages &gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Select the used project and click the <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="19"><ri:attachment ri:filename="import_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ac0e76a5-8ae3-4829-8bdb-a3a08b2944bf"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Shortcut menu</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>This step can be done via shortcut menu. Right-click project and then select <strong>Import</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_usages_import.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Importing the content of a used project into your project" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li>In the <strong>Import</strong> dialog, choose how to import the elements of the used project: with new IDs or with old.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">If you receive a message asking to commit changes made in your project before the import, click <strong>Yes</strong> and wait while the import completes. This may take some time.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="17f6ab1e-9cfb-4f41-9fdf-79440af6a9a2"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The imported project no longer appears in the <strong>Project Usages</strong> dialog.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p>The imported project also disappears from the package <em>Project Usages</em> and a new package named after the imported project appears under the root package in the Model Browser. If the imported project uses other projects, these projects will be directly used in your project.</p><p>After it is imported to the contents of your project, a used project will still exist in the Teamwork Cloud (TWCloud) repository.</p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169677711 space=MD2024xR1 version=3 -->
## PAGE 00601: Importing the content of a used projects in 3DEXPERIENCE platform

- page_id: `169677711`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169677711/Importing+the+content+of+a+used+projects+in+3DEXPERIENCE+platform
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-02-04T15:05:56.630+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform > Managing project usages via 3DEXPERIENCE platform
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If you no longer need to keep a part of the model in the used project, you can import the content of that used project into your project. After the import, all the elements of the used project are copied to the main project. The inner structure of the used project is included in the project as a package.

Standard/system profiles, such as *UML_Standard_Profile.mdzip*, cannot be imported.

To import the contents of a used project into your project

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Locking models in 3DEXPERIENCE' space='MD2024xR1'] .
2. In the main menu, click Collaborate > Project Usages > Project Usages .
3. In the **Project Usages** dialog, do one of the following:
  - Select a used project and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
  - Right-click a used project and select Import . [ATTACHMENT filename='importing_used_projects.png']
4. In the Import dialog, do one of the following:
  - Click the **Import with New IDs** button to create new IDs for the imported elements in the main project. This way, element IDs are not duplicated, ensuring the main project remains intact. However, if the elements of the used project are used in other projects, choosing **Import with****New IDs** may break those references. Importing elements with new IDs is highly recommended as it is the safest option regarding project consistency.
  - Click the **Import with Old IDs** button to import the elements with their existing IDs, which can be useful if the elements are used in multiple other projects. However, this may cause duplicate element IDs, resulting in the main project being corrupted. To proceed with the import using the old IDs, click **Yes**in the question****dialog, acknowledging that the procedure might corrupt the data of the main project.
5. If you receive a message asking to commit changes made in your project before the import, click **Yes** and wait while the import completes. This may take some time.
6. Click OK to close the dialog .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><span>If you no longer need to keep a part of the model in the used project, you can import the content of that used project into your project. After the import, all the elements of the used project are copied to the main project. The inner structure of the used project is included in the project as a package.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5a3d4aa3-1caa-4487-8226-47573b3c0e12"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Standard/system profiles, such as <em>UML_Standard_Profile.mdzip</em>, cannot be imported.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To import the contents of a used project into your project</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Locking models in 3DEXPERIENCE" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Lock the used project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>In the main menu, click <strong> Collaborate</strong> &gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong> &gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <strong>Project Usages</strong> dialog, do one of the following:</p><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Select a used project and click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="19"><ri:attachment ri:filename="import_button.png" /></ac:image>.</p></li><li>Right-click a used project and select <strong>Import</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="importing_used_projects.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li></ul></li><li>In the <strong>Import</strong> dialog, do one of the following: <ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click the <strong>Import with New IDs</strong> button to create new IDs for the imported elements in the main project. This way, element IDs are not duplicated, ensuring the main project remains intact. However, if the elements of the used project are used in other projects, choosing <strong>Import with </strong><strong>New IDs</strong> may break those references.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f73d9e1f-6144-49a9-b69c-3c28a0861585"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Importing elements with new IDs is highly recommended as it is the safest option regarding project consistency.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click the <strong>Import with Old IDs</strong> button to import the elements with their existing IDs, which can be useful if the elements are used in multiple other projects. However, this may cause duplicate element IDs, resulting in the main project being corrupted. To proceed with the import using the old IDs, click <strong>Yes </strong>in the question<strong> </strong>dialog, acknowledging that the procedure might corrupt the data of the main project.</p></li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">If you receive a message asking to commit changes made in your project before the import, click <strong>Yes</strong> and wait while the import completes. This may take some time.</p></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> to close the dialog<strong>.</strong></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673370 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00602: Improving performance

- page_id: `169673370`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673370/Improving+performance
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-26T13:46:46.636+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Getting started
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1427324068

1427324070

1427324069

INLINE

When working with very large models or a number of diagrams at a time, the modeling tool performance may decrease. To increase efficiency, we suggest the following solutions:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Memory allocation' space=''] (java heap size). If you want to make sure you have a sufficient amount of memory allocated to your modeling tool, use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Memory monitor' space=''] to track memory usage.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Active validation period' space=''] and/or do not keep unused diagrams open.
- Split a project into several separate project files, and load only the files that you work with. This may help only if your project contains several parts with minimal dependencies between them. For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project partitioning' space=''].
- Use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Memory monitor' space=''] to free unused memory.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Background Task Manager' space=''] that run too long.
- Set the limit of open diagrams in the Environment Options dialog ( Options > Environment > General > General > Diagram Tabs Limit ).

1427324067

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Getting started' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing environment options' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing and Selecting Perspective' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Configuration files' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Disabling inbound network activity' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Support_obsolete' space='NMDOC']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2a61e022-7559-4d3f-b56c-3dee6baa15de"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1427324068</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="163095b8-9d1f-4648-bcc3-550ea595c28e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1427324070</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7d690d15-ffd8-4de9-b158-88970fb6091a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1427324069</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dea99695-1d3c-4cd0-93b9-438f4b087bb8"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>When working with very large models or a number of diagrams at a time, the modeling tool performance may decrease. To increase efficiency, we suggest the following solutions:</p><ul><li><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Memory allocation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Increase memory allocation value]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (java heap size). If you want to make sure you have a sufficient amount of memory allocated to your modeling tool, use the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Memory monitor" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[memory monitor]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to track memory usage.</p></li><li><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Active validation period" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Increase an active validation period]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and/or do not keep unused diagrams open.</p></li><li><p>Split a project into several separate project files, and load only the files that you work with. This may help only if your project contains several parts with minimal dependencies between them. For more information, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Project partitioning" /></ac:link>.</p></li><li><p>Use <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Memory monitor" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Garbage Collector]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to free unused memory.</p></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Background Task Manager" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cancel the background tasks]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> that run too long.</li><li>Set the limit of open diagrams in the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog (<strong>Options</strong> &gt;<strong> Environment</strong> &gt; <strong>General</strong> &gt; <strong>General </strong>&gt; <strong>Diagram Tabs Limit</strong>). </li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bee057bf-13ac-4c87-a9c6-d0ef312493f8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1427324067</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Getting started" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Customizing environment options" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Customizing and Selecting Perspective" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Configuration files" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Disabling inbound network activity" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Support_obsolete" ri:space-key="NMDOC" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675160 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00603: Include

- page_id: `169675160`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675160/Include
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-12-19T15:43:52.296+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Include relationship is used when there are common parts of the behavior among two or more Use Cases. Each common part is then extracted to a separate Use Case, to be included by all base Use Cases having this part in common. The Include relationship is represented by a base Use Case to an inclusion Use Case.

An Include relationship between Use Cases indicates that an instance of the including Use Case also contains the behavior as specified by the included Use Case.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of Include relationship

In the example above, the*Make Item Reservation*Use Case is a base Use Case. It includes the*Find Item*Use Case. The*Make Item Reservation*Use Case is the including Use Case and the*Find Item*Use Case is the included Use Case.

To use the Include relationship

1. Create two or more Use Cases by dragging and dropping the Use Case element [ATTACHMENT filename='Screen Shot 2017-06-16 at 1.28.57 PM.png'] onto your diagram pane.
2. Click on the Include relationship [ATTACHMENT filename='Screen Shot 2017-06-16 at 1.30.51 PM.png'] and connect it between two Use Cases.
3. The result should look like the aforementioned example.

You can format comment symbol properties in the **Symbol Properties** dialog.

#### TIP: More Information

More Information

For more information about symbol representation properties, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MD2024xR1'].

You can specify a comment in the Include Specification Window. In the same window, you can find the description of each Include property in the description area of the Specification Window.

#### TIP: More Information

More Information

For more information about the Specification window usage, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1'].

For more information about specifying property values, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing property values' space='MD2024xR1'].

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case diagram' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Behavior' space='MD2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The Include relationship is used when there are common parts of the behavior among two or more Use Cases. Each common part is then extracted to a separate Use Case, to be included by all base Use Cases having this part in common. The Include relationship is represented by a base Use Case to an inclusion Use Case.</p><p>An Include relationship between Use Cases indicates that an instance of the including Use Case also contains the behavior as specified by the included Use Case.</p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:width="600"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Screen Shot 2017-06-16 at 11.35.50 AM.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Include" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>Example of Include relationship</h6><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);font-size: 14.0px;">In the example above, the </span><em>Make Item Reservation</em><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);font-size: 14.0px;"> Use Case is a base Use Case. It includes the </span><em>Find Item</em><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);font-size: 14.0px;"> Use Case. The </span><em>Make Item Reservation</em><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);font-size: 14.0px;"> Use Case is the including Use Case and the </span><em>Find Item</em><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);font-size: 14.0px;"> Use Case is the included Use Case.</span></p><p><br /></p><p>To use the Include relationship</p><hr /><ol><li>Create two or more Use Cases by dragging and dropping the Use Case element <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="90"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Screen Shot 2017-06-16 at 1.28.57 PM.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Include" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>onto your diagram pane.</li><li>Click on the Include relationship <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="80"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Screen Shot 2017-06-16 at 1.30.51 PM.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Include" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and connect it between two Use Cases.</li><li>The result should look like the aforementioned example.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>You can format comment symbol properties in the <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="19aa51b2-b4f6-4ae5-b87a-0d31db6145b3"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">More Information</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">For more information about symbol representation properties, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Formatting symbols" /></ac:link>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>You can specify a comment in the Include Specification Window. In the same window, you can find the description of each Include property in the description area of the Specification Window.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="02202a08-01b8-4664-b9d1-3104c6e4ecc5"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">More Information</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">For more information about the Specification window usage, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">For more information about specifying property values, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Editing property values" /></ac:link>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ff1aef2b-4429-4784-bb8f-aa49ccc1bf16"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Formatting symbols" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Use Case diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Behavior" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169678233 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00604: Including external images

- page_id: `169678233`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678233/Including+external+images
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-01-05T04:20:37.216+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Helper tools > Image tool
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

2050285233

2050285245

2050285231

**On this page**

43

2050285234

You can include images from an external source in a report. The supported image types are *BMP*, *JPG*, *WBMP*, *GIF*, and *PNG*.

###### **$image.include(location)**

Include an external image from a local file or *URL* in a report.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | location | java.lang.String | An image’s location. The location format can be either a URL or a local file. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image included in a report |

For example:

```text

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The supported image types are <em>BMP</em>, <em>JPG</em>, <em>WBMP</em>, <em>GIF</em>, and <em>PNG</em>.</p><h4><strong>$image.include(location)</strong></h4><p>Include an external image from a local file or <em>URL</em> in a report.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s)</td><td>location</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>An image’s location. <br />The location format <br />can be either a URL <br />or a local file.</td></tr><tr><td>Return</td><td>-</td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image</td><td>An image included <br />in a report</td></tr></tbody></table><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bd86fd2e-acc9-4364-82e6-34794a984140"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$image.include("C:\Users\username\images\logo.gif")
````html
$image.include("http://www.magicdraw.com/images/product_boxes/MD.gif")]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675689 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00605: Incompatible properties and related elements

- page_id: `169675689`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675689/Incompatible+properties+and+related+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2016-04-25T17:34:49.760+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Refactoring > Converting Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

After the conversion, a converted element owns the same compatible specification properties and is connected with the same related elements as the original element was connected. The incompatible properties of the original element are lost, that is, are deleted from the project.

Incompatible means that the converted element can NOT own particular properties or can NOT be associated with particular related elements of the original element.

For example, let’s convert the component to package. See an example in the following figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

As you see, the component has the assigned port. According to UML guidelines, a package cannot own ports. So, in this case, ports are incompatible with the packages. After the conversion, the package, that is, a converted element, has no assigned port. The port is deleted from the project.

To prevent from losing model data, you always get a message with a warning that incompatible properties will be lost after the element conversion.

##### Changes after conversion

###### Converting a property to a port

If the property visibility is *private*, *protected,* or *package* , then after the conversion visibility of the created port will be set to *public*.

#### PANEL: On this page

On this page

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>After the conversion, a converted element owns the same compatible specification properties and is connected with the same related elements as the original element was connected. The incompatible properties of the original element are lost, that is, are deleted from the project.</p><p>Incompatible means that the converted element can NOT own particular properties or can NOT be associated with particular related elements of the original element.</p><p>For example, let’s convert the component to package. See an example in the following figure.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="Example of conversion when original and converted elements has incompatible property (Port)" ac:title="Example of conversion when original and converted elements has incompatible property (Port)"><ri:attachment ri:filename="converting_elements_incompatible_elements.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Incompatible properties and related elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>As you see, the component has the assigned port. According to UML guidelines, a package cannot own ports. So, in this case, ports are incompatible with the packages. After the conversion, the package, that is, a converted element, has no assigned port. The port is deleted from the project.</p><p>To prevent from losing model data, you always get a message with a warning that incompatible properties will be lost after the element conversion.</p><h3>Changes after conversion</h3><h4>Converting a property to a port</h4><p>If the property visibility is <em>private</em>, <em>protected,</em> or <em>package</em> , then after the conversion visibility of the created port will be set to <em>public</em>.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="67b51616-1257-45d8-b179-df6555185bfc"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">On this page</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2fc359cb-dee4-4291-9163-fcd677e10dd5" /> </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673630 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00606: Indexing

- page_id: `169673630`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673630/Indexing
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-08-30T08:33:44.674+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Searching
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Model elements representation text indexing allows users to find elements in the model more quickly. It starts automatically when a Teamwork Cloud project is opened for the first time; you can track the progress in the status bar (see the image below). The index is automatically updated when the model changes.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The indexing progress shown in the status bar.

Please note that indexing may take some time depending on the project size. The more elements the project contains, the longer it takes to index the model elements representation text.

If representation text building is in progress, searches performed in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting elements' space='MD2024xR1'] dialog may be slower. In this case, a warning appears next to the search progress bar (see an image below).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If a search does not return expected results, we recommend that you rebuild the model elements representation text index (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Indexing' space='MD2024xR1'] below).

The**Rebuild Text Index** action is available in the Expert mode only.

instructionsTo rebuild the text index

- In the main menu , click Tools > Rebuild Text Index . [ATTACHMENT filename='rebuild_text_index_dialog.png']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673672 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00607: Indexing of unloaded projects

- page_id: `169673672`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673672/Indexing+of+unloaded+projects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-04-27T14:18:44.939+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Project partitioning > Decomposing model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Proxy

628699399

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Proxy

628699401

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Proxy

628699400

If you have a large project and, due to performance issues, some used projects are not loaded, you may use indexing to work with the project smoothly.Indexing can be considered as an intermediate form of work, falling between working with a fully loaded used project and working with a not-loaded used project.

#### INFO: Proxy

Proxy

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Maintaining decomposed model integrity' space='MD2024xR1']true

When a used project is unloaded/not loaded in the project, only proxies of necessary elements are shown in place of the used project. Using indexing allows you to show more proxy elements from the unloaded project. These proxies can be used as normal model elements in the project using them without loading the used project. Keeping the used project not loaded saves a considerable amount of computer resources. If you ever need full information from that used project, you can load it at any time.

A typical case where this functionality is useful is outlined below:

A large software library is used in a project. Only certain classes are important for the main project: some library classes are set as types of properties in the model classes, some model classes inherit from the library classes, etc. In this case, the sub-elements of the library classes (their properties and methods) are not important, and we can index classes in the library and keep it not loaded in the main project. Indexing will still show all the classes in the main project from the library.

To specify elements for indexing

1. Open the used project (that is, not loaded) separately from the using project.
2. From the Options menu, select Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
3. Select the Indexing property group. [ATTACHMENT filename='project_options_indexing.png']
4. Select the **Common Indexing** option. This enables indexing of the used project and determines what information will be indexed.
5. When common indexing is chosen, classifiers and their inheritance relationships will be indexed. If you want more elements to be indexed, select the **Custom Indexing** option and fine-tune which element types (properties, methods, etc) should be indexed. The more elements you select, the more elements will be accessible in the project using them as proxies. However, your gains in performance from the used project unloading will also diminish. Thus, a balance is needed when customizing the index. It is usually better to use the common indexing variant. Click **OK**.
6. In the project using the indexed project, go to the options of the used indexed project (from the main menu, select **Options** > **Project Usages**) and select the **Use Index** check box.

628699398

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Maintaining decomposed model integrity' space='MD2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b67429c1-fe33-441f-95d4-891a75370dfe"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">628699399</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d5323c54-ea67-42f4-b33b-56df6d2842e0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">628699401</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5a7a20e1-4dc1-4bd9-bb11-8b409706345d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">628699400</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="291b13b2-a9df-4d8c-9830-9a083f16b245">If you have a large project and, due to performance issues, some used projects are not loaded, you may use indexing to work with the project smoothly. </ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="291b13b2-a9df-4d8c-9830-9a083f16b245">Indexing</ac:inline-comment-marker> can be considered as an intermediate form of work, falling between working with a fully loaded used project and working with a not-loaded used project.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f1c1ae84-92a9-410a-9e4a-0cda821f843b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Proxy</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt-include" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8e66c221-e641-4588-b01e-0fca83433f19"><ac:parameter ac:name=""><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Maintaining decomposed model integrity" /></ac:link></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="nopanel">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>When a used project is unloaded/not loaded in the project, only proxies of <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c07af6fa-3fd0-4554-a664-3e5b51394736">necessary element</ac:inline-comment-marker>s are shown in place of the used project. Using indexing allows you to show more proxy elements from the unloaded project. These proxies can be used as normal model elements in the project using them without loading the used project. Keeping the used project not loaded saves a considerable amount of computer resources.  If you ever need full information from that used project, you can load it at any time.</p><p>A typical case where this functionality is useful is outlined below:</p><p>A large software library is used in a project. Only certain classes are important for the main project: some library classes are set as types of properties in the model classes, some model classes inherit from the library classes, etc. In this case, the sub-elements of the library classes (their properties and methods) are not important, and we can index classes in the library and keep it not loaded in the main project. Indexing will still show all the classes in the main project from the library.</p><p><br /></p><p>To specify elements for <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="108124cd-4ada-4792-92e5-e3120682817a">indexing</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><hr /><ol><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="77374505-fd83-4671-b796-37de755e139c">Open the used project (that is, not loaded) separately from the using project.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li>From the <strong>Options</strong> menu, select <strong>Project</strong>. The <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select the <strong>Indexing</strong> property group.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_options_indexing.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Indexing of unloaded projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><p>Select the <strong>Common Indexing</strong> option. The more elements you select, the more elements will be accessible in the project using them as proxies. However, your gains in performance from the used project unloading will also diminish. Thus, a balance is needed when customizing the index. It is usually better to use the common indexing variant.</p><p>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</p></li><li><p>In the project using the indexed project, go to the options of the used indexed project (from the main menu, select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong>) and select the <strong>Use Index</strong> check box. </p></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d3bdc33-2e2d-4238-8f19-7a0e4fd3b85c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">628699398</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Maintaining decomposed model integrity" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675164 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00608: Information Flow

- page_id: `169675164`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675164/Information+Flow
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:07.751+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Information Flow specifies that one or more information items circulate from its sources to its targets. Information flows require an “information channel” for transmitting information items from the source to the destination.

An information channel is represented in various ways, depending on the nature of its sources and targets. It can be represented by connectors, links, associations, or dependencies. For example, if the source and destination are parts in composite structure diagrams such as a collaboration, the information channel is likely to be represented by a connector between them. Or, if the source and target are objects (which are a kind of Instance Specification), they can be represented by a link that joins the two, and so on. The Information Flow and the information item notation are added. You can draw them using the Information Flows toolbar in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MD2024xR1'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Composite Structure diagram' space='MD2024xR1'].

##### Setting an Information Flow

Information Flows can be set on [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Association' space='MD2024xR1']in the Class Diagram and on [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connector' space='MD2024xR1']in the Composite Structure Diagrams.

To set an Information Flow

1. Create an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Association' space='MD2024xR1'] ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connector' space='MD2024xR1']) relationship between [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MD2024xR1'] ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part' space='MD2024xR1']). Make sure the properties for displaying a Conveyed Information on relationships are selected.To display Conveyed Information:Right-click the Association (Connector) and choose the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='MD2024xR1']** command.In the open dialog, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Property group toolbar' space='MD2024xR1'] to **Expert** or **All**.Set the **Show Conveyed Information A** and **Show Conveyed Information B** check boxes to *true*.
2. Specify a Conveyed Information in the following ways: 
- Drag the Class or Information Item on an Association (Connector) or Information Flow and select the Set as Conveyed Information command. 
- Select an Association (Connector) relationship and from the smart manipulator toolbar click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. 
- In the diagram palette, select the needed Conveyed Information (e.g., Class) and drag it onto the Information Flow on the diagram pane.
3. In the **New Conveyed Information** dialog, specify **Conveyed Information**, **Information Flow**, **Direction** and click **OK**. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Conveyed informationOne Information Flow may have several Conveyed Information items. If a new Information Flow is set on a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connector' space='MD2024xR1'] that is represented in other diagrams, the new Conveyed Information is displayed only on the diagram on which you set it.You can reverse the direction of the [Information Flow](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Information+Flow) on [Connectors](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Connector). [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Reversing Information Flow direction' space='MD2024xR1']>]]> An Information Flow is set.

##### Changing the arrow notation visibility

You can choose whether you want to hide the arrow notation on the realizing elements. The **Hide Information Flow If Conveyed Information Is Missing** [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space='MD2024xR1'] controls the arrow visibility.

To change the arrow notation visibility on realizing element when the Conveyed Information is hidden or unspecified

1. From the modeling tool main menu, choose Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. In the dialog, go to General > Diagrams .
3. In the Diagrams property group, find the Hide Information Flow If Conveyed Information Is Missing option and set it to true.
4. Click OK when you are done.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Reversing Information Flow direction' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Information Item' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>An Information Flow specifies that one or more information items circulate from its sources to its targets. Information flows require an “information channel” for transmitting information items from the source to the destination.</p><p>An information channel is represented in various ways, depending on the nature of its sources and targets. It can be represented by connectors, links, associations, or dependencies. For example, if the source and destination are parts in composite structure diagrams such as a collaboration, the information channel is likely to be represented by a connector between them. Or, if the source and target are objects (which are a kind of Instance Specification), they can be represented by a link that joins the two, and so on. The Information Flow and the information item notation are added. You can draw them using the Information Flows toolbar in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Class diagram" /></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram" /></ac:link>.</p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Setting an Information Flow</h3><p>Information Flows can be set on <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Association" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Associations ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>in the Class Diagram and on <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Connector" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Connectors ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>in the Composite Structure Diagrams.</p><p>To set an Information Flow</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Create an <ac:link><ri:page ## PAGE 00609: Information Flow has no Conveyed Information
````

- page_id: `169676939`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169676939/Information+Flow+has+no+Conveyed+Information
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-04-18T17:00:40.934+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Validation > UML validation rules > UML completeness constraints rules
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Abbreviation**

IFHNCI[1]

**Description**

This validation rule checks if there are any Information Flows without Conveyed Information.

**Severity**

warning

**Constrained Element**

Information Flow

**Solvers**

- **Select Conveyed Information** - opens the Select Conveyed Information dialog where you can choose the Conveyed Information for the validated Information Flow.

**Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An Information Flow has no Conveyed Information. The error is resolved by specifying Conveyed Information via the **Select Conveyed Information**solver.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Abbreviation</strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">IFHNCI</span><span class="error" style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">[1]</span></p><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>This validation rule checks if there are any <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Information Flows without Conveyed Information.</span></p><p><strong>Severity</strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">warning</span></p><p><strong>Constrained Element</strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Information Flow</span></p><p><strong>Solvers</strong></p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><strong>Select Conveyed Information</strong> - <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">opens the Select Conveyed Information dialog where you can choose the Conveyed Information for the validated Information Flow.</span></span></li></ul><p><strong>Example</strong></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Information_Flow_has_no_Conveyed_Information.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Information Flow has no Conveyed Information" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">An Information Flow has no Conveyed Information. The error is resolved by specifying Conveyed Information via the <strong>Select Conveyed Information </strong>solver.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675168 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00610: Information Item

- page_id: `169675168`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675168/Information+Item
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-03-19T13:21:52.453+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Quick search

633879781

INLINE

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Quick search

633879783

INLINE

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Quick search

633879782

INLINE

The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Information Flow' space='MD2024xR1'] package provides mechanisms for specifying the exchange of information between entities in a system at a high level of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Navigating between different levels of abstraction' space='MD2024xR1']. Information Flows describe the circulation of information in a system in a general manner. They do not specify the nature of the information or the mechanisms by which this information is conveyed (message passing, signal, common data store, parameter of operation, etc.). Also, they do not specify sequences or any control conditions. It is intended that representation and realization [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Link' space='MD2024xR1'] will be able to specify which model element implements the specified information flow, and how the information will be conveyed while modeling in detail.

An Information Item is an abstraction of all information that can be exchanged between objects. It is a kind of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying internal structure on structured classifiers' space='MD2024xR1'] intended to represent information in a very abstract way, one which cannot be instantiated.

In a classifier, the Information Item can be represented as a name inside a rectangle. The black triangle icon at the top of the rectangle indicates that it is an Information Item.

Information Items, or any conveyed classifiers, can be displayed on any relationships.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of an Information Item named Wage

One purpose of Information Items is to enable you to define preliminary models before making detailed modeling decisions on types or structures. Another purpose of Information Items and Information Flows is to abstract complex models by using a less specific more general representation of the information exchanged between entities of a system.

To create an Information Item

1. In a model or diagram, select a relationship or create a new one.
2. Set a relationship as an Information Flow, using one of the following methods:
  - Click a relationship. The smart manipulator opens. Select the New Conveyed Information command. [ATTACHMENT filename='smart_manipulator_new_conveyed_information.png']
  - Open the relationship [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1'] . Select the Conveyed Information property group and click the Add button.
3. The New Conveyed Information dialog opens. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='open_dialog_button.png'] next to the Conveyed Information text box.
4. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting elements' space='MD2024xR1'] dialog opens. Click the Creation Mode button to enable the creation of new elements.
5. Select the package where you want to create a new element.
6. Click the **Create** button and select **Information Item**element in the list. Quick searchStart typing the element name to find the element quicker.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
7. The Specification window of the newly created element opens. Type the element name and specify other properties according to your needs.
8. After you finish, close all the dialogs one by one. The Information Item element is created.

633879780

INLINE

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Information Flow' space='MD2024xR1']

Click the Creation Mode button to enable the creation of new elements.</li><li>Select the package where you want to create a new element.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click the <strong>Create</strong> button and select <strong>Information Item </strong>element in the list.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8e069926-c278-4dfe-b6b2-fa4ee690c03c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Quick search</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Start typing the element name to find the element quicker.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_information_item.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Information Item" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>The Specification window of the newly created element opens. ## PAGE 00612: Inheritance tab

- page_id: `169673283`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673283/Inheritance+tab
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-04-22T07:32:37.653+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Getting started > Understanding the user interface > Model Browser
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1425598369

1425598371

1425598370

INLINE

The Inheritance tab represents classifiers (e.g., packages, data types, and stereotypes) hierarchy within your project. Inheritance according to the UML Specification is shown using the generalization relationship.

If a model element has no generalization relationship, it will not be represented in the Inheritance tab.

To open a new Inheritance tree

1. Open the Containment , Structure , or Inheritance tab.
2. In the open tab toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='open_new_tab_button.png'] and then select Open in New Inheritance Tree .

You can change some Inheritance tab properties in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing environment options' space='MD2024xR1']**Environment Options** dialog in the **Browser** options group.

The Inheritance tab consists of the following two components:

- Inheritance tab toolbar
- Inheritance tree

##### Inheritance tab toolbar

The Inheritance tab toolbar contains the following buttons

| Button | Tooltip text | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Collapse All | Collapses all currently expanded elements in the Containment tree. |
|  | Collapse Selected Recursively | Collapses only selected node and all subnodes in that node. |
|  | Open in New Tree | Opens the contents of the selected element in a new tab of the Model Browser. The selected element becomes the root node of the new tree.You can choose to open elements in:New Containment TreeNew Structure TreeNew Inheritance Tree |
|  | Favorites | Opens the Favorites menu for choosing to do one of the following:Add a selected element to favoritesOpen the dialog for managing favorites Navigate to a desired favorite in the Containment tree |
|  | Quick Find | Opens the Quick Find dialog. |
|  | Options | Click to open the Options menu and then select:Invert Tree - inverts the tree hierarchy - shows the childelement at the top of the hierarchy.Show Applied Stereotypes - shows or hides the applied stereotypes next to the element name in the Containment tree. |

##### Working with model elements in the Inheritance tree

You can work with elements in the Inheritance tree using the commands available from the selected element's shortcut menu. The shortcut menu contains the following commands:

| Command | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Select in Containment Tree | Opens the Containment tab and selects the current element in the Containment tree. |
| Select in Structure Tree | Opens the Structure tab and the structured classifier is selected.This command is available for structured classifiers (SysML). |

1425598368

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='MD2024xR1']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675173 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00613: Initial Node

- page_id: `169675173`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675173/Initial+Node
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:08.185+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Initial node is a control node where flow starts when the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity' space='MD2024xR1'] is invoked. An Activity may have more than one Initial node.

You can specify an Initial node by changing its property values in the Initial node[Specification window](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Specification+window). Each property is described in the description area on this window.

You can format Initial Node symbol properties in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='MD2024xR1'].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>An Initial node is a control node where flow starts when the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Activity" /></ac:link> is invoked. An Activity may have more than one Initial node.</p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">You can specify an Initial node by changing its property values in the Initial node </span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Specification+window">Specification window</a><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. Each property is described in the description area on this window.</span></p><p>You can format Initial Node symbol properties in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Symbol Properties dialog" /></ac:link>.    </p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="initial_node.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Initial Node" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675416 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00614: Initial state

- page_id: `169675416`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675416/Initial+state
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-09T21:53:54.774+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > State Machine > Pseudo States
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Every object belongs to a particular state as soon as it is created. So, it is useful to explicitly show that particular state. A solid filled circle represents the initial state of an object. There can only be one initial state for an object. The initial state denotes the starting place for a transition, the target of which is a composite state.

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1']

The initial state denotes the starting place for a transition, the target of which is a composite state.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="36e20852-22dd-420b-8d4b-52b136b22981"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification Window]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f01b84d9-d9d1-4049-8315-15a6a05d850d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="a91c5c61-63c8-4aaa-bfa5-b56b64a56477"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Pseudo States" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169679163 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00615: Initializing a Custom Model

- page_id: `169679163`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169679163/Initializing+a+Custom+Model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-09-21T09:42:17.797+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > UML Profiling and DSL Guide > Domain Specific Language customization > Using Customization Data
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

With DSL customization, you can modify a model element to act according to your needs depending on the stereotype you apply.

###### Default values

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Tag' space='MD2024xR1'] have default values. Use the default values when you create the tag, making sure its definition has multiplicity greater than zero. In this case it is automatically created when applying a stereotype.

###### Stereotypes on relationship ends

At times, DSL requires applying [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MD2024xR1']to some elements after DSL relationship connection to those elements.

Use the following properties for this purpose:

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Apply to Source | Specifies stereotypes that must be applied to the source element of this relationship after connection. |
| Apply to Target | Specifies stereotypes that must be applied to the target element of this relationship after connection. |

#### INFO: Source and Target in an example

Source and Target in an example

“Source” and “Target” are respectively the “source” and “target” of a directed relationship.

The first connected element acts as a“source” for a non-directed relationship.

Example:

1. Create a stereotype «serve» and select the Dependency metaclass.
2. Create two stereotypes «main» and «servant» and select the Element metaclass.
3. Create a customization element.
4. In the customization [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1'] , specify the following property values:
  1. for the Customization Target property, select the «serve» stereotype,
  2. for the Apply To Source property, select the «main» stereotype,
  3. for the Apply To Target property, select the «servant» stereotype.
5. Reopen the project. Draw the «serve» dependency from one element to another. The «main» and «servant» stereotypes are applied to these elements.

###### Required Generalization or Interface Realization

Sometimes DSL requires elements to be subtypes of a general abstract [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MD2024xR1']or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interface' space='MD2024xR1']. Use the **Super Types**property for this purpose. The **Super Types** property specifies types to be super types of the DSL element. The*Generalization*or*InterfaceRealization*(if using an interface) will be created in the model after applying the customized stereotype.

#### INFO: Example

Example

The following is an example of customization. Every JAVA class will be a subclass of the *Object*class. In other words, every Class marked with the «JAVAClass» stereotype is inherited from the Object.

1. Create a customization element.
2. In the customization Specification window, specify the **Customization Target**property as the «JAVAClass» stereotype.
3. In the customization Specification window, specify the **Super Types**property as the *Object* class.

#### PANEL: On this page

On this page

44

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Customization Data' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Customization Data' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Tag' space='MD2024xR1']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169679018 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00616: Initializing opposite association end

- page_id: `169679018`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169679018/Initializing+opposite+association+end
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-09-21T10:03:48.243+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > UML Profiling and DSL Guide > Working with Profiles > Creating Profiles
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If two stereotypes are connected with an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Association' space='MD2024xR1']and there are roles at both ends, the role at one end will be automatically initialized when the opposite role is specified.

#### INFO: Example:

Example:

When «dog» Rex is set as a pet for «human» Tom, Tom will be automatically set as “main” for Rex:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Association' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Association End' space='MD2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="layoutArea"><div class="column"><p><span>If two stereotypes are connected with an <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Association" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Association ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>and there are roles at both ends, the role at one end will be <span>automatically </span> initialized when the opposite role is specified. </span></p><p><span><br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="079e92f2-14af-4600-9586-9e0aabb85b18"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example:</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><div class="layoutArea"><div class="column"><p><span>When «dog» Rex is set as a pet for «human» Tom, Tom will be automatically set as “main” for Rex:</span></p><p><span>  <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Initializing Opposite Association End.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Initializing opposite association end" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /> </span></p></div></div></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span> <br /> </span></p></div></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7c94b9e3-fe28-468c-a38a-773ee720d4ca"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Stereotype" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Association" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Association End" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169674750 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00617: Inserting a shape on the path

- page_id: `169674750`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674750/Inserting+a+shape+on+the+path
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-09-22T13:56:29.636+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Working with paths and relationships
- labels: ['split-transition', 'insert-shape-on-path', 'split-path']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This functionality is available in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='MD2024xR1']StateMachine and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MD2024xR1'] diagrams.

In the State and Activity diagrams, you can split a path into two paths by dropping a shape on it. This is valid for Transition/ Control Flow/ Object Flow relationships and allows you to connect the element shape with the elements connected via the path. 
 
To insert a new shape by splitting the path on the diagram pane

1. Select the shape you want to insert or create a new one.
2. Drag the shape on the path (the path is highlighted in blue) and drop it.
3. In the **Split Transition** dialog, click the appropriate button according to where you want to insert the shape on the path:

- **Before Transition**button. The shape is inserted before the path. It means that a new path is created, then the dropped shape is drawn and then the existing path is drawn. For example, *Password read* transition is drawn from *Read Name* State to*Verification* State. If you want to insert the*Read password* State before the *Password read* Transition, drop the *Read password* State on the Transition, and in the **Split Transition** dialog, choose the **Before Transition** button.[IMAGE alt='' src=''][IMAGE alt='' src='']
- **After Transition**button. The shape is inserted before the path. It means the existing path is created, the dropped element symbol is drawn, and then a new path is drawn. For example, *Name**read* Transition is drawn from *Read Name* State to *Verification* State. If you want to insert the *Read**password* State after the *Name read* Transition, drop the *Read password* State on the Transition, and in the **Split Transition** dialog, choose the **After Transition** button.[IMAGE alt='' src=''][IMAGE alt='' src='']
- **Do Not Insert**button. Action is canceled and the dialog is closed.

In the **Split Transition** dialog, select the **Remember my choice** check box and the next time the shape will be inserted before or after the path, depending on your selection.

****

This is valid for Transition/ Control Flow/ Object Flow relationships and allows you to connect the element shape with the elements connected via the path. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" />To insert a new shape by splitting the path on the diagram pane</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Select the shape you want to insert or create a new one.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Drag the shape on the path (the path is highlighted in blue) and drop it.<br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> In the <strong>Split Transition</strong> dialog, click the appropriate button according to where you want to insert the shape on the path:</span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span></li></ol><ul><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Before Transition </strong>button. The shape is inserted before the path. It means that a new path is created, then the dropped shape is drawn and then the existing path is drawn.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b22b0b0c-fd35-4399-84a2-38e5a213de0b"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">For example, <em>Password read</em> transition is drawn from <em>Read Name</em> State to<em> Verification</em> State. The shape is inserted before the path. It means the existing path is created, the dropped element symbol is drawn, and then a new path is drawn.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="125c74a4-a702-4fa7-af1e-ad6533f7bf0f"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">For example, <em>Name </em><em>read</em> Transition is drawn from <em>Read Name</em> State to <em>Verification</em> State. Action is canceled and the dialog is closed.</span></li></ul><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="69da0fcd-52d9-42db-9ab9-6f589edb6390"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">In the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Split Transition</strong></span> dialog</span>, select the <strong>Remember my choice</strong> check box and the next time the shape will be inserted before or after the path, depending on your selection.</span></p><ac:image ac:title="Split Transition dialog" ac:alt="Split Transition dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="split_transition_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Inserting a shape on the path" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong><br /></strong></span></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675942 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00618: Inserting images into HTML text

- page_id: `169675942`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675942/Inserting+images+into+HTML+text
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-07-28T17:26:08.591+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > HTML editor
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### EXCERPT: By pasting or dragging rich text with an image

INLINE

To insert an image into HTML text

#### EXPAND: From the Containment tree

From the Containment tree

- When the image is already an AttachedFile, please refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with attached files' space='MD2024xR1'] .

#### EXPAND: By using the Advanced HTML Editor

By using the Advanced HTML Editor

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Advanced HTML Editor dialog' space='MD2024xR1'] .
2. On the toolbar, click Insert Image button [ATTACHMENT filename='insert_image_button.png'] , then click one of the following:
  - [ATTACHMENT filename='insert_image_button.png'] From File – to browse the image in your file system or paste the attached image file URL into the Picture source field.
  - [ATTACHMENT filename='insert_attached_image.png'] From Attached File – to open the Select Elements dialog and select the appropriate parent element to which the AttachedFile image should be attached.
  - [IMAGE alt='' src=''] **From Image Library** – to use images from a bundled image collection or your own added sets of images.
3. Click OK twice to close both dialogs.

#### EXPAND: By pasting or dragging the image file from file system

By pasting or dragging the image file from file system

1. Open the image file location, select the file(s) you want to insert, then do one of the following:
  - copy/paste them to the HTML Editor. In the **Paste Special**menu, select **Image**to paste image and attach it to the project or **Image (without attaching)** to paste it only for referencing in the diagram.
  - drag them to the HTML Editor. In the **Select Image Owner**, select the appropriate parent element to which the AttachedFile image should be attached. If you click **Cancel**, the image will be displayed in the diagram, but not attached to the project.
2. Click**OK**to close the dialogs. If you chose to not attach the image, it is displayed in the HTML Editor, but when you delete the file from your file system, it is no longer displayed. That also means other users you want to share the project with won't be able to see the image if they open the project from another device.This option is very useful when the file you want to insert is large and you don't want to increase the project size. This feature works on both the HTML Editor and Advanced HTML Editor.

#### EXPAND: By pasting or dragging an image from a web browser

By pasting or dragging an image from a web browser

1. Do either:
  - copy/paste the image to the HTML Editor. In the Paste Special menu, select Image to paste image and attach it to the project or HTML Text to paste it only for referencing in the diagram.
  - drag the image to the HTML Editor.
2. Click **OK** to close the dialogs. This feature works only on the HTML Editor. This feature works only with Google Chrome and Mozilla Firefox.

#### EXPAND: By pasting or dragging rich text with an image

By pasting or dragging rich text with an image

1. Do either:
  - copy/paste content to the HTML Editor. In the Paste Special menu, select HTML Text to choose whether paste it only for referencing in the diagram or attach it to the project, or select Image to paste and attach it to the project, or select Plain Text to paste only text without an image.
  - drag the image to the HTML Editor. In the Select Image Owner , select the appropriate parent element to which the AttachedFile image should be attached. If you click**Cancel**, the image will be displayed in the diagram, but not attached to the project.
2. Click **OK** to close the dialogs. This feature works only on HTML Editor. Only rich text from MS Office files is supported. If the image from the rich text document is not stored in your file system, you are not asked to choose whether to attach the file or not.

To insert multiple image files

1. Open the image files location, select the files you want to insert, then do either:
  - copy/paste them to the HTML Editor. In the Paste Special menu, select Image to paste images and attach them to the project or Image (without attaching) to paste them only for referencing in the diagram.
  - drag them to the HTML Editor. In the Select Image Owner , select the appropriate parent element to which the AttachedFile images should be attached.
2. Click OK to close the dialogs.

This feature works on both HTML Editor and Advanced HTML Editor.

That also means other users you want to share the project with won't be able to see the image if they open the project from another device. </span>This option is very useful when the file you want to insert is large and you don't want to increase the project size.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="47f1a584-94e6-4a50-a47c-7934abbd0568"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This feature works on both the HTML Editor and Advanced HTML Editor.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="145a8115-0b56-4910-b30f-a0c0c178fb48"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">By pasting or dragging an image from a web browser</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ol><li>Do either:<br /><br /><ul><li>copy/paste the image to the HTML Editor. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169676061 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00619: Inspecting changes in the Change details panel

- page_id: `169676061`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169676061/Inspecting+changes+in+the+Change+details+panel
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-04-10T10:50:03.804+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Tools > Model Merge > Analyzing and managing merge results
- labels: ['change-details', 'merge-result']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1991256206

INLINE

1991256208

INLINE

1991256207

INLINE

The **Change details** panel displays a tree structure reflecting the changes that occurred either on the element selected in the **Merged** **Result** tree/list or on the property selected in the **Specification** panel. The panel is located under the **Specification** panel on the **Merge** window.

The panel contains a tree structure that has two top-level branches – Source changes and Target changes – in 3-way merge, and 1 top-level branch – Source changes – in 2-way merge. In this panel, you can accept or reject changes using the shortcut menu.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In this panel, you can see dependent changes. In order to see them, expand the **Dependent changes** branch under an element with dependent changes.

For the dependent changes description, refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding change concept' space='MD2024xR1'].

The Multiplicity attribute is merged as a string, meaning that Lower Value and Upper Value are merged and transformed to numerical/text types accordingly.

1991256204

INLINE

**Related pages**

The panel is located under the <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Specification</strong> panel on the <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Merge</strong> window.</p><p>The panel contains a tree structure that has two top-level branches – Source changes and Target changes – in 3-way merge, and 1 top-level branch – Source changes – in 2-way merge.  In this panel, you can accept or reject changes using the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="f6f9e97b-ed3f-47ff-b1a3-c7c78e1aed44">shortcut</ac:inline-comment-marker> <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="10b9e792-a7a8-44b1-91c8-5e43686a6ff3">menu</ac:inline-comment-marker>.  <span style="color: rgb(129,137,156);"> <br /> </span></p><p><ac:image ac:title="Change details dialog" ac:alt="Change details dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="change_details_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Inspecting differences in the Change details panel" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>In this panel, you can see dependent changes. In order to see them, expand the <strong>Dependent changes</strong> branch under an element with dependent changes.</p><p>For the dependent changes description, refer to <ac:link
